questions.stem: The graph represents the total budget \(y\), in dollars, that a high school student government has after \(x\) weeks of the school year. The student government starts with a fixed budget and allots a weekly amount to spend. The slope of the graph can best be interpreted in which of the following ways?
choices: The total amount that the student government spends
The minimum amount that the student government spends
questions.explanation:
Add the two equations to eliminate \(y\).
$$\begin{aligned}(x+y)+(x-y) &= 5+1 \\ 2x &= 6 \\ x &= 3\end{aligned}$$
Substitute \(x=3\) into \(x+y=5\).
$$3+y=5 \Rightarrow y=2$$
The solution is \((3,2)\).

questions.stem: The graphs of the equations \(y=3x-5\) and \(y = \frac{1}{3}x\) intersect how many times in the \(xy\)-plane?
questions.explanation: The slope of \(y=3x-5\) is \(3\) and the slope of \(y=\frac{1}{3}x\) is \(\frac{1}{3}\). When two linear equations have different slopes, they intersect once.

questions.stem:
$$n-25=130$$
What value of \(n\) is a solution to the given equation?
questions.explanation: $$\begin{aligned}n - 25 &= 130 \\ n - \cancel{25} + \cancel{25} &= 130 + 25 \\ n&=155 \end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: Which expression is equal to \(32x-(10x+7x)\)?
questions.explanation: $$\begin{aligned}&=32x - (10x + 7x) \\ &=32x - (17x) \\ &=15x \end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: The number \(n\) is \(13\) more than the number \(k\). Which equation represents the relationship between \(k\) and \(n\)?
questions.explanation: Translate words to symbols working left to right. “\(n\) is” translates to “\(n=\)” , “\(13\) more than the number \(k\)” is “\(13+k\)”.

questions.stem: The function \(f\) is defined by \(f(x)=-3x+19\). What is the value of \(f(x)\) when \(x=2\)?
questions.explanation:
Substitute \(2\) into the equation and simplify:
$$\begin{aligned}&f(2)=-3(2)+19 \\ &f(2)=-6+19=13 \end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: Katie traveled a total of \(170\) miles by driving \(65\) miles to a location where she rode for \(h\) hours at \(18\) miles per hour. Which of the following equations represents this situation?
questions.explanation:
Use the formula Distance = rate x time. Substituting Katie's biking rate (\(18\) miles per hour) and time (\(h\) hours), her total distance biked is \(18h\). She also drove \(65\) miles making her total distance \(18h+65=170\).
$$\begin{aligned}\text{Distance} &= \text{rate} \times \text{time} \\ 170 &= 18h + 65 \end{aligned}$$

questions.stem:
$$27=3-5y$$
Which equation has the same solution as the given equation?
questions.explanation: None of the answer choices show the equation completely solved, but all have \(-5y\) alone, so isolate \(-5y\). Subtract \(3\) from both sides to get \(24=-5y\).

questions.stem:
$$8b+3.5d=256$$
The given equation describes the relationship between the number of dollhouses, \(d\), and the number of board games, \(b\), that can a toy factory can produce on a given day. If the factory makes \(32\) board games on a given day, how many dollhouses can it make on this day?
questions.explanation:
In the equation \(8b+3.5d=256\), \(b\) is the number of board games. The number of board games given is \(32\). Substitute \(32\) into the equation for \(b\) and solve for \(d\), the number of dollhouses.
$$\begin{aligned}8(32)+3.5d&=256 \\ 256+3.5d&=256 \\ 3.5d&=0 \\ d&=0 \end{aligned}$$

questions.stem:
$$17x=901$$
What is the solution to the given equation?
questions.explanation:
To solve for \(x\), divide both sides by \(17\).
$$\begin{aligned}17x&=901 \\ x&=53 \end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: While building a tile border along a wall, Ada calculates that she uses \(3.9\) tiles per inch in length. Which function \(g\) models the number of tiles Ada will need for a length of \(x\) inches at this rate?
questions.explanation:
One method for finding the correct equation is to test a value for \(x\) the length to be tiled. Let \(x=10\). If Ada needs \(3.9\) tiles for each inch, in total she needs \(10\times3.9=39\). This means that multiplying \(x\) by \(3.9\) is the correct operation. But if you don't see that, you can also substitute \(x=10\) in each answer choice and look for the answer that gives \(39\).
Checking answers:
A) \(g(10)=10-3.9\neq39\)
C) \(g(10)=3.9(10)=39\).

questions.stem: \(9\) less than the quotient of \(n\) and \(2\) equals \(17\). Which equation represents this situation?
questions.explanation: Translate words to symbols working left to right. "\(9\)" less than a number is \(n-9\), but this problem says "…the quotient of \(n\) and \(2\)". Quotient means division, so \(\frac{n}{2}-9\). All answer choices end with \(=17\), so the correct answer is C.

questions.stem:
$$\frac{1}{3} y=x$$
$$y-x=8$$
What is the solution, \((x,y)\) to the given system of equations?
questions.explanation:
This system of equations is best solved by the substitution method. First, multiply the first equation by \(3\) to clear the fraction:
$$\begin{aligned}\ 3 \left(\frac{1}{3}y \right)&=x(3) \\ y&=3x \end{aligned}$$
Substitute \(3x\) in for \(y\) into the equation \(y-x=8\), so \(3x-x=8\). Then solve for \(x\):
$$\begin{aligned}\ 3x - x &= 8 \\ \frac{\cancel{2}x}{\cancel{2}} &= \frac{8}{2} \\ x &= 4 \end{aligned}$$
The problem asks you to solve for \((x,y)\), but all of the \(x\)-values are distinct in the answers, so you can stop here.

questions.stem:
\(x\)\(g(x)\)
\(-2\)\(-34\)
\(-1\)\(-38\)
\(0\)\(-42\)
For the linear function \(g\), the table shows three values of \(x\) and their corresponding values of \(g(x)\). Which equation defines \(g(x)\)?
questions.explanation: To find the equation, you need to find the slope and the \(y\)-intercept because the answers contain different values for each of these. The \(y\)-intercept definition is the value of \(y\) when \(x=0\). From the table you can see this is \(-42\). Eliminate B and D. To find the slope, count the change in \(y\) for the corresponding change in \(x\). As \(y\) decreases by \(4\), \(x\) increases by \(1\). The slope is \(\frac{-4}{1} = -4\).

questions.stem:
$$x+2y=4$$
$$-2y=10$$
The solution to the given system of equations is \((x,y)\). What is the value of \(x\)?
questions.explanation:
The first equation has \(+2y\) and the second equation gives the value of \(-2y=10\). This means you don't need to solve for \(y\), but can divide both sides by \(-1\) to get \(+2y=-10\), which can then be substituted into the first equation:
$$\begin{aligned}\ x+2y &= 4 \\ x+(-10) &= 4 \\ x - \cancel{10} + \cancel{10} &= 4 + 10 \\ x&=14 \end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: Which expression is equivalent to \(-3n^4+10n^4\)?
questions.explanation: In the expression \(-3n^4+ 10n^4\), \(-3n^4\) and \(10n^4\) are considered like terms because they have the same variables with the same exponents. This means you can combine like terms, so \(-3+10=7\), making the correct answer \(7n^4\).

questions.stem:
$$3c+5=3c-2$$
How many solutions does the given equation have?
questions.explanation:
With \(3c\) on both sides of the equation, adding \(5\) to one and subtracting \(2\) from the other cannot produce equivalent values. There is no value of \(c\) that will make the two sides equivalent. Attempting to solve the equation will show this result too:
$$\begin{aligned}\ 3c+5 &= 3c-2 \\ \cancel{3c} +5 - \cancel{3c} &= \cancel{3c} -2 -\cancel{3c} \\ 5&=-2 \end{aligned}$$
Since this is a false equation, there is no solution.

questions.stem: A line in the \(xy\)-plane passes through the point \((0,-3)\) and has a slope of \(\frac{2}{5}\). Which equation represents this line?
questions.explanation: The point \((0,-3)\) is the \(y\)-intercept, or \(b\) in the slope-intercept equation \(y=mx+b\). \(m\) is the slope, so \(m=\frac{2}{5}\), making the equation \(y=\frac{2}{5}x-3\).

questions.stem:
$$y+4=-3x+5$$
$$y=-3x+1$$
How many solutions does the system of equations have?
questions.explanation:
The second equation, \(y=-3x+1\) is already in slope-intercept form (\(y=mx+b\), where \(m\) is slope and \(b\) is the \(y\)-intercept). Transform the first equation to the same form by subtracting \(4\) from both sides:
$$\begin{aligned}\ y+4&=-3x+5 \\y + \cancel{4} - \cancel{4} &= -3x+5-4 \\ y&=-3x+1 \end{aligned}$$
Since the first and second equations are equivalent (same slope and same \(y\)-intercept) they have infinitely many solutions. In other words, they produce the same line.

questions.stem: An event planner is designing the floral arrangements for a party and has \(120\) flowers. Each vase will have either \(2\) flowers or \(5\) flowers. Which of the following equations represents this situation, where \(a\) represents the number of \(2\)-flower vases and \(b\) represents the number of \(5\)-flower vases?
questions.explanation: The situation will be represented by an equation that shows the total number of flowers (\(120\)) as the sum of flowers in each of the two types of vases. \(b\) is the number of \(5\)-flower vases, so the total number of flowers in those vases is \(5b\). And \(a\) is the number of \(2\)-flower vases, so the total number of flowers in those vases is \(2a\). These can be added to show the total, which makes the correct equation \(2a+5b=120\).

questions.stem: The equation \(g(n)=11n-5\) defines the function \(g\). What is the value of \(g(n)\) when \(n=6?\)
questions.explanation:
Substitute \(6\) into the equation and evaluate.
$$\begin{aligned}\ g(6)&=11(6)-5 \\ g(6)&=66-5\\ g(6)&=61 \end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: After a typing course, George can type at an average of \(2.3\) seconds per word. Which function \(t\) represents the number of seconds it would take George to type \(x\) words at this rate?
questions.explanation:
Choose a value for \(x\), the number of words George types. Let \(x=10\). If George takes \(2.3\) seconds to type each word, he takes \(10\times2.3=23\). From here you can see that you multiplied \(x\) by \(2.3\) to find the answer. But if you don't see that, you can also substitute \(10\) in for \(x\) in each answer choice and look for the answer that gives \(23\).
Examples:
A) \(t(10)=10+2.3\neq23\)
C) \(t(10)=2.3(10)=23\)

questions.stem: The function \(h\) is defined by \(h(x)=\frac{3}{8}x-7\). What is the \(y\)-intercept of the graph of \(y=h(x)\) in the \(xy\)-plane?
questions.explanation: The \(y\)-intercept is the value of \(y\) when \(x=0\), so eliminate A and D. The equation \(h(x)=\frac{3}{8}x-7\) is in slope-intercept form \(y=mx+b\) where \(b\) is the \(y\)-intercept, so the \(y\)-intercept is \(-7\).

questions.stem:
$$2c-4=116$$
What value of \(c\) is the solution to the given equation?
questions.explanation:
To solve for \(c\), add \(4\) to both sides, then divide by \(2\) on both sides. \(2c=120\). Then divide both sides by \(2\), so \(c=60\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ 2c - \cancel{4} + \cancel{4} &= 116+4 \\ 2c &= 120 \\ \frac{\cancel{2}c}{\cancel{2}} &= \frac{120}{2}\\ c &= 60 \end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: If \(2n-6=22\), what is the value of \(8n-24\)?
questions.explanation:
Instead of solving for \(n\) to answer this question, notice that \(2n-6\) can be transformed to equal \(8n-24\) by multiplying it by \(4\). To do this, multiply both sides of the equation by \(4\):
$$\begin{aligned}\ 2n-6&=22 \\ 4(2n-6)&=22\times4\\ 8n-24&=88\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem:
$$x-75=110$$
What value of \(c\) is the solution to the given equation?
questions.explanation:
To solve, add \(75\) to both sides:
$$\begin{aligned}\ x-75&=110\\x - \cancel{75} + \cancel{75} &= 110+75 \\ x&=185\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem:
$$f(h)=-12h+90$$
The function \(f\) describes the number of pages remaining in a book after Isaac reads for \(h\) hours. According to the function, about how many pages does Isaac read each hour?
questions.explanation:
In the equation \(f(h)=-12h+90\), the rate of change, or slope, is Isaac's reading rate. But if you don't see that, you can substitute \(1\) and \(2\) for \(h\) (hours) and find the change between the number of pages Isaac has left to read. This will be his reading rate (the number of pages he read in that one hour):
$$\begin{aligned}\ &f(1)=-12(1)+90=78 \\&f(2)=-12(2)+90=66 \end{aligned}$$
This means Isaac read \(78-66=12\) pages in one hour, so his reading rate is \(12\) pages per hour.

questions.stem: The line \(f\) is defined by \(f(x)=-\frac{2}{3}x+1\). Line \(g\) is parallel to line \(f\) in the \(xy\)-plane. What is the slope of line \(g\)?
questions.explanation: Parallel lines have the same slope. The equation \(f(x)=-\frac{2}{3}x+1\) is in slope-intercept form, or \(y=mx+b\) where \(m\) is the slope. The slope of \(f(x)\) is \(-\frac{2}{3}\), so that will also be the slope of function \(g\).

questions.stem: A landscaper plants \(x\) gardens with the same dimensions and shape in a public park, using a specific type of flower seed. The landscaper charges a fee calculated by the expression \(xKlw\), where \(x\) is the number of gardens, \(K\) is a constant with units of dollars per square yard of flower seed, \(w\) is the width of each garden in yards, and \(l\) is the length of each garden in feet. If the landscaper uses a less expensive flower seed, which of the variables in the expression would change?
questions.explanation: Using a less expensive type of flower seed means you need to look for the variable that pertains to cost of flower seed. This is \(K\), or dollars per square yard of flower seed.

questions.stem: The graph above shows a system of equations. What is the solution \((x,y)\) to the system?
questions.explanation: To solve a system of equations by graphing, look for the point where the lines intersect. These lines intersect at \((2,-5).\)

questions.stem: If \(12n=-30\), what is the value of \(4n\)?
questions.explanation:
Instead of solving for \(n\) (which has a fractional solution), solve for \(4n\). Divide both sides of the given equation, \(12n=-30\) by \(3\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ \frac{12n}{3}&=\frac{-30}{3} \\4n &= -10 \end{aligned}$$

questions.stem:
\(x\)\(h(x)\)
\(0\)\(-9\)
\(1\)\(-2\)
\(2\)\(5\)
For the linear function \(h\), the table shows three values of \(x\) and their corresponding values of \(h(x)\). Which equation defines \(h(x)\)?
questions.explanation:
All of the answer choices are in the form \(y=mx+b\) where \(m\) is the slope and \(b\) is the \(y\)-intercept. The \(y\)-intercept is the value of \(y\) when \(x=0\). From the table the \(y\)-intercept is \(9\), so eliminate A and C. Find the slope;
$$\begin{aligned}\ m&=\frac{y_2 - y_1}{x_2 - x_1} \\ m&=\frac{5 - (-2)}{2-1}\\ m &= 7\end{aligned}$$
The slope is \(7\), so the equation is \(h(x)=7x-9\).

questions.stem: Lisa studies vocabulary flash cards at a rate of \(4\) cards per minute and science flash cards at a rate of \(6\) flash cards per minute.  She studies vocabulary for \(v\) minutes and science cards for \(s\) minutes for a combined total of \(42\) flash cards.  Which equation represents this situation?
questions.explanation: Lisa studies a total of \(42\) flash cards, so the expression must equal \(42\). Eliminate C and D. She studies vocabulary cards for \(v\) minutes at a rate of \(4\) cards per minute, so she studies \(4v\) vocabulary cards. Likewise, she studies science cards for \(s\) minutes at a rate of \(6\) cards per minute, so she studies \(6s\) science cards. In total, Lisa studies \(4v+6s\) flash cards.

questions.stem:
$$12S+32L=\text{1,050}$$
At a particular toy factory, the number of minutes it takes to package a toy depends on the size of the toy. On a particular day \(S\) small toys and \(L\) large toys are packaged taking a total of \(\text{1,050}\) minutes. The situation is represented by the given equation. How many more minutes does it take to package a large toy than a small toy?
questions.explanation: The total number of minutes spent packaging toys is \(\text{1,050}\). \(S\) represents the number of small toys and \(L\) is the number of large toys, so \(12S\) and \(32L\) represent the number of minutes to package small and large toys, respectively. It takes 12 minutes to package one small toy and \(32\) minutes to package one large toy, so it takes \(32-12=20\) minutes longer to package a large toy.

questions.stem:
$$2y+50=72$$
What is the solution to the given equation?
questions.explanation:
Subtract \(50\) from both sides and then divide both sides by \(2\):
$$\begin{aligned}\ 2y + \cancel{50} - \cancel{50} &= 72-50 \\ \frac{\cancel{2}y}{\cancel{2}} &= \frac{22}{2}\\ y&=11\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: Which expression is equal to \(54n-(6n-20n)\)?
questions.explanation:
First, combine the like terms inside the parentheses, so \(54n-(-14n)\). Then change \(-(-14n)\) to \(+14n\), so \(54n+14n=68n\).\\ Combine like terms inside parentheses, distribute \(-1\) to eliminate the parentheses, and simplify:
$$\begin{aligned}\ &=54n - (6n-20n)\\ &=54n - (-14n)\\&=54n + 14n =68n \end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: The number \(n\) is \(5\) less than twice the number \(m\). Which equation represents the relationship between \(m\) and \(n\)?
questions.explanation:
Start with twice \(m\), which is \(2m\). Then \(5\) less than \(2m\) is \(2m-5\). Alternatively, choose a value for \(m\) and solve for \(n\). Then substitute into each answer to find the correct value of \(n\). Let \(m=3\). Twice \(3\) is \(6\) and \(5\) less than \(6\) is \(1\). Now substitute \(m=3\) into each equation to find \(n=1\):
A) \(5(3)-2\neq1\)
B) \(2(3)-5=1\)
Note: it is important when using this strategy to check all four answers as sometimes a second answer has the correct result. If this happens, change your initial value (let \(m=\) a new number) and re-check the answers that gave the correct result in the first round.

questions.stem: The total revenue \(g(x)\), in dollars, earned by a particular hotel with \(52\) rooms is given by \(g(x)=52x+\text{2,500}\) where \(x\) is the cost per room, in dollars. What is the total revenue earned by the hotel if all rooms are occupied when the cost for each room is \(\$180\)?
questions.explanation:
Since \(x\) is the cost of each room in the equation \(g(x)=52x+\text{2,500}\), substitute \(180\) in for \(x\) and evaluate (do the math):
$$g(180)=52(180)+\text{2,500}=\text{11,860}$$

questions.stem: In the linear function \(g\), \(g(-1)=6\) and \(g(0)=11\). Which equation defines \(g\)?
questions.explanation:
\(g(0)=11\) is the ordered pair \((0,11)\), which is the \(y\)-intercept. In slope-intercept form, \(y=mx+b\), the \(y\)-intercept is \(b\), so \(y=mx+11\). Eliminate B and C. Then substitute the other ordered pair \((-1,6)\) into the equation to solve for \(m\):
$$\begin{aligned}\ y &= -1m+11 \\6 &= -1m+11 \\6 -11&= -1m+\cancel{11} - \cancel{11}\\-5 &= -1m\\ \frac{-5}{-1}&=\frac{\cancel{-1}m}{\cancel{-1}}\\m &= 5 \end{aligned}$$
The slope is \(5\).

questions.stem: Lines \(m\) and \(n\) are perpendicular lines graphed in the \(xy\)-plane. Line \(n\) is defined by \(-4y-16x=15\). What is the slope of line \(m\)?
questions.explanation:
Perpendicular lines have slopes that are negative reciprocals of each other. First, find the slope of line \(n\) and then take the negative reciprocal. To find the slope, get the equation into slope-intercept form (\(y=mx+b\)):
$$\begin{aligned}\ -4y-16x &= 15 \\ -4y-\cancel{16x} + \cancel{16x}&=15+16x\\ \frac{\cancel{-4}y}{\cancel{-4}} &=\frac{15}{-4}+\frac{16x}{-4}\\y&=-4x-\frac{15}{4}\end{aligned}$$
Since you are only concerned with the slope, you can essentially ignore the \(y\)-intercept. The slope-intercept form is \(y=-4x-\)…, so the slope is \(-4\) or \(-\frac{4}{1}\). The “negative” or opposite of that is \(\frac{4}{1}\), and the reciprocal of \(\frac{4}{1}\) is \(\frac{1}{4}\), or \(0.25\).

questions.stem: A marine museum has a \(80\)-liter touch tank and a \(250\)-liter view only tank. The total number of fish in the two tanks is \(\text{5,020}\). The equation \(80x+250y=\text{5,020}\) represents this situation. Which of the following is the best interpretation of \(y\) in this situation?
choices: The average number of fish per liter in the view only tank
The average number of fish per liter in the touch tank
The total number of fish in the touch tank
The total number of fish in the view only tank
questions.explanation: To interpret \(y\), interpret \(250y\) in the equation \(80x+250y=\text{5,020}\). In the equation, \(\text{5,020}\) is the total number of fish, so \(250y\) is the total number of fish in the tank with \(250\) liters. Since \(250\) is the number of liters, \(y\) alone is the number of fish per \(1\) liter in that tank.

questions.stem:
$$\frac{r}{1-3s}=t+6$$
The given equation shows the relationship between the distinct numbers \(r\), \(s\), and \(t\). Which equation expresses \(1-3s\) in terms of \(r\) and \(t\)?
questions.explanation:
This is a literal equation, but instead of solving for one variable, you are asked to solve for the expression \(1-3s\): To start, multiply both sides by \(1-3s\). Then divide both sides by \(t+6\) to isolate \(1-3s\):
$$\begin{aligned}\ \frac{r}{\cancel{1-3s}} \times{\cancel{1-3s}}&=t+6 \times (1-3s) \\ \frac{r}{t+6}&=\cancel{t+6} \times (1-3s) \div \cancel{(t+6)} \\1-3s&=\frac{r}{t+6} \end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: Which expression is equivalent to \((5m^4-5m^2 )-(m^4-3m^2 )\)?
questions.explanation:
To simplify this expression, eliminate the parentheses and combine like terms (same variables with same exponents). First, distribute \(-1\) to the second set of parentheses, then combine the \(m^4\) terms and the \(m^2\) terms.
$$\begin{aligned}\ &=5m^4-5m^2-1(m^4-3m^2) \\&=5m^4-5m^2-m^4+3m^2 \end{aligned}$$
Combine like terms, so \(5m^4-m^4-5m^2+3m^2=4m^4-2m^2\).

questions.stem: The function \(g\), defined by \(g(x)= 4x+ 128\), is graphed in the \(xy\)-plane. What is the \(x\)-intercept of the graph of \(y=g(x)\)?
questions.explanation:
The definition of an \(x\)-intercept is the \(x\)-value when \(y=0\). But even if you don't know that, all of the answers have a \(y\)-value of \(0\). Substitute \(0\) in for \(g(x)\) and solve for \(x\). To solve, subtract \(128\) from both sides and then divide both sides by \(4\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ 0-128&=4x+\cancel{128}-\cancel{128}\\ \frac{-128}{4}&=\frac{\cancel{4}x}{\cancel{4}}\end{aligned}$$
\(\frac{-128}{4}=-32\).

questions.stem: A wholesaler will ship its rolls and boxes of fabric free of charge if the combined area of the rolls and boxes is at least \(\text{3,200}\) square yards. What is the minimum number of rolls a buyer must order with an order of \(600\) square yards in boxed fabric if each roll has \(250\) square yards of fabric?
questions.explanation: An inequality describes this situation; boxed fabric \(+\) rolled fabric \(\geq\) the total minimum. Substitute each of these with algebraic expressions and solve. The area of boxed fabric is \(600\) square yards. For the rolled fabric, let \(r=\) the number of rolls, so \(250r\) is the total area of rolled fabric. The combined area must be greater than or equal to \(\text{3,200}\) square yards. Substituting those values, the inequality is \(600+250r\geq\text{3,200}\). To solve, subtract \(600\) from both sides, so \(250r\geq\text{2,600}\). Then divide both sides by \(250\), so \(r\geq10.4\). Since \(r\) is a whole number of rolls, the minimum must be the next integer after \(10.4\), or \(11\).

questions.stem: A podcast host is analyzing their audience statistics. The data shows that the number of people who downloaded an audio version of the podcast in the previous year was four times the number of people who watched the podcast video online. The data further shows that \(\text{42,000}\) fewer people watched it online than the number of people who downloaded the audio version. Based on these data, how many people watched the podcast video online last year?
questions.explanation:
This is a system of equations. Audio downloads are described in terms of video views, so let \(a=\) audio downloads and \(v=\) video views. Since audio downloads is four times the video views, \(a=4v\). And since \(\text{42,000}\) fewer people watched it than downloaded the audio \(a=v+\text{42,000}\). To solve the system, write and solve an equation that relates the two expressions for \(a\):
$$\begin{aligned}\ 4v &= v+\text{42,000} \\ 4v-v&=\cancel{v} +\text{42,000}- \cancel{v}\\ 3v&=\text{42,000}\\ \frac{\cancel{3}v}{\cancel{3}} &=\frac{\text{42,000}}{3}\\ v&=\text{14,000} \end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: Tom is mowing a lawn. The function \(g(x)=-8x+75\) approximates the area, in square yards, of lawn Tom had remaining after \(x\) minutes. Which statement is the best interpretation of the \(y\)-intercept of the graph of \(y=g(x)\) in the \(xy\)-plane in this context?
choices: Tom mowed approximately 8 square yards each minute.
Tom had approximately 8 square yards to mow when he began mowing the lawn.
Tom had approximately 75 square yards to mow when he began mowing the lawn.
Tom mowed approximately 75 square yards each minute.
questions.explanation: In the equation \(g(x)=-8x +75\), the \(y\)-intercept is \(75\), or the value of \(y\) when \(x=0\). Since \(x\) is time in minutes that Tom has been mowing, and when \(x=0\) minutes, Tom has \(75\) square yards to mow, it must be the area he has to mow when he begins.

questions.stem: A line in the \(xy\)-plane passes through the point \((-10,1)\) and has a slope of \(\frac{1}{2}\). Which equation represents this line?
questions.explanation: All of the answers are in point-slope form, which is \(y-y_1=m(x-x_1 )\) where \(m\) is the slope and \((x_1,y_1)\) is a point the line passes through. Substituting the given values, the equation is \(y-1=\frac{1}{2} (x--10)\), or \(y-1=\frac{1}{2} (x+10)\).

questions.stem: What is the value of \(\frac{n}{28}\) if \(\frac{28}{n}=-4\)?
questions.explanation: The expression you are asked to evaluate, \(\frac{n}{28}\), is the reciprocal of the given value, \(\frac{28}{n}=-4\). This means there is no need to solve for \(n\). Instead, take the reciprocal of both sides, so \(-\frac{4}{1}\) becomes \(-\frac{1}{4}\) or \(-0.25\).

questions.stem: The graph of \(y=h(x)-18\) is shown. Which equation defines function \(h\)?
questions.explanation:
The equation for the given line is \(y=h(x)-18\) and is it also \(y=\frac{1}{5}x-8\) (note: there is no need to count the slope as all of the answers have the same slope). Set these equations equal to each other and solve for \(h(x)\) by adding \(18\) to both sides:
$$\begin{aligned} \ h(x)-18 &= \frac{1}{5}x-8 \\ h(x)\cancel{-18} + \cancel{18}&=\frac{1}{5}x-8+18 \\h(x)&=\frac{1}{5}x+10 \end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: What is the value of \(k\) if \(\frac{a}{b}=-7\) and \(\frac{ka}{12b}=-7\)?
questions.explanation:
Since \(\frac{a}{b}=-7\) and \(\frac{a}{b}\) appears in the second equation, substitute \(-7\) into the second equation. Now \(\frac{k}{12}(-7)=-7\), so \(k=12\). This problem can also be solved directly. First, substitute \(-7\) into the equation for \(\frac{a}{b}\). Then divide both sides by \(-7\), and multiply both sides by \(12\) to isolate \(k\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ \frac{k}{12}\times{-7} &= -7\\ \frac{\frac{k}{12}\times \cancel{-7}}{\cancel{-7}} &= \frac{\cancel{-7}}{\cancel{-7}} \\ \frac{k}{12}&=-1\\ k&=-12\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: The function \(f\) is defined by \(f(x)= \frac{1}{2}x-8\). The graph of \(y=f(x)\) in the \(xy\)-plane has an \(x\)-intercept at \((c,0)\) and a \(y\)-intercept at \((0,d)\), where \(c\) and \(d\) are constants. What is the value of \(c-d\)?
questions.explanation:
In the equation \(f(x)= \frac{1}{2}x-8\), \(-8\) is the \(y\)-intercept. The point \((0,d)\) is also the \(y\)-intercept, so \(d=-8\). To find \(c\) in the point \((c,0)\), substitute \(0\) in for \(f(x)\) and solve. Then find \(c-d\).
$$\begin{aligned}0&=\frac{1}{2}c-8\\0+8&=\frac{1}{2}c-\cancel{8}+\cancel{8}\\2\times8&=\frac{1}{\cancel{2}}c\times\cancel{2}\\c&=16\end{aligned}$$
$$\begin{aligned}&=c-d\\&=16-(-8)\\&=24\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: The graph of the linear function \(g\) in the \(xy\)-plane, contains the points \((5,21)\) and \((0,6)\). Which equation defines \(g\), where \(y=g(x)\)?
questions.explanation:
The point \((0,6)\) is the \(y\)-intercept, or \(b\) in the slope-intercept form \(y=mx+b\). Since the equation is \(g(x)=mx+6\), eliminate A and B. Next, substitute \((5,21)\) into the equation to solve for \(m\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ g(x) &=mx+6 \\ 21&=5m+6\\21-6&=5m+\cancel{6}-\cancel{6}\\ \frac{15}{5}&=\frac{\cancel{5}m}{\cancel{5}}\\m&=3 \end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: Erika does an exercise program that lasts \(64\) minutes. The exercise program is composed of \(4\)-minute intervals on a stationary bicycle, and \(3\)-minute intervals on a treadmill. If Erika did a total of eight intervals on the treadmill, how many intervals did she do on the stationary bicycle?
questions.explanation:
Define variables for the number of intervals Erika does on each machine; let \(b=\) number of bike intervals and \(t=\) number of treadmill intervals. This means \(4b\) is total time on the bike and \(3t\) is total time on the treadmill. Write an equation for the total time of the exercise program, \(4b+3t=64\). Substitute \(t=8\) because Erika did \(8\) intervals on the treadmill. Solve for \(b\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ 4b+3(8)&=64\\4b+24&=64\\ 4b+\cancel{24}-\cancel{24}&=64-24\\ \frac{\cancel{4}b}{\cancel{4}}&=\frac{40}{4}\\b&=10\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: A craft store sells a particular type of glass bead that comes in two colors. There are \(6\) bins of blue glass beads and \(8\) bins of red glass beads. The total number of glass beads is \(\text{6,381}\). This situation can be represented by the equation \(6x+8y=\text{6,381}\). Which of the following is the best interpretation of \(y\) in this situation?
choices: The average number of blue glass beads per bin
The average number of red glass beads per bin
The total number of blue glass beads
The total number of red glass beads
questions.explanation:
In the equation \(6x+8y=\text{6,381}\), the total number of glass beads is \(\text{6,381}\), and \(8y\) is the total number of red glass beads. This can be represented as a fraction:
$$\begin{aligned}\ \frac{8y \, \text{red beads}}{1 \text{store}} \end{aligned}$$
The goal now is to isolate \(y\), and to do that, \(8\) must be eliminated, or canceled. Since \(8\) is the number of bins, write a ratio that contains \(8\) bins in the denominator, so \(\frac{1 \, \text{store}}{8 \text{bins}}\). Multiply the two fractions:
$$\begin{aligned}\ \frac{\cancel{8}y \, \text{red beads}}{1 \, \cancel {\text{store}}}\times \frac{1 \, \cancel{\text{store}}}{\cancel{8} \, \text{bins}}=\frac{y \, \text{red beads}}{1 \, \text{bin}} \end{aligned}$$
This means \(y\) is the (average) number of red beads per bin.

questions.stem:
$$10=6y$$
$$x-6y=-2$$
The solution to the given system of equations is \((x,y)\). What is the value of \(x\)?
questions.explanation:
Instead of solving for \(y\) in the first equation, substitute \(10\) into the second equation for \(6y\). Then solve for \(x\) in one step by adding \(10\) to both sides.
$$\begin{aligned}\ x-10&=-2\\ x-\cancel{10}+\cancel{10}&=-2+10\\x&=8\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: Sri can make \(m\) liters of juice for every \(k\) pounds of fruit. Which expression represents the amount of juice, in liters, Sri can make from \(24k\) pounds of fruit?
questions.explanation:
The answers contain algebraic expressions, so choose values for the variable(s) and then test answers. Let \(k=2\) and \(m=1\); Sri makes \(1\) liter of juice for every \(2\) pounds of fruit. With \(24k\) pounds of fruit, Sri can make \(24(2)=48\) liters of juice. Substitute \(m=2\) into the answers to find which one equals \(48\):
A) \(m+24=1+24 eq48\)
B) \(m-24=1-24 eq48\)
C) \( rac{m}{24}= rac{1}{24} eq48\)
D) \(24m=24(2)=48\)

questions.stem: A jewelry maker is selling bracelets. She has already earned \(\$85\) toward her goal, and she plans to charge \(\$18.15\) for each bracelet. Her goal is to earn at least \(\$700\). What is the minimum number of bracelets she must sell in order to meet or exceed her earnings goal?
questions.explanation:
Begin by defining a variable for the number of bracelets and then write an inequality for her earnings goal. Let \(b=\) number of bracelets and since she earns \(\$18.15\) from each bracelet sale, she earns a total of \(\$18.15b\) on the sale of bracelets. The earnings goal inequality is \(18.15b+85\geq700\). Solve for \(b\):
$$\begin{aligned}\ 18.15b+85&\geq700 \\ 18.15b+\cancel{85}-\cancel{85}&\geq700-85\\ \frac{\cancel{18.15}b}{\cancel{18.15}}&\geq\frac{615}{18.15}\\b&\geq33.9\end{aligned}$$
To reach her earnings goal, she must sell at least \(34\) bracelets.

questions.stem: The graph shows the relationship between the number of hours at Job M, \(x\), and the number of hours at Job N, \(y\), that Dalila can work in a given period of time. Which equation could represent this relationship?
questions.explanation:
The graph has a \(y\)-intercept of \(250\). Eliminate C (\(y\)-int is \(10\)) and D (\(y\)-int is \(15\)). A and B have different \(y\)-intercepts, so find the \(y\)-intercept of one. Write \(10x+15y=2,500\) in slope-intercept form.
Instead of solving for \(y\) in the first equation, substitute \(10\) into the second equation for \(6y\). Then solve for \(x\) in one step by adding \(10\) to both sides.
$$\begin{aligned}\ \cancel{15x}+10y-\cancel{15x}&=-15x+2500\\ \frac{\cancel{10}y}{\cancel{10}}&=\frac{-15x+2500}{10}\\ y&=\frac{-15x}{10}+250 \end{aligned}$$
The \(y\)-intercept is \(250\). There is no need to simplify the slope here because you don't need it to find the correct answer.

questions.stem:
$$n=15+.1t$$
The equation gives the speed, \(n\), in miles per minute, of a particular cyclist in a bike race \(t\) seconds after crossing the starting line. What is the speed, in miles per minute, of the cyclist \(30\) seconds after crossing the starting line?
questions.explanation:
In the equation \(n=15+.1t\), \(t\) is time in seconds after crossing the starting line. The question asks you to find the speed \(30\) seconds after crossing the starting line, so substitute \(t=30\) and evaluate:
$$\begin{aligned}\ n&=15+.1(30) \\n&=15+3\\n&=18 \end{aligned}$$

Method \(1\): Choose a value for \(p\). Let \(p= ext{4,000}\). If one teaspoon is used for \( ext{1,000}\) liters, \(4\) teaspoons are needed for \( ext{4,000}\) liters. To chlorinate this amount for \(4\) days, \(4 imes ext{4,000}= ext{16,000}\) liters are needed. Test the answers by substituting \(p= ext{4,000}\) to find the answer that gives \(C= ext{16,000}\):
A) \(C= ext{1,000} imes ext{4,000} eq ext{16,000}\)
B) \(C= ext{4,000} imes ext{4,000} eq ext{16,000}\)
C) \(C= rac{ ext{4,000}}{ ext{1,000}} eq ext{16,000}\)
D) \(C= rac{ ext{4,000}}{250}= ext{16,000}\)
Method \(2\): Start with \( rac{1 \, ext{teaspoon}}{ ext{1,000} \, ext{liters}}\). Use unit conversions to calculate the number of teaspoons at the water park for \(4\) days:
$$ egin{aligned} rac{1\, ext{teaspoon}}{1000\,\cancel{ ext{liters}}} imes rac{p\,\cancel{ ext{liters}}}{1\, ext{day}} &= rac{ rac{p}{1000}\, ext{teaspoons}}{1\, ext{day}}\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: A vendor at a holiday market has a goal of earning at least \(\$\text{3,500}\) from selling products at the market. The vendor can bring a maximum of \(500\) products to sell at the market. If the vendor charges \(\$3.80\) for bracelets and \(\$9.10\) for necklaces, what is the maximum number of bracelets the vendor can sell to meet the goal?
questions.explanation:
Let \(b=\) number of bracelets and \(n=\) number of necklaces. Since the maximum number of products is \(500\), \(n+b\leq500\) or \(n\leq500-b\). Write a second inequality with the earning goal: \(3.8b+9.1n\geq\text{3,500}\).\\Substitute \(500-b\) into the second inequality and solve for \(b\):
$$\begin{aligned}\ 3.8b+9.1(500-b)&\geq\text{3,500} \\ 3.8b+\text{4,550}-9.1b&\geq\text{3,500}\\ -5.3b+\cancel{\text{4,550}}-\cancel{\text{4,550}}&\geq\text{3,500}-\text{4,550}\\ \frac{\cancel{-5.3}b}{\cancel{-5.3}}&\geq\frac{-\text{1,050}}{-5.3}\\ b&\leq198.1 \end{aligned}$$
Since \(b\leq198.1\), the maximum number of bracelets is \(198\).

questions.stem: A cereal factory started the day with \(\text{216,000}\) grams (g) of cereal ready to be packaged. Exactly \(44\) minutes after the factory turned on its machines for the day, the factory had a total of \(\text{490,648}\) g of cereal that were ready to be packaged. On average, approximately how much cereal, in g, did the factory produce each minute after the machines were turned on for the day?
questions.explanation:
The difference between the starting value and the total after \(44\) minutes is the total amount made in the \(44\) minutes. Divide this difference by \(44\) to find the amount of cereal produced per minute:
$$\begin{aligned}\ \frac{\text{490,648}-\text{216,000}}{44}&=\text{6,242} \end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: In the linear function \(g\), \(g(2)=9\) and \(g(0)=15\). Which equation defines \(g\)?
questions.explanation:
Since \(g(0)=15\), the \(y\)-intercept, or \(b\) in the slope-intercept equations \(y=mx+b\) is \(15\). Eliminate B and D. Substitute \((2, 9)\) into the equation \(y=mx+15\) and solve for the slope, or \(m\):
$$\begin{aligned}\ 9&=2m+15\\9-15&=2m+\cancel{15}-\cancel{15}\\ \frac{-6}{2}&=\frac{\cancel{2}m}{\cancel{2}}\\ m&=-3 \end{aligned}$$
The slope is \(-3\), so the equation is \(g(x)=-3x+15\).

questions.stem:
$$x<73$$
$$10y+x<5$$
The point \((41,y)\) is a solution to the system of inequalities in the \(xy\)-plane. Which of the following could be the value of \(y\)?
questions.explanation:
Since only the second inequality has \(y\), substitute \(x=41\) into the second inequality and solve for \(y\):
$$\begin{aligned}\ 10y+41&\leq5 \\ 10y+\cancel{41}-\cancel{41} &\leq5-41 \\ \frac{\cancel{10}y}{\cancel{10}}&\leq\frac{-36}{10}\\ y&\leq-3.6 \\ -4&\leq-3.6 \end{aligned}$$

questions.stem:
$$\frac{4c}{5d}=\frac{3}{7f}$$
The given equation relates the positive numbers \(c\), \(d\), and \(f\). Which equation correctly expresses \(c\) in terms of \(d\) and \(f\)?
questions.explanation:
To isolate \(c\), multiply both sides by \(5d\) and then divide both sides by \(4\):
$$\begin{aligned}\ \cancel{5d}\times\frac{4c}{\cancel{5d}}&=\frac{3}{7f}\times5d \\ \frac{\cancel{4}c}{\cancel{4}}&=\frac{15d}{7f}\times\frac{1}{4}\\ c&=\frac{15d}{28f} \end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: The total cost \(g(x)\), in dollars, to rent a desk for \(24\) weeks in a certain communal office space is given by \(g(x)=24x+200\) where \(x\) is the weekly rental fee, in dollars. What is the total cost to rent a desk that has a weekly rental fee of \(\$500\)?
questions.explanation:
The weekly rental fee is \(x\), so to find the cost when the weekly rental fee is \(\$500\), substitute \(x=500\) and evaluate:
$$\begin{aligned} \ g(x)&=24x+200\\g(500)&=24(500)+200\\ g(500)&=\text{12,000}+200\\ g(500)&=\text{12,200} \end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: The function \(f\) is defined by \(f(x)=3x+18\). If the graph of \(y=f(x)\) in the \(xy\)-plane has an \(x\)-intercept at \((t,0)\) and a \(y\)-intercept at \((0,v)\), where \(t\) and \(v\) are constants, what is the value of \(v-t\)?
questions.explanation:
In the equation \(f(x)=3x+18\), the \(y\)-intercept is \(18\), so \(v=18\). Substitute \(f(x)=0\) into the equation and solve for \(x\), or \(t\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ 0&=3x+18\\ 0-18&=3x+\cancel{18}-\cancel{18}\\ \frac{-18}{3}&=\frac{\cancel{3}x}{\cancel{3}}\\ x&=-6 \end{aligned}$$
Substitute the value of \(t\) from \(v\): \(v-t=18-(-6)=24\).

questions.stem:
$$y=-2x+9$$
The equation above shows one of the equations in a system of two linear equations. The system has infinitely many solutions. Which equation could be the second equation in the system?
questions.explanation: A system that has infinitely many solutions means the two equations are equivalent, or produce the same line. Since the equation \(y=-2x+9\) is in slope-intercept form, but all of the answers are in standard form, the quickest way to find the correct answer is to transform the given equation into standard form by adding \(2x\) to both sides. The transformed equation is \(2x+y=9\).

questions.stem: An author has a typing rate of \(4\) pages per hour and a handwriting rate of \(2\) pages per hour. She types for \(t\) hours and writes for \(w\) hours for a combined total of \(30\) pages. Which equation represents this situation?
questions.explanation: The author types \(4\) pages per hour for \(t\) hours, so types \(4t\) pages. And she writes \(2\) pages per hour for \(w\) hours, so she writes \(2w\) pages. The sum of these pages typed and written is \(30\), so the correct equation is \(4t+2w=30\).

questions.stem: Line \(l\) is defined by \(12y-3x=16\). What is the slope of perpendicular line \(m\) in the \(xy\)-plane?
questions.explanation:
The slopes of perpendicular lines are negative reciprocals of one another. Start by transforming \(12y-3x=16\) into slope-intercept form to find the slope.
$$\begin{aligned} \ 12y-\cancel{3x}+\cancel{3x}&=3x+16\\ \frac{\cancel{12}y}{\cancel{12}}&=\frac{3x}{12}+\frac{16}{12}\\ y&=\frac{1}{4}x+\frac{16}{12} \end{aligned}$$
The slope of line \(l\) is \(\frac{1}{4}\), so the slope of line \(m\) is \(-4\).

questions.stem: The function \(h\) is defined by \(h(x)=\frac{x}{9}\). For what value of \(x\) does \(h(x)=3\)?
questions.explanation:
Substitute \(h(x)=3\) into the equation and solve for \(x\):
$$\begin{aligned}\ 3\times9&=\frac{x}{\cancel{9}}\times\cancel{9}\\x&=27 \end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: If \(\frac{k}{24}=\frac{k-3}{12}\), what is the value of \(\frac{24}{k}\)?
questions.explanation:
In the equation \(\frac{k}{24}=\frac{k-3}{12}\), since \(12\times2=24\), \(2(k-3)=k\). Solve this equation for \(k\) and plug the answer back into \(\frac{24}{k}\).
$$\begin{aligned} \ 2(k-3)&=k\\ 2k-6&=k\\ \cancel{2k}-6+\cancel{2k}&=k-2k\\ -6&=-k\\k&=6 \end{aligned}$$
\(k=6\), so \(\frac{24}{k}=\frac{24}{6}=4\).

questions.stem:
$$-7y=35$$
$$x+6y=-8$$
The solution to the given system of equations is \((x,y)\). What is the value of \(x-y\)?
questions.explanation:
This system can be solved without directly solving for \(x\) and \(y\). Since \(6y-7y=-y\), subtracting the first equation from the second gives \(x-y\):
$$\begin{array}{rcl}&x+6y&=-8\\+&-7y&=35\\\hline&x-y&=27\end{array}$$

questions.stem: A certain yarn used for knitting has a wholesale cost to a store of \(\$0.36\) per inch. Which of the following equations represents the total wholesale cost \(w\), in dollars, for \(f\) feet of the same yarn?
questions.explanation:
Convert the cost per inch to cost per feet, and then multiply by \(f\) feet to find the total cost.
$$\begin{aligned} \frac{0.36\,\text{dollars}}{1\,\cancel{\text{{inch}}}\times \frac{12\,\cancel{\text{{inches}}}{1\,\text{foot}} &= \frac{0.36\times12\,\text{dollars}}{1\,\text{foot}}\\ \frac{0.36\times12\,\text{dollars}}{1\,\text{foot}}\times \frac{f\,\cancel{\text{{feet}}}{1} &= 0.36\times12f \end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: The number of new schools that opened in New York City between 1950 and 1985 is one third the number of new schools that opened between 1986 and 2021. If \(42\) new schools opened between 1950 and 1985 and \(n\) new schools opened between 1986 and 2021, which of the following equations is true?
questions.explanation: The number of new schools that opened 1950-1985 is one third the number of new schools that opened 1986-2021, or \(\text{new schools 1950-1985} = \frac{1}{3} \text{(new schools 1986-2021)}\). Then substitute \(42\) as the number of new schools from 1950-1985, and substitute \(n\) as the number of new schools from 1986-2021. Now the equation is \(42=\frac{1}{3}n\) or \(42=\frac{n}{3}\).

questions.stem: If \(\frac{7}{15} n-\frac{2}{15}n=\frac{5}{6}+\frac{1}{2}\), what is the value of \(n\)?
questions.explanation: $$\begin{aligned} \frac{7}{15}n-\frac{2}{15}n&=\frac{5}{6}+\frac{1}{2}\\ \frac{5}{15}n&=\frac{5}{6}+\frac{6}{12}\\ 3\times \frac{1}{3}n&=\frac{11}{12} \times 3\\ n&=4 \end{aligned}$$

questions.stem:
$$2x+3y=9$$
$$5x+ny=10$$
In the system of equations above, \(n\) is a constant and \(x\) and \(y\) are variables. For what value of \(n\) will the system of equations have no solution?
questions.explanation:
When a system of equations has no solution, its equations have the same slope. Find the slope of the first equation and use that to write an equation and solve for \(n\).
$$\begin{aligned} 2x+3y &= 9\\ \cancel{2x}+3y-\cancel{2x} &= -2x+9\\ \frac{\cancel{3}y}{\cancel{3}} &= \frac{-2x}{3} + \frac{9}{3}\\ y &= \frac{-2}{3}x + 3 \end{aligned}$$
$$\begin{aligned}5x+ny &= 10\\ \cancel{5x}+ny-\cancel{5x} &= -5x+10\\ \frac{\cancel{n}y}{\cancel{n}} &= \frac{-5x}{n} + \frac{10}{n}\\ y &= \frac{-5}{n}x + \frac{10}{n} \end{aligned}$$
The slope of the first equation is \(\frac{-2}{3}\) and the slope of the second is \(\frac{-5}{n}\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ \frac{-2}{3}&=\frac{-5}{n}\\ \frac{\cancel{-2}n}{\cancel{-2}}&=\frac{-15}{-2}\\ n&=\frac{15}{2} \end{aligned}$$

questions.stem:
\(\boldsymbol{x}\)\(2\)\(3\)\(4\)\(5\)\(6\)
\(\boldsymbol{g(x)}\)\(-1\)\(3\)\(7\)\(11\)\(15\)
The table above shows some values of the linear function \(g\). Which of the following defines \(g\)?
questions.explanation:
The \(y\)-intercepts of all of the equations are different, so you only need to find the slope from the table:
Find the slope. \(\text{Slope} = \frac{\text{change in } g(x)}{\text{change in } x} = \frac{4}{1} = 4\).

questions.stem: The sum of three numbers is \(792\). One of the numbers, \(n\), is \(20\%\) less than the sum of the other two numbers. What is the value of \(n\)?
questions.explanation:
Let \(x=\) the sum of the other two numbers, so \(n+x=792\). Since \(n\) is \(20\%\) than \(x\), \(n=x-.2x\) or \(n=.8x\). Substitute \(.8x\) into the first equation to solve for \(x\). Then use the value of \(x\) to find \(n\).
$$\begin{aligned}n+x&=792\\8x+x&=792\\ 1.8x&=792\\ \frac{\cancel{1.8}x}{\cancel{1.8}}&=\frac{792}{1.8}\\x&=440\end{aligned}$$
$$\begin{aligned}n&=.8(440)\\n&=352\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: Megan has \(x\) stickers that she wants to put in gift bags for a party. If she puts \(4\) stickers in each bag, she will have \(4\) remaining stickers. If she wants to put \(5\) stickers in each bag, she would need an additional \(17\) stickers. How many gift bags does Megan have?
questions.explanation:
This can be solved directly or by testing answers. To solve directly, let \(b=\)number of bags. When Megan puts \(4\) stickers in each bag, she has \(4\) stickers leftover, so \(x=4b+4\). When she puts \(5\) stickers in each bag, she is short by \(17\) stickers, so \(x=5b-17\). Set these expressions for \(x\) equal to each other and solve for \(b\):
$$ egin{aligned}\ 4b+4&=5b-17\ \cancel{4b}+4+17-\cancel{4b}&=5b-4b-\cancel{17}+\cancel{17}\b&=21 \end{aligned}$$
Test answers:
A) \(4\) stickers in each bag: \(4 imes12+4=52\) stickers. \ \(5\) stickers in each bag: \(5 imes12-17=43\) stickers, incorrect (\(52 eq43\)).
B) \(4 imes17+4 eq5 imes17-17\)
C) \(4 imes19+4 eq5 imes19-17\)
D) \(4 imes21+4=5 imes21-17\)

questions.stem:
$$4x^3+5x^2-7$$
$$3x^3-9x^2+1$$
Which of the following is the sum of the two polynomials shown above?
questions.explanation:
Combine like terms to find the sum:
$$\begin{aligned}\ &=4x^3+5x^2-7+3x^3-9x^2+1\\ &=4x^3+3x^3+5x^2-9x^2-7+1\\&=7x^3-4x^2-6\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: If \(\frac{2}{5} f=\frac{5}{6}\), what is the value of \(f\)?
questions.explanation:
To solve for \(f\), divide both sides by \(\frac{2}{5}\), which is the equivalent to multiplying both sides by the reciprocal:
$$\begin{aligned}\ \frac{2}{5}f&=\frac{5}{6}\ \frac {\cancel{5}}{\cancel{2}}\times \frac{\cancel{2}}{\cancel{5}}f=\frac{5}{6} \times \frac{5}{2}\\ f&=\frac{25}{12} \end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: At Oceanview Aquarium, approximately \(9\) percent of the small tank fish and \(14\) percent of the large tank fish were donated to a new local aquarium. If there were \(632\) total small tank fish and \(817\) large tank fish at Oceanview Aquarium before they were donated, which of the following is closest to the total number of small and large tank fish that were donated to the new aquarium?
questions.explanation: The aquarium is donating \(9\%\) of the \(632\) fish in the small tank, so \(632\times.09=56.88\) fish. The aquarium is donating \(14\%\) of the \(817\) fish in the large tank, so \(817\times.14=114.38\) fish. The total fish the aquarium is donating is the sum of \(56.88\) and \(114.38\), so \(171.26\) or \(171\) fish.

questions.stem: Transportation to a science museum for a field trip includes vans that hold \(8\) people and cars that hold \(5\) people. If at least \(26\) but no more than \(39\) people plan to attend in \(n\) cars and \(2\) vans, what is one possible value of \(n\)?
questions.explanation:
This problem can be solved with a compound inequality. Write an expression that describes the total number of people on the field trip; there are \(2\) vans carrying \(8\) people each, so there are \(16\) people traveling in vans. There are \(n\) cars carrying \(5\) people each, so there are \(5n\) people in cars. In total, there are \(5n+16\) people going on the field trip. If there must be between \(26\) and \(39\) people (inclusive) on the trip, solve the inequality \(26\leq5n+16\leq39\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ 26&\leq5n+16\leq39\\ 26-16&\leq5n+\cancel{16}-\cancel{16}\leq39-16\\ \frac{10}{5}&\leq \frac{\cancel{5}n}{\cancel{5}}\leq \frac{23}{5}\\ 2&\leq n\leq \frac{23}{5} \end{aligned}$$
Since the answer is a number of cars, it must be a positive integer, so the options are \(2\), \(3\), or \(4\) cars. You can also test values of \(n\) by multiplying a tested value of \(n\) by \(5\) and adding \(16\) until you find a result between \(26\) and \(39\).

questions.stem: The average number of birds per acre in Cedar Park from 1990 to 2020 can be modeled by the equation \(y=-0.81x+1014.9\), where \(x\) represents the number of years since 1990, and \(y\) represents the average number of birds per acre in the park. Which of the following best describes the meaning of the number \(-0.81\) in the equation?
choices: The total number of birds in the park in 1990
The average number of birds per acre in 1990
The estimated decrease in the average number of birds per acre each year
The estimated difference between the average number of birds per acre in 1990 and in 2010
questions.explanation: In the equation \(y=-0.81x+1014.9\), \(-0.81\) is the slope, or rate of change. Since it is negative, this means it is decreasing. Since \(x\) is the number of years and \(y\) is the number of birds per acre, the slope, or change in \(y\) per unit of change in \(x\) is the decrease in the number of birds per acre per year.

questions.stem: If \(9-n=15\), what is the value of \(2n-18\)?
questions.explanation: You don't need to solve for \(n\) directly. Instead, transform \(9-n\) into \(2n-18\). When you do this, multiply both sides of the equation. Start by multiplying both sides by \(2\), so \(2(9-n)=15\times2\). Simplify to get \(18-2n=30\). The question asks you to find \(2n-18\), which is the opposite of \(18-2n\). Multiply both sides by \(-1\), so \(-1(18-2n)=30\times(-1)\), and \(-18+2n=-30\). Note: \(-18+2n=2n-18\).

questions.stem: How many times do the graphs of the equations \(x-4y=8\) and \(-2x+8y=8\) intersect in the \(xy\)-plane?
questions.explanation:
To find the number of solutions, transform both equations to slope-intercept (\(y=mx+b\)) and compare.
$$\begin{aligned}x-4y &= 8\\ \cancel{x}-4y-\cancel{x} &= -x+8\\ \frac{\cancel{-4}y}{\cancel{-4}} &= \frac{-x}{-4} + \frac{8}{-4}\\ y &= \frac{1}{4}x- 2 \end{aligned}$$
$$\begin{aligned}-2x+8y &= 8\\ \cancel{-2x}+8y+\cancel{2x} &= 2x+8\\ \frac{\cancel{8}y}{\cancel{8}} &= \frac{2x}{8} + \frac{8}{8}\\ y &= \frac{1}{4}x +1 \end{aligned}$$
Since the equations have the same slope (\(\frac{1}{4}\)) but different \(y\)-intercepts, they are parallel lines. Parallel lines do not intersect, and therefore the system has \(0\) solutions.

questions.stem: What is the value of \(3y\), if \(7=\frac{1}{2}y\)?
questions.explanation:
To solve for \(3y\) directly (without solving for \(y\) first), multiply both sides of the given equation by \(6\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ 7&=\frac{1}{2}y\\ 7\times 6&=\frac{1}{2}y \times 6\\42&=3y \end{aligned}$$
If you don't see that multiplying by \(6\) will give \(3y\) on the right hand side, you can also solve for \(y\) directly by multiplying both sides by \(2\), so \(y=14\). Then multiply by \(3\) to get \(3y=42\).

questions.stem:
$$E(S)=160+3S$$
The money that a university budgets for spending is a function of the number of students enrolled in the university. The function \(E(S)\) gives the amount of money, in thousands of dollars, that the university budgets for expenditures when \(S\) students enroll at the university. How will the amount budgeted for expenditures change if the number of enrolled students decreases by \(10\)?
choices: The expenditure budget will decrease by \(\$\text{30,000}\).
The expenditure budget will increase by \(\$\text{30,000}\).
The expenditure budget will decrease by \(\$\text{10,000}\).
The expenditure budget will increase by \(\$\text{10,000}\).
questions.explanation:
To find what happens when the number of enrolled students decreases by \(10\), substitute \(S=10\) and \(S=0\) into the given equation:
$$\begin{aligned} E(10)&=160+3(10)\\E(10)&=190 \end{aligned}$$
$$\begin{aligned}E(0)&=160+3(0)\\E(0)&=160\end{aligned}$$
Since the expenditure is in thousands of dollars, when the number of students goes from \(10\) to \(0\), the expenditure decreases from \(190-160=30\) thousand dollars.

questions.stem:
$$9c+16d=f$$
The given equation relates \(c\), \(d\), and \(f\). Which equation correctly expresses \(c\) in terms of \(d\) and \(f\)?
questions.explanation:
To isolate \(c\), subtract \(16d\) from both sides and then divide both sides by \(9\):
$$\begin{aligned}\ 9c+\cancel{16d}-\cancel{16d}&=f-16d \\ rac{\cancel{9}c}{\cancel{9}}&=\frac{f-16d}{9}\\ c&=\frac{f-16d}{9} \end{aligned}$$

questions.stem:
$$12x-20y=32$$
$$3x-5y=8$$
For each real number, \(k\), which of the following points lies on the graph of each equation in the \(xy\)-plane for the given system?
questions.explanation:
This is a special system of equations. Divide the first equation by \(4\):
$$\begin{aligned}\ \frac{12x}{4}-\frac{20y}{4}&=\frac{32}{4} \\ 3x-5y&=8 \end{aligned}$$
Since the equations are equivalent, any solution for one is a solution for the other. Substitute answer choices into the second equation. Start with the easiest substitutions, such as \(x=k\) (which appears in two answers).
$$\begin{aligned}\ \cancel{3k}-5y-\cancel{3k}&=8-3k \\ \frac{\cancel{-5}y}{\cancel{-5}}&=\frac{8}{-5}-\frac{3k}{-5} \\ y&=\frac{8}{-5}+\frac{3k}{5} \end{aligned}$$
Eliminate C and D. Next, substitute \(y=k\) and solve for \(x\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ 3x-\cancel{5k}+\cancel{5k}&=8+5k \\ \frac{\cancel{3}x}{\cancel{3}}&=\frac{8}{3}+\frac{5k}{3} \end{aligned}$$
This is equivalent to the \(y\)-value in answer choice B.

questions.stem:
\(x\)\(g(x)\)
\(1\)\(56\)
\(2\)\(0\)
\(3\)\(-56\)
For the linear function \(g\), the table shows three values of \(x\) and their corresponding values of \(g(x)\). Function \(g\) is defined by \(g(x)=cx+d\), where \(c\) and \(d\) are constants. What is the value of \(d-c\)?
questions.explanation:
In the function \(g(x)=cx+d\), \(c\) is the slope and \(d\) is the \(y-\)intercept. Use the table and the slope formula (\(m=\frac{y_2 - y_1}{x_2-x_1}\)) to find the slope. Substitute values from the table; \(m=\frac{56-0}{1-2}=-56\). This means \(c=-56\). To find the \(y-\)intercept, substitute an ordered pair from the table into the equation \(g(x)=-56x+d\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ 0&=-56(2)+d\\ 0+112&=\cancel{-112}+d+\cancel{112} \\ 112&=d\end{aligned}$$
Substitute \(c=-56\) and \(d=112\) into the expression \(d-c\) and evaluate; \(112- (-56)=168\).

questions.stem: A graph in the \(xy\)-plane has a \(y\)-intercept at \((0,c)\) and an \(x\)-intercept at \((d,0)\), where \(c\) and \(d\) are constants. If the graph has the equation \(-3x+11y=47\), what is the value of \(\frac{d}{c}\)?
questions.explanation:
To find the values of \(c\) and \(d\), substitute \(0\) in for each of \(x\) and \(y\).
$$\begin{aligned}-3(0)+11c&=47\\ rac{\cancel{11}c}{\cancel{11}}&=\frac{47}{11}\\c&=\frac{47}{11}\end{aligned}$$
$$\begin{aligned}-3d+11(0)&=47\\ rac{\cancel{-3}d}{\cancel{-3}}&=\frac{47}{-3}\\d&=\frac{47}{-3}\end{aligned}$$
Evaluate \(\frac{d}{c}\):
$$\begin{aligned}\frac{d}{c}&=\frac{47}{-3}\div \frac{47}{11}=\frac{\cancel{47}}{-3} imes \frac{11}{\cancel{47}} \\ &=-\frac{11}{3} \end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: The graph of \(y=h(x)-8\) is shown. Which equation defines function \(h\)?
questions.explanation:
The equation for the graph is given as \(y=h(x)-8\) and from the graph, the equation can also be written as \(y=\frac{2}{3}x-1\). Set these expressions for \(y\) equal to each other and solve for \(h(x)\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ h(x)-8&=\frac{2}{3}x-1\\ h(x)-\cancel{8}+\cancel{8}&=\frac{2}{3}x-1+8\\ h(x)&=\frac{2}{3}x+7\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem:
\(x\)\(y\)
\(29\)\(194\)
\(37\)\(234\)
\(42\)\(259\)
For line \(l\), the table shows three values of \(x\) and their corresponding values of \(y\). Line \(m\) is the result of translating line \(l\) up \(7\) units in the \(xy\)-plane. What is the \(x\)-intercept of line \(m\)?
questions.explanation:
Translating line \(m\) up \(7\) units adds \(7\) to all \(y\)-values as shown in the table. The slope of line \(m\) is \(\frac{40}{8}=5\). Use the slope formula \(m=\frac{y_2 - y_1}{x_2 - x_1}\) to find the \(x\)-intercept. Use one point from the table and \(y=0\) to solve for the \(x\)-intercept.
$$\begin{aligned}\ 5&=\frac{201-0}{29-x}\\ 5(29-x)&=\frac{201}{\cancel{29-x}} imes \cancel{(29-x)} \\ \cancel{145}-5x-\cancel{145}&=201-145 \\ \frac{\cancel{-5}x}{\cancel{-5}}&=\frac{56}{-5} \\x&=-\frac{56}{5}\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: If \(21x+20=3(5x+12)+2x\), what is the value of \(4x\)?
questions.explanation:
Instead of solving the equation for \(x\), do a series of operations to solve for \(4x\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ 21x+20&=3(5x+12)+2x\\ 21x+20&=15x+36+2x\\21x+\cancel{20}-\cancel{20}-15x-2x&=\cancel{15x}+36+\cancel{2x}-\cancel{15x}-\cancel{2x}-20\\ 4x&=16 \end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: In the \(xy\)-plane, line \(l\) is parallel to the line represented by the equation \(y=x-6\) and line \(l\) also passes through the point \((0,4)\). If line \(l\) also passes through the point \((n,13)\), what is the value of \(n\)?
questions.explanation:
Parallel lines have the same slope, so the slope of line \(l\) is \(1\). The y-intercept of line \(l\) is (0,4), so \(b\) is 4 in \(y=mx+b\). The equation for line \(l\) is \(y=x+4\). Substitute the point \((n,13)\) and solve for \(n\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ 13&=n+4\\13-4&=n+\cancel{4}-\cancel{4} \\ n&=9\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem:
$$4x+3y=-21$$
$$3x+4y=7$$
f \((x,y)\) is a solution to the system of equations above, what is the value of \(x+y\)?
questions.explanation:
Instead of solving this system completely, start by adding the equations. The result is a multiple of \(x+y\), so can be divided to solve directly for \(x+y\).
$$\begin{aligned} 4x + 3y &= -21 \\ 3x + 4y &= 7 \\ \hline 7x + 7y &= -14 \end{aligned}$$
Divide the entire equation by \(7\) to get \(x+y=-2\).

questions.stem:
$$4x-5y=3$$
$$jx-ky=3$$
In the given pair of equations, \(j\) and \(k\) are constants and the graphs of these equations in the \(xy\)-plane form perpendicular lines. Which of the following pairs of equations also have graphs of perpendicular lines?
questions.explanation:
Perpendicular lines have slopes that are negative reciprocals of each other. Find the slope of \(4x-5y=3\). The equation \(4x-5y=3\) is in standard form \(Ax+By=C\), where the slope is \(\frac{-A}{B}\), or \(\frac{-4}{-5}=\frac{4}{5}\).
In the same way, the slope of \(jx-ky=3\) is \(\frac{-j}{-k}\). Using perpendicular slopes it is also \(-\frac{5}{4}\). Since \(\frac{j}{k}=-\frac{5}{4}\), you can say \(j=-5\) and \(k=4\).
Test answers by finding their slopes and determining whether they are negative reciprocals of each other.
A) \(4x-15y=3\): slope\(=\frac{-4}{-15}=\frac{4}{15}\)
\(-3jx-ky=3\): slope\(=\frac{-(-3)(-5)}{-(4)}=\frac{15}{4}\)
Since \(\frac{4}{15}\) and \(\frac{15}{4}\) are not negative reciprocals, A is incorrect.
B) \(4x-15y=3\): slope\(=\frac{4}{15}\)
\(3jx-ky=3\): slope\(=\frac{-(3)(-5)}{-(4)}=-\frac{15}{4}\)
Since \(\frac{4}{15}\) and \(-\frac{15}{4}\) are negative reciprocals, B is correct.

questions.stem: A bicycle rental company charges a daily rental fee of \(\$30\) for regular bicycles and \(\$40\) for electric bicycles. On a particular day, the company earns \(\$\text{1,760}\) and rents \(52\) of the bicycles in its inventory that day. How many of the bicycles were regular bicycles?
questions.explanation:
Method \(1\): Write and solve a system of equations. Let \(r=\) number of regular bikes and \(e=\) number of electric bikes. There are \(52\) bikes in total, so \(r+e=52\). Each regular bike costs \(\$30\) so the total for regular bikes is \(30r\) and each electric bike costs \(\$40\), so the total for electric bikes is \(40e\). The company earns \(\$\text{1,760}\), so \(30r+40e=\text{1,760}\). To find the number of regular bikes, re-write the first equation, so \(e=52-r\) and substitute \(52-r\) into the second equation.
$$\begin{aligned}\ 30r+40e&=\text{1,760} \\30r+40(52-r)&=\text{1,760}\\ 30r+\text{2,080}-40r&=\text{1,760} \\ -10r+\cancel{\text{2,080}}-\cancel{\text{2,080}}&=\text{1,760}-\text{2,080} \\ \frac{\cancel{-10}r}{\cancel{-10}}&=\frac{-320}{-10} \\ r&=32\end{aligned}$$
Method 2: Test answers:
A) \(8\) regular bikes means \(44\) electric bikes. \(30(8)+40(44) eq \text{1,760}\)
B) \(20\) regular bikes means \(32\) electric bikes. \(30(20)+40(32) eq \text{1,760}\)
C) \(32\) regular bikes means \(20\) electric bikes. \(30(32)+40(20)=\text{1,760}\)

questions.stem:
$$10x+30y=3-10x$$
$$nx=\frac{3}{5}-6y$$
In the given system of equations, \(n\) is a constant. If the system has infinitely many solutions, what is the value of \(n\)?
questions.explanation:
If a system has infinitely many solutions, the two equations are equivalent (same slope and same \(y\)-intercept). In other words, they produce the same line. To find the value of \(n\) put the equations in the same form. In this case, standard form, \(Ax+By=C\) works well.
$$\begin{aligned}10x+30y &= 3-10x\\10x+30y +10x&= 3-\cancel{10x}+\cancel{10x}\\20x+30y&=3 \end{aligned}$$
$$\begin{aligned}nx &= \frac{3}{5}-6y\\nx+6y&= \frac{3}{5}-\cancel{6y}+\cancel{6y}\\5(nx+6y)&=\frac{3}{\cancel5} imes \cancel{5}\\ 5nx+30y&=3\end{aligned}$$
div class='explanation-step'>Since \( oldsymbol{20}x+30y=3\) and \( oldsymbol{5n}x+30y=3\), \(20=5n\) and \(n=4\).

questions.stem: One liter of a cleaning solution will wash \(60\) square meters of a surface. A hotel has a floor area of \(f\) square meters. Which equation represents the total amount of cleaning solution \(C\), in liters, needed to wash the floors of the hotel three times?
questions.explanation:
Method \(1\): Choose a value for \(f\). Let \(f=180\). If one liter is used for \(60\) square meters, \(3\) liters are needed for \(180\) square meters. To wash the \(180\) square meters \(3\) times, \(3 imes3=9\) liters are needed. Test the answers by substituting \(f=180\) to find the answer that gives \(C=9\):
A) \(C=\frac{180}{20}=9\)
Test the other answers in case the value you chose for \(f\) gives a false positive answer.
B) \(C=20(180) eq9\)
C) \(C=\frac{180}{60} eq9\)
D) \(C=60(180) eq9\)
Method \(2\): Start with \(\frac{1\, \text{liter}}{60\, \text{square meters}}\). Use unit conversions to calculate the number of liters needed to wash the floors \(3\) times:
$$\begin{aligned}\ \frac{1\, \text{liter}}{60\, \text{\cancel{square meters}}} imes \frac{f \, \text{\cancel{square meters}}}{1\, \text{wash}}&=\frac{\frac{f}{60}\text{liters}}{1\, \text{wash}} \\ frac{\frac{f}{60}\text{liters}}{1\, \text{\cancel{wash}}} imes3 \, \text{\cancel{washes}}&=\frac{3f}{60}=\frac{f}{20}\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem:
$$x=3y-5$$
$$5x=17y+7$$
The solution to the given system of equations is \((x,y)\). What is the value of \(x+y\)?
questions.explanation:
To find \(x+y\), solve the system of equations. Substitute \(3y-5\) into the second equation in place of \(x\).
$$\begin{aligned}5x&=17y+7\\5(3y-5)&=17y+17\\15y-25&=17y+17\\15y-\cancel{25}+\cancel{25}-17y&=\cancel{17y}+17+25-\cancel{17y}\\ \frac{\cancel{-2}y}{\cancel{-2}}&=\frac{32}{-2}\\y&=-16\end{aligned}$$
$$\begin{aligned}x&=3y-5\\x&=3(-16)-5\\x&=-48-5\\x&=-53\end{aligned}$$
Since \(x=-53\) and \(y=-16\), \(x+y=-53+-16=-69\).

questions.stem: At an office supply store, each box of plastic folders costs 40 cents less than each box of paper folders. If \(4\) boxes of plastic folders and \(3\) boxes of paper folders cost a total of \(\text{1,800}\) cents, how many cents is one box of plastic folders?
questions.explanation:
Write and solve a system of equations. Let \(x=\) cost of one box of plastic folders and \(y=\) cost of one box of paper folders. A box of plastic folders costs \(40\) cents less than one box of paper folders, so \(x=y-40\). The sum of \(4\) boxes of plastic folders and \(3\) boxes of paper folders costs \(1800\) cents, so \(4x+3y=1800\). Re-write the first equation to \(y=x+40\) and substitute \(x+40\) into the second equation in place of \(y\) and solve for \(x\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ 4x+3y&=1800\\4x+3(x+40)&=1800\\4x+3x+120&=1800\\7x+\cancel{120}-\cancel{120}&=1800-120\\ \frac{\cancel{7}x}{\cancel{7}}&=\frac{1680}{7}\\x&=240\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: In the \(xy\)-plane, line \(m\) is perpendicular to the line represented by the equation \(y=3x+7\) and line \(m\) also passes through the point \((0,1)\). If line \(m\) also passes through the point \((9,f),\) what is the value of \(f\)?
questions.explanation:
Line \(m\) is perpendicular to the graph of \(y=3x+7\), so their slopes are \textit{negative reciprocals}. The slope of \(y=3x+7\) is \(3\), so the slope of line \(m\) is \(-\frac{1}{3}\). Additionally, \((0,1)\) is the \(y\)-intercept of line \(m\). The slope-intercept equation for line \(m\) is therefore \(y=-\frac{1}{3}x+1\). Substitute \((9,f)\) into the equation to find the value of \(f\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ y&=-\frac{1}{3}x+1\\f&=-\frac{1}{3}(9)+1\\f&=-3+1\\f&=-2 \end{aligned}$$

questions.stem:
$$2x+3y=7$$
$$cx+dy=7$$
In the given pair of equations, \(c\) and \(d\) are constants and the graphs of these equations in the \(xy\)-plane form parallel lines. Which of the following pairs of equations have graphs of perpendicular lines?
questions.explanation:
Parallel lines have equal slopes, so find the slope of \(2x+3y=7\). The equation is in standard form \(x+By=C\), where the slope is \(\frac{-A}{B}\), or \(\frac{-2}{3}\).
The slope of \(cx+dy=7\) is \(\frac{-c}{d}\). Set the slopes equal to find the possible values of \(c\) and \(d\); \(\frac{-2}{3}=\frac{-c}{d}\). Multiply (or divide) both sides by \(-1\) to get \(\frac{2}{3}=\frac{c}{d}\). Therefore, you can say \(c=2\) and \(d=3\) (or some equal ratio).
The graphs of the answer choice pairs form perpendicular lines, so their slopes are \textit{negative reciprocals}. Test answers by finding their slopes and determining whether they are negative reciprocals of each other.
A) \(x-2y=7\): slope\(=\frac{-1}{-2}=\frac{1}{2}\)
\(-cx-3dy=7\): slope\(=\frac{c}{-(3d)}=\frac{2}{-9}\)
Since \(\frac{1}{2}\) and \(\frac{2}{-9}\) are not negative reciprocals, A is incorrect.
B) \(x+2y=7\): slope\(=\frac{-1}{2}\)
\(cx-3dy=3\): slope\(=\frac{-c}{-(3d)}=-\frac{-2}{-9}\)
Since \(\frac{-1}{2}\) and \(-\frac{2}{9}\) are not negative reciprocals, B is incorrect.
C) \(x+2y=7\): slope\(=\frac{-1}{2}\)
Parallel lines have equal slopes, so find the slope of \(2x+3y=7\). The equation is in standard form \(Ax+By=C\), where the slope is \(\frac{-A}{B}\), or \(\frac{-2}{3}\).
The slope of \(cx+dy=7\) is \(\frac{-c}{d}\). Set the slopes equal to find the possible values of \(c\) and \(d\); \(\frac{-2}{3}=\frac{-c}{d}\). Multiply (or divide) both sides by \(-1\) to get \(\frac{2}{3}=\frac{c}{d}\). Therefore, you can say \(c=2\) and \(d=3\) (or some equal ratio).
The graphs of the answer choice pairs form perpendicular lines, so their slopes are \textit{negative reciprocals}. Test answers by finding their slopes and determining whether they are negative reciprocals of each other.
A) \(x-2y=7\): slope\(=\frac{-1}{-2}=\frac{1}{2}\)
\(-cx-3dy=7\): slope\(=\frac{c}{-(3d)}=\frac{2}{-9}\)
Since \(\frac{1}{2}\) and \(\frac{2}{-9}\) are not negative reciprocals, A is incorrect.
B) \(x+2y=7\): slope\(=\frac{-1}{2}\)
\(cx-3dy=3\): slope\(=\frac{-c}{-(3d)}=-\frac{-2}{-9}\)
Since \(\frac{-1}{2}\) and \(-\frac{2}{9}\) are not negative reciprocals, B is incorrect.
C) \(x+2y=7\): slope\(=\frac{-1}{2}\)
\(3cx-dy=3\): slope\(=\frac{-(3c)}{-d}=-\frac{6}{-3}=-2\)
Although \(\frac{-1}{2}\) and \(-2\) are reciprocals, they are not opposites, so C is incorrect.
D) \(x-2y=7\): slope\(=\frac{-1}{-2}=\frac{1}{2}\)
\(3cx-dy=3\): slope\(=\frac{-(3c)}{-d}=-\frac{6}{-3}=-2\)
Since \(\frac{1}{2}\) and \(-2\) are negative reciprocals, D is correct.

questions.stem:
$$2ab+3c=a+6c$$
The given equation relates \(a\), \(b\), and \(c\). Which equation correctly expresses \(a\) in terms of \(b\) and \(c\)?
questions.explanation:
To isolate \(a\), get the terms with \(a\) on one side of the equation (and the other terms on the other side of the equation).
$$\begin{aligned}\ 2ab-a\cancel{3c}-\cancel{3c}&=\cancel{a}-\cancel{a}+6c-3c\\ 2ab-a&=3c \end{aligned}$$
Since \(2ab\) and \(a\) cannot be combined (not like terms), factor out \(a\) on the left hand side of the equation, so \(2ab-a=a(2b-1)\). Divide both sides by \(2b-1\):
$$\begin{aligned}\ \frac{a(\cancel{2b-1})}{\cancel{2b-1}}&=\frac{3c}{2b-1} \end{aligned}$$
The equation that expresses \(a\) in terms of \(b\) and \(c\) is \(a=\frac{3c}{2b-1}\).

questions.stem: If \(8(2x-1)+4x=2x+16\), what is the value of \(\frac{9x}{2}\)?
questions.explanation:
Instead of solving the equation for \(x\), do a series of operations to solve for \(\frac{9x}{2}\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ 8(2x-1)+4x&=2x+16\\16x\cancel{-8}+4x-2x\cancel{+8}&=\cancel{2x} \cancel{-2x}+16+8\\ 18x&=24 \end{aligned}$$
To solve for \(9x\), divide both sides by \(2\), so \(9x=12\). Finally, to solve for \(\frac{9x}{2}\), divide both sides again by \(2\), so \(\frac{9x}{2}=6\).

questions.stem: Each year, the attendance at a conference increases by \(0.49\%\) of its attendance from the previous year. Which of the following functions best models how the conference attendance changes over time?
questions.explanation: Change by a constant multiplier (increasing by \(0.49\%\), so \(1+.0049=1.0049\)) is exponential growth, so is modeled by an increasing exponential function.

questions.stem: The \(x\)-intercept of the graph shown is \((x,0)\). What is the value of \(x\)?
questions.explanation: The \(x\)-intercept is the point or points where the graph intersects the \(x\)-axis. The graph shows this happens at \((14,0)\), so \(x=14\).

questions.stem: The value of a car depreciates from \(\$\text{21,000}\) to \(\$\text{10,500}\) at a rate of \(10\%\) per year. What type of function best models the relationship between the car’s value and time?
questions.explanation: Change by a constant multiplier (depreciates by \(10\%\), so \(1-.1=.9\)) is exponential decay, so is modeled by a decreasing exponential function.

questions.stem: What is the \(y\)-intercept of the graph shown?
questions.explanation: The \(y\)-intercept is the point where the graph intersects the \(y\)-axis. The graph shows this happens at \((0,4)\).

questions.stem: The graph of \(y=g(x)\) is shown in the \(xy\)-plane. What is the value of \(g(0)\)?
questions.explanation: The \(y\)-value at \(g(0)\) is the \(y\)-intercept, or the point where the graph intersects the \(y\)-axis. The graph shows this happens at \((0,-6)\), so the \(y\)-value is \(-6\).
