questions.stem: The area of a rectangle, in square meters, is defined by the function \(g(l)=\frac{1}{2}l^2\), where its length is \(l\) m and its width is \(\frac{1}{2}\) its length. Which of the following is the best interpretation of \(g(32)=512\)?
choices: If the length of the rectangle is \(32\) m, then the area of the rectangle is \(512\) m\(^2\).
If the length of the rectangle is \(32\) m, then the width of the rectangle is \(512\) m.
If the length of the rectangle is \(512\) m, then the width of the rectangle is \(32\) m.
If the length of the rectangle is \(512\) m, then the area of the rectangle is \(32\) m\(^2\).
questions.explanation: \(l\) is the length of the rectangle and \(g(l)\) is the area of the rectangle. Since \(g(l)=\frac{1}{2} l^2\) , and \(g(32)=512\), \(32\) is the length and \(512\) is the area.

questions.stem: Which expression is equivalent to \((a^{-4}b^{}c^{7} )(a^{6} b^{3} c^{2} )\), where \(a\), \(b\), and \(c\) are positive?
questions.explanation:
Use the laws of exponents to simplify \((a^{-4}b^{}c^{7} )(a^{6} b^{3} c^{2} )\). The product rule states that when multiplying powers with the same base, you can add the exponents.
$$\begin{aligned}\ &=(a^{-4}b^{}c^{7} )(a^{6} b^{3} c^{2} )\\ &=a^{-4}a^{6}b^{1} b^3c^{7} c^{2}\\&=a^{2}b^{4} c^{9} \end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: The equation \(F(t)=8(1.3)^t\) gives the estimated number of trees in a new public park, where t is the number of years since the park was established. Which of the following is the best interpretation of the number \(8\) in this context?
choices: The estimated number of trees in the park when it was first established
The increase in the estimated number of trees each year
The estimated number of years since the park was established
The percent increase in the estimated number of trees each year
questions.explanation:
In the equation \(y=a imes b^x\), \(a\) is the starting value, so in the function \(F(t)=8(1.3)^t\), \(8\) is the starting value. This can also be shown by substituting \(t=0\) years into the equation:
$$\begin{aligned}\ F(0)&=8(1.3)^0\\F(0)&=8\times1\\F(0)&=8 \end{aligned}$$
\(0\) years after the park was established there were \(8\) trees.

questions.stem: Each year, the value of a car decreases by \(\$\text{1,200}\) of its value the previous year. Which of the following functions best represents the change in the value of the car over time?
questions.explanation: Change by a constant value is linear, and since this situation has a decrease of a constant value of \(\$\text{1,200}\), it is modeled by a decreasing linear function.

questions.stem:
$$x=55$$
$$x=y^2+6$$
The graphs of the given equations in the \(xy\)-plane intersect at the point \((x,y)\). What is a possible value of \(y\)?
questions.explanation:
This is a non-linear system of equations. Since \(x\) has a constant value of \(55\), substitute \(55\) into the second equation for \(x\) and solve for \(y\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ x&=y^2+6\\55&=y^2+6\\55-6&=y^2+\cancel{6}-\cancel{6}\\ \sqrt{49}&=\sqrt{y^2}\\y&=\pm 7\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: The equation \(f(x)=x^2-4x+39\) defines the function \(f\). Which is the value of \(f(-1)\)?
questions.explanation:
Substitute \(-1\) in for \(x\) in the function \(f(x)=x^2-4x+39\) and evaluate.
$$\begin{aligned}\ f(-1)&=(-1)^2-4(-1)+39\\f(-1)&=1+4+39=44\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: Which expression is equivalent to \((r s^4t^3 )^2(r^2 s^6 t^2)\), where \(r\), \(s\), and \(t\) are positive?
questions.explanation:
Use the laws of exponents to simplify the given expression. Use the Power of a Product Rule for \((r s^4t^3 )^2\) by multiplying the exponents inside the parentheses by the exponent (\(2\)) outside the parentheses. \((r s^4t^3 )^2=r^2s^8t^3\). Next use the Product Rule; add the exponents of like bases.
$$\begin{aligned}\ &=(r^2s^8t^3)(r^2 s^6 t^2)\\&=r^2r^2s^8s^6t^3t^2=r^4s^{14}t^8\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: The function \(g\) is defined by \(g(x)=x^2-10x+112\). What is the value of \(g(4)\)?
questions.explanation:
Substitute \(4\) into the equation and simplify (do the math):
$$\begin{aligned}\ &f(4)=4^2-10(4)+112\\&f(4)=16-40+112=88\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: The function \(g\) is defined by \(g(x)=500(\frac{1}{2})^{\frac{x}{72}}\) and models the number of mice there are in a certain population \(x\) days after an initial observation. How many days does it take for the number of mice in the population to halve?
questions.explanation:
In the exponential function \(y=a \times b^{rt}\), \(r\) is the rate of growth or decay. In the function \(g\) \(x\) is \(t\) or time, and \(\frac{1}{72}\) is the rate of decay, so every \(72\) days the mouse population halves. This can also be seen by substituting \(t=72\):
$$\begin{aligned}\ g(72)&=500 \left(\frac{1}{2} \right)^{\frac{72}{72}}\\ g(72)&=500 \left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^{1}\\ g(72)&=500 \left(\frac{1}{2}\right)=250\end{aligned}$$
The population goes from \(500\) to \(250\) in \(72\) days; when substituting \(t=72\), the exponent simplifies to \(1\), which represents one halving period.

questions.stem: The function \(h\) is defined by \(h(x)=x^2-8\). Which can be a value of \(x\) when \(h(x)=24\)?
questions.explanation:
Substitute \(24\) into the equation in place of \(h(x)\), and solve for \(x\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ h(x)&=x^2-8\\ 24&=x^2-8\\ 24+8&=x^2-\cancel{8}+\cancel{8}\\ \sqrt{x^2}&=\sqrt{32}\end{aligned}$$
To simplify \(\sqrt{32}\), split \(32\) into the product of a perfect square and its factor pair. \(\sqrt{32}=\sqrt{16 imes 2}=4 imes \sqrt{2}\).

questions.stem:
$$t^2-14t-32=0$$
What is one of the solutions to the given equation?
questions.explanation: To find the solutions to this quadratic equation, factor the left hand side. Find factors of \(-32\) that have a sum of \(-14\). Since \(-16 \times 2 = -32\) and \(-16+2=-14\), the equation can be written as \((t-16)(t+2)=0\). Use the Zero Product Property to solve: \(t-16=0\) and \(t+2=0\), so \(t=16\) or \(t=-2\).

questions.stem: <div class='stem-text'>A ball is rolled off of a roof.  The equation \(h=-16t^2-25t+44\) represents this situation, where \(h\) is the height of the ball above the ground, in feet, \(t\) seconds after it is rolled off of the roof.  Which number represents the height, in feet, of the roof off which the ball was rolled?
questions.explanation:
The height of the roof will be the height of the ball at \(0\) seconds. Substitute \(t=0\) into the equation and evaluate.
$$\begin{aligned}\ h&=-16(0)^2-25(0)+44\\ h&=0+0+44\\ h&=44\end{aligned}$$
It is also useful to know that in this type of question, the initial height will always be the \(y\)-intercept, or the value of \(c\) in the equation \(y=ax^2+bx+c\).

questions.stem: A rectangle has a width of \(n\) units and a length of \((n+16)\) units. If the rectangle has an area of \(105\) square units, what is the value of \(n\)?
questions.explanation:
Use the area formula to write and solve an equation for the value of \(n\). The formula for the area of a rectangle is \(A=l imes w\). Substitute the given values, so \(105=n(n+16)\). Distribute the right hand side and move \(105\) to the right to set the equation equal to \(0\):
$$\begin{aligned}\ 105&=n(n+16)\\105&=n^2+16n\\0&=n^2+16n-105 \end{aligned}$$
Factor by finding factors of \(-105\) that have a sum of \(16\); since \(-5 imes 21=-105\) and \(-5+21=16\):
$$\begin{aligned}\ 0&=n^2+16n-105\\0&=(n+21)(n-5) \end{aligned}$$
Use the Zero Product Property to solve: \(n+21=0\) and \(n-5=0\), so \(n=-21\) or \(n=5\). Since \(n\) is a dimension of a rectangle, it must be positive, so \(n=5\).

questions.stem: The function \(h\) is defined by \(h(x)= 1,400(\frac{3}{2})^x\). What is the value of \(h(0)\)?
questions.explanation:
To find \(h(0)\), substitute \(0\) in for \(x\) and evaluate. This expression uses the Zero Exponent Rule; \(a^0=1\) for any non-zero value of \(a\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ h(0)&= \text{1,400}\left( \frac{3}{2} \right)^0\\h(0)&= \text{1,400}(1)\\h(0)&= \text{1,400} \end{aligned}$$

questions.stem:
$$h(x)=16(\frac{1}{5})^x$$
If the given function \(h\) is graphed in the \(xy\)-plane, where \(y=h(x)\), what is the \(y\)-intercept of the graph?
questions.explanation:
The \(y\)-intercept is the value of \(y\) when \(x=0\). Substitute \(0\) in for \(x\) and evaluate. This expression uses the Zero Exponent Rule; \(a^0=1\) for any non-zero value of \(a\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ h(0)&= 16 \left(\frac{1}{5}\right)^0\\ h(0)&= 16(1)\\ h(0)&= 16\end{aligned}$$

$$f(x)=x^2-5$$
Which table gives three values of \(x\) and their corresponding \(f(x)\) for the given function \(f\)?
questions.explanation:
Substitute values of \(x\) from the tables to determine which table is the table for the given function.
$$\begin{aligned}\ \text{Substitute} x=1:\\ f(1)=1^2-5 = -4\\ \text{Don't test} x=2 \text{because answers A and B have the same} y-\text{values.}\\\text{Substitute} x=3:\\f(3)=3^2-5 =9-5=4 \text{Answer B has the correct corresponding} y-\text{value for} x=3. \end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: Given the function \(h\), defined by \(h(k)= 2k^4\), what is the value of \(k\) when \(h(k)\) is equal to \(162\)?
questions.explanation:
Substitute \(162\) in for \(h(k)\) and solve for \(k\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ h(k)&= 2k^4\\ \frac{162}{2}&= \frac{\cancel{2}k^4}{\cancel{2}}\\ \sqrt[4]{81}&= \sqrt[4]{k^4}\\ k&=3\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: Which expression is equivalent to \(\sqrt[5]{a^3 b^3}\), where \(a\) and \(b\) are positive?
questions.explanation: To convert between exponential and radical form, use \(\sqrt[n]{a^m}=a^{\frac{m}{n}}\), so \(\sqrt[5]{a^3 b^3}=a^{\frac{3}{5}}b^{\frac{3}{5}}\). Then use the power of a product rule: \(a^{\frac{3}{5}}b^{\frac{3}{5}}=(ab)^{\frac{3}{5}}\).

questions.stem: The value of a certain investment increased by \(9\%\) from 1996 to 1997. If the value of the investment in 1997 is \(n\) times the value of the investment in 1996, what is the value of \(n\)?
questions.explanation: Since the value of the investment increased by \(9\%\) from 1996 to 1997, the investment in 1997 is \(100\%\) of its value in 1996 plus an additional \(9\%\), or \(I_{1997}=I_{1996} + 9\%(I_{1996})=109\%(I_{1996})=1.09 imes I_{1996}\).

questions.stem: If \(|5n+25|=230\), what is the positive value of \(n+5\)?
questions.explanation:
Since you only need to solve for the positive value of \(n+5\), drop the absolute value sign and divide the entire equation by \(5\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ 5n+25&=230\\ rac{\cancel{5}n}{\cancel{5}}+\frac{25}{5}&=\frac{230}{5}\\n+5&=46 \end{aligned}$$
Note: solving for the positive value doesn't always mean you set the equation equal to the positive value (\(230\) instead of \(-230\)). For example, in the equation \(|-5n+25|=230\), you would need to solve the equation \(-5n+25=-230\) in order to find the positive solution.

questions.stem: Which expression is equivalent to \(4n^5+10n^6\)?
questions.explanation:
From the answers, notice that this is a factoring problem. Eliminate A because if the terms cannot be combined before factoring, they cannot be combined after factoring. Find the greatest common factor (GCF) of \(4n^5\) and \(10n^6\) and divide both terms by it to find what remains inside the parentheses. The GCF is \(2n^5\) because \(2\) is the largest integer that \(4\) and \(10\) are divisible by and \(5\) is the smaller of the two exponents of \(n\).
$$\begin{aligned}\frac{4\cancel{n^5}}{2\cancel{n^5}} &= 2\end{aligned}$$
$$\begin{aligned}\frac{10n^6}{2n^5} &= 5n^{6-5}=5n\\\end{aligned}$$
Factor to get \(4n^5+10n^6=2n^5(2+5n)\)

questions.stem:
$$g(x)=-6x^2+30x+72$$
The given function, \(g(x)\) defines \(x\). For what value of \(x\) does the function reach its maximum?
questions.explanation:
The maximum value of \(x\) occurs at the vertex of the parabola. To find the vertex, use \(x=-\frac{b}{2a}\) where \(y=ax^2+bx+c\). In the function \(g(x)=-6x^2+30x+72\), \(a=-6\) and \(b=30\).
$$\begin{aligned}\x&= -\frac{30}{2(-6)}\\x&= -\frac{30}{-12}\\x&= \frac{5}{2} \end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: Which expression is equivalent to \(2y^3- 15y^2\)?
questions.explanation:
From the answers, notice that this is a factoring problem. Find the greatest common factor of \(2y^3\) and \(15y^2\) and divide both terms by it to find what is remaining inside the parentheses. The greatest common factor is \(y^2\) because \(2\) and \(15\) do not share factors other than \(1\), and \(2\) is the smaller of the two exponents of \(y\).
$$\begin{aligned}\frac{2y^3}{y^2}&= 2y^{3-2}=2y\end{aligned}$$
$$\begin{aligned}\frac{15{\cancel{y^2}}}{\cancel{y^2}}&= 15\\ \end{aligned}$$
Factor to get \(2y^3+15y^2=y^2(2y-15)\)

questions.stem:
$$y=x^2+12$$
$$y=133$$
The graphs of the given equations in the \(xy\)-plane intersect at the point \((x,y)\). What is a possible value of \(x\)?
questions.explanation:
This is a non-linear system of equations. Since \(y\) has a constant value of \(133\), substitute \(133\) into the first equation for \(y\) and solve for \(x\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ y&=x^2+12\\133&=x^2+12\\133-12&=x^2+\cancel{12}-\cancel{12}\\\sqrt{121}&=\sqrt{x^2}\\x&=\pm11 \end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: The function \(g\) is defined by \(g(x)=9(\frac{1}{3})^x-30\). What is the \(y\)-intercept of the graph of \(y=g(x)\) in the \(xy\)-plane?
questions.explanation:
The \(y\)-intercept is the value of \(y\) when \(x=0\). Substitute \(0\) in for \(x\) and evaluate the function.
$$\begin{aligned}\ g(0)&=9 \left(\frac{1}{3} \right)^0-30\\ g(0)&=9(1)-30\\ g(0)&=9-30\\ g(0)&=-21\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem:
$$y=2$$
$$y=x^2+6x+m$$
In the given system of equations, \(m\) is a positive constant. The system has exactly one distinct real solution. What is the value of \(m\)?
questions.explanation:
This is a non-linear system of equations. Since \(y\) has a constant value of \(2\), substitute \(2\) into the second equation for \(y\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ 2&=x^2+6x+m\end{aligned}$$
div class='explanation-step'>Since this is a system with one real solution, you can treat the equation above like a quadratic equation with one solution. If a quadratic equation has one real solution, the discriminant, \(b^2-4ac\) must equal \(0\). Put the equation into \(y=ax^2+bx+c\) form in order to determine the values of \(a\), \(b\), and \(c\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ \cancel{2}-\cancel{2}&=x^2+6x+m-2\\ 0&=x^2+6x+m-2 \end{aligned}$$
div class='explanation-step'>In this equation, \(a=1\), \(b=6\) and \(c=(m-2)\). Use the discriminant to solve for \(m\):
$$\begin{aligned}\ b^2-4ac&=0\\ 6^2-4(1)(m-2)&=0\\ 36-4m+8&=0\\ 44-\cancel{4m}+\cancel{4m}&=0+4m\\ \frac{44}{4}&=\frac{\cancel{4}m}{\cancel{4}}\\ m&=11\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: Which of the following is the diameter of the circle in the \(xy\)-plane with equation \((x+2)^2+(y+9)^2=36\)?
questions.explanation: The equation of a circle is \((x-h)^2+(y-k)^2=r^2\), where \((h,k)\) is the center of the circle and \(r\) is the radius. Since you are only concerned with the diameter, which is \(2 imes r\), you only need to find the radius. In the equation \((x+2)^2+(y+9)^2=36\), \(r^2=36\), so \(r=6\) and \(d=2(6)=12\).

questions.stem: Which expression is equivalent to a \(n^{\frac{15}{8}}\), where \(n>0\)?
questions.explanation:
To convert between exponential and radical form, use \(\sqrt[b]{m^a}=m^{\frac{a}{b}}\). Since the answer choices don't all have the numbers \(15\) and \(8\), which appear in the original expression, it can be quicker to convert the answers to exponential form and compare the fractional exponents.
A) \(\sqrt[8]{n^{120}}=n^{\frac{120}{8}}\); \(\frac{120}{8} eq \frac{15}{8}\)
B) \(\sqrt[64]{n^{120}} =n^{\frac{120}{64}}\); \(\frac{120}{64} = \frac{15}{8}\)

questions.stem:
$$\frac{72}{n-14}=n$$
What is the positive solution to the given equation?
questions.explanation:
To begin solving this equation, multiply both sides by \((n-14)\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ \cancel{(n-14)} imes \frac{72}{\cancel{n-14}}&=n imes (n-14)\\ 72&=n(n-14)\\ 72&=n^2-14n\end{aligned}$$
Solve this quadratic equation by setting it equal to zero and factoring. To factor, determine factors of \(-72\) that have a sum of \(-14\). The factors are \(-18\) and \(4\) because \(-18 imes 4=-72\) and \(-18+4=-14\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ \cancel{72}-\cancel{72}&=n^2-14n-72\\ 0&=n^2-14n-72\\ 0&=(n-18)(n+4)\end{aligned}$$
Use the Zero Product Property to solve: \(n-18=0\) and \(n+4=0\), so \(n=18\) or \(n=-4\).

questions.stem: What are the solutions to \(4x^2-48x+36=0\)?
questions.explanation:
The answer choices are a clue that this problem is best solved using the quadratic formula (or completing the square). To make the problem slightly easier to work with, first divide the entire equation by \(4\), so \(x^2-12x+9=0\). This equation in the form \(y=ax^2+bx+c\), \(a=1\), \(b=-12\), and \(c=9\). Substitute these values into the quadratic formula and evaluate.
$$\begin{aligned}\ x&=\frac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2-4ac}}{2a}\\ x&=\frac{-(-12) \pm \sqrt{(-12)^2-4(1)(9)}}{2(1)}\\ x&=\frac{12 \pm \sqrt{144-36)}}{2}\\ x&=\frac{12 \pm \sqrt{108)}}{2} \end{aligned}$$
Simplify \(\sqrt{108}\). The largest perfect square factor of \(108\) is \(36\), so \(\sqrt{108}=\sqrt{36 imes 2}\), which equals \(6 \sqrt{2}\). Simplify further:
$$\begin{aligned}\ x&=\frac{12 \pm 6\sqrt{2}}{2}\\ x&=6 \pm 3\sqrt{2}\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: Of the following four bird population growth models, which demonstrates exponential growth of the population?
choices: Each successive year, the bird population grows by \(3\%\) of the initial bird population.
Each successive year, the bird population grows by \(5\%\) of the initial bird population and \(20\) new birds are added to the bird population.
Each successive year, the bird population grows by \(4\%\) of the previous year's bird population.
Each successive year, the bird population grows by \(50\) birds
questions.explanation:
Use process of elimination to determine which answer is exponential.
A) Each successive year, the bird population grows by \(3\%\) of the initial bird population.
This answer choice demonstrates linear growth because \(3\%\) of the initial bird population is a constant, and adding a constant is linear growth.
B) Each successive year, the bird population grows by \(5\%\) of the initial bird population and \(20\) new birds are added to the bird population.
This answer choice demonstrates linear growth because \(5\%\) of the initial bird population is a constant, and adding that number (and adding \(20\)) each year is linear growth.
C) Each successive year, the bird population grows by \(4\%\) of the previous year's bird population.
This answer choice demonstrates exponential growth; if the population grows by \(4\%\) of the previous year, each year will be \(1.04\) or \(104\%\) of the previous year. Growing by a constant multiplier demonstrates exponential growth.

questions.stem: A large-scale bee farm started the day with a population of \(\text{9,000,000}\) bees. There is a bee disease at this farm and the number of bees halves every \(6\) hours. How many bees will there be \(24\) hours after the start of the day?
questions.explanation:
Method \(1\): Write an equation that models the situation. In the exponential function \(y=a imes b^{x}\) \(a\) is the starting population, \(b\) is the growth or decay multiplier, \(x\) is the number of halving periods, and \(y\) is the population of bees. The equation is \(y=\text{9,000,000} imes \left(\frac{1}{2} ight)^x\). \(b=\frac{1}{2}\) because the population halves each period of time. This occurs every \(6\) hours, so after \(24\) hours, there are \(\frac{24}{6}=4\) halving periods. Substitute \(x=4\) into the equation to find the bee population.
$$\begin{aligned}\ y&=\text{9,000,000} imes \left(\frac{1}{2} ight)^4\\ y&=\text{9,000,000} imes \left(\frac{1}{16} ight)\\ y&=\text{562,500}\end{aligned}$$
Method \(2\): Since the halving occurs every \(6\) hours and you are asked to find the population after \(24\) hours, use \(\frac{24}{6}=4\) as the number of halving periods. Cut the population in half (divide by \(2\)) \(4\) times.
$$\begin{aligned}\frac{\text{9,000,000}}{2}=\text{4,500,000}\\ rac{\text{4,500,000}}{2}=\text{2,250,000}\end{aligned}$$
$$\begin{aligned}\frac{\text{2,250,000}}{2}=\text{1,125,000}\\ rac{\text{1,125,000}}{2}=\text{562,500}\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem:
$$2|n-10|-1=49$$
What is one possible solution to the given equation?
questions.explanation:
To begin solving this absolute value equation, isolate the absolute value:
$$\begin{aligned}\ 2|n-10|-\cancel{1}+\cancel{1}&=49+1\\ \frac{\cancel{2}|n-10|}{\cancel{2}}&=\frac{50}{2}\\\ |n-10|&=25\end{aligned}$$
Write and solve one of two equations:
$$\begin{aligned}n -\cancel{10}+\cancel{10}&=25+10\\n &=35\end{aligned}$$
$$\begin{aligned}n -\cancel{10}+\cancel{10}&=-15+10\\n &=-5\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: The number of people visiting an amusement park triples every year. In the year the park opens, \(\text{15,000}\) people visit the park. Which of the following represents the number of people who visit the park, \(y\), \(t\) years after the park first opens?
questions.explanation: In the exponential function \(y=a imes b^x\), \(a\) is the starting value and \(b\) is the growth or decay factor. In this situation, the number of people who visit the park is \(\text{15,000}\), so \(a=\text{15,000}\). Eliminate A and B. The number of visitors triples, so grows by a factor of \(3\). This means \(b=3\). The equation that models the situation is \(y=\text{15,000}(3)^t\).

questions.stem: The function \(h\) is defined by \(h(x)=x^2-36\). Which is the value of \(x\) when \(h(x)=64\)?
questions.explanation:
To find the value of \(x\) when \(h(x)=64\), substitute \(64\) into the equation for \(h(x)\) and solve for \(x\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ 64&=x^2-36\\ 64+36&=x^2-\cancel{36}+\cancel{36}\\ \sqrt{100}&=\sqrt{x^2}\\ x&=\pm 10\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem:
$$f(x)=20(\frac{1}{3})^x$$
If the given function \(f\) is graphed in the \(xy\)-plane, where \(y=f(x)\), what is the \(y\)-value of the \(y\)-intercept of the graph?
questions.explanation:
The \(y\)-intercept is the value of \(y\) when \(x=0\). Substitute \(0\) in for \(x\) and evaluate the function.
$$\begin{aligned}\ f(0)&=20 \left(\frac{1}{3} ight)^0\\ f(0)&=20(1)\\ f(0)&=20\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: The function \(g\) is defined by \(g(x)=-4x^4\). In the \(xy\)-plane, the graph of \(y=h(x)\) is the result of shifting the graph of \(y=g(x)\) up \(5\) units. Which equation defines function \(h\)?
questions.explanation: In a polynomial function \(ax^n\) a vertical shift (up or down) appears in the function as \(ax^n+c\) where \(c\) is a constant. Eliminate C and D. Since the function \(h\) is the function \(g\) shifted up, the constant must be positive, so \(h(x)=-4x^4+5\).

questions.stem:
$$4|1-2n|-10|1-2n|=-42$$
What is the largest solution to the given equation?
questions.explanation:
In the absolute value equation \(4|1-2n|-10|1-2n|=-42\), the two terms on the left can be combined as like terms, both containing \(|1-2n|\). The simplified equation is \(-6|1-2n|=-42\). Solve the equation by isolating the absolute value, so dividing both sides by \(-6\). Then divide the equation \(|1-2n|=7\) into two equations and solve.
$$\begin{aligned}1 -2n&=7\\ \cancel{1} -{2n}-\cancel{1}&=7-1\\ \frac{\cancel{-2}n}{\cancel{-2}}&=\frac{6}{2}\\ n &=-3\end{aligned}$$
$$\begin{aligned}1 -2n&=-7\\ \cancel{1} -{2n}-\cancel{1}&=-7-1\\ \frac{\cancel{-2}n}{\cancel{-2}}&=\frac{-8}{-2}\\ n &=4\end{aligned}$$
Since \(4>-3\), \(4\) is the correct answer.

questions.stem: A particular investment fund is growing over time. The fund started with \(\$\text{200,000}\). The fund doubles every \(4\) years. How much will be in the fund \(20\) years after the fund was first started?
questions.explanation:
Method \(1\): Start by writing an equation that models the situation. In the exponential function \(y=a imes b^{x}\) \(a\) is the initial investment, \(b\) is the growth or decay multiplier, \(x\) is the number of doubling periods, and \(y\) is the value of the investment. The equation is \(y=\text{200,000} imes (2)^x\). \(b=2\) because the fund doubles each given period of time. Since the doubling occurs every \(4\) years and you are asked to find the value of the investment after \(20\) years, use \(\frac{20}{4}=5\) as the \(x\)-value. Substitute \(x=5\) into the equation and evaluate to find the value of the investment.
$$\begin{aligned}\ y&=\text{200,000} imes (2)^5\\ y&=\text{200,000} imes 32\\ y&=\text{6,400,000}\end{aligned}$$
Method \(2\): Since the doubling occurs every \(4\) years and you are asked to find the value of the fund after \(20\) years, use \(\frac{20}{4}=5\) as the number of doubling periods. Double the investment \(5\) times.
$$\begin{aligned}\ \text{200,000} imes2=\text{400,000}\\ \text{400,000} imes2=\text{800,000}\\ \text{800,000} imes2=\text{1,600,000}\\ \text{1,600,000} imes2=\text{3,200,000}\\ \text{3,200,000} imes2=\text{6,400,000}\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem:
$$h(x)=-2x^2+70x-46$$
The given equation defines the function \(h\). What is the value of \(x\) for which \(h(x)\) reaches its maximum?
questions.explanation:
The maximum value of \(x\) occurs at the vertex of the parabola. To find the vertex, use \(x=-\frac{b}{2a}\) where \(y=ax^2+bx+c\). In the function \(h(x)=-2x^2+70x-46\), \(a=-2\) and \(b=70\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ x&= -\frac{b}{2a}\\ x&= -\frac{70}{2(-2)}\\ x&= -\frac{70}{-4}= \frac{35}{2}\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: Which of the following is equal to \(d^{\frac{2}{5}}\), for all values of \(a\)?
questions.explanation: To convert between exponential and radical form, use \(a^{\frac{m}{n}}=\sqrt[n]{a^m}\), so \(d^{\frac{2}{5}}=\sqrt[5]{d^2}\).

questions.stem:
$$9x^2-30x+25=0$$
How many distinct real solutions does the given equation have?
questions.explanation:
To find the number of solutions that a quadratic function has, use the discriminant, \(b^2-4ac\). If \(b^2-4ac<0\) the function has no real solutions, if \(b^2-4ac=0\) it has one real solution and if \(b^2-4ac>0\) it has two real solutions. The equation \(9x^2-30x+25=0\) is in the form \(y=ax^2+bx+c\) where \(a=9\), \(b=-30\), and \(c=25\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ &=b^2-4ac\\ &=(-30)^2-4(9)(25)\\ &=900-900\\ &=0\end{aligned}$$
Since the discriminant is \(0\), there is exactly one real solution.

questions.stem:
$$\frac{24}{x-10}=x$$
What is the positive solution to the given equation?
questions.explanation:
To begin solving this equation, multiply both sides by \((x-10)\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ \cancel{(x-10)} imes \frac{24}{\cancel{x-10}}&=x imes (x-10)\\ 24&=x^2-10x\end{aligned}$$
Set the equation equal to zero and factor. Determine factors of \(-24\) that have a sum of \(-10\). The factors are \(-12\) and \(2\) because \(-12 imes 2=-24\) and \(-12+2=-10\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ \cancel{24}-\cancel{24}&=x^2-14x-24\\ 0&=x^2-10x-24\\ 0&=(x-12)(x+2)\end{aligned}$$
Use the Zero Product Property to solve: \(x-12=0\) and \(x+2=0\), so \(x=12\) or \(x=-2\). Since the answer must be positive, \(12\) is the correct answer.

questions.stem: The function \(k\) is defined by \(k(x)=2x^3\). What is the value of \(x\) when \(k(x)\) is equal to \(54\)?
questions.explanation:
To find the value of \(x\) when \(k(x)=54\), substitute \(54\) into the equation for \(k(x)\) and solve for \(x\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ k(x)&=2x^3\\ \frac{54}{2}&=\frac{\cancel{2}x^3}{\cancel2}\\ \sqrt[3]{27}&=\sqrt[3]{x^3}\\ x&=3\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem:
$$x=\frac{72}{x-1}$$
Which of the following is a solution to the given equation?
questions.explanation:
To begin solving this equation, multiply both sides by \((x-1)\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ \cancel{(x-1)} imes \frac{72}{\cancel{x-1}}&=x imes (x-1)\\ 72&=x(x-1)\\ 72&=x^2-x\end{aligned}$$
Solve this quadratic equation by setting it equal to zero and factoring. To factor, determine factors of \(-72\) that have a sum of \(-1\). The factors are \(-9\) and \(8\) because \(-9 imes 8=-72\) and \(-9+8=-1\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ \cancel{72}-\cancel{72}&=x^2-1x-72\\ 0&=x^2-x-72\\ 0&=(x-9)(x+8)\end{aligned}$$
Use the Zero Product Property to solve: \(x-9=0\) and \(x+8=0\), so \(x=9\) or \(x=-8\), which means B) \(9\) is the correct answer.

questions.stem:
$$h(x)=3x^2-25$$
What is the minimum value of the given function?
questions.explanation:
The minimum value of the function occurs at the vertex of the parabola. The minimum value refers to the \(y\)-value at the vertex. To find the vertex, use \(x=-\frac{b}{2a}\) where \(y=ax^2+bx+c\). In the function \(h(x)=3x^2-25\), \(a=3\) and \(b=0\). Since \(b=0\), the \(x\)-value of the vertex will also be \(0\) (\(x=\frac{0}{-6}=0\)).
Substitute \(x=0\) it into the original function to find the minimum value of \(y\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ h(0)&=3(0)^2-25\\ h(0)&=0-25\\ h(0)&=-25\end{aligned}$$
Alternatively, if you recognize that this function is the parent function \(y=x^2\) shifted down \(25\), you can determine that the vertex is \((0,-25)\) and the minimum value is \(-25\).

questions.stem:
$$y-4=12$$
$$(y-4)^2=x$$
Which ordered pair \((x,y)\) is a solution to the given system of equations?
questions.explanation: This is a non-linear system of equations where the second equation is the square of the first equation. To solve, square both sides of the first equation to get \(12^2=x\) on the right hand side, so \(x=144\).

questions.stem: For the function \(t\), each increase in the value of \(x\) by \(1\) results in an increase in the value of \(t(x)\) by \(37\%\). If \(t(0)=27\), which equation defines \(t\)?
questions.explanation: Since \(t(0)=27\), \(27\) is the starting value, or \(a\) in the exponential function \(y=a imes b^x\). Eliminate A and B. In the given function, as \(x\) increases by \(1\), the value of \(t(x)\) also increases (by \(37\%\)). This is exponential growth, so in the function \(y=a imes b^x\), \(b>1\). This means B is the correct answer. This can also be seen by writing an increase by \(37\%\) as \(b=1+0.37=1.37\).

questions.stem:
$$p(m)=\text{12,800}(0.75)^m$$
The given function \(p\) models the number of people who visited a ski resort each month, where \(m\) represents the number of months since the end of January, and \(0\leq{m}\leq4\). If \(y=p(m)\) is graphed in the \(xy\)-plane, which of the following it the best interpretation of the \(y\)-intercept of the graph in this context?
choices: The minimum estimated number of people who visited the ski resort during the \(4\) months was \(\text{5,400}.\)
The minimum estimated people who visited the ski resort during the \(4\) months was \(\text{12,800}\).
The estimated number of people who visited the ski resort in January was \(\text{5,400}\).
The estimated number of people who visited the ski resort in January was \(\text{12,800}\).
questions.explanation:
The \(y\)-intercept is the value of \(y\) when \(x=0\). This is also the starting value \(a\) in the function \(y=a imes b^x\). The starting value is \(12,800\); eliminate A and C. January is the first month of this model, so D is the correct answer. This can also be seen by substituting \(m=0\) months into the equation and solving for \(y\) (the \(y\)-intercept).
$$\begin{aligned}\ p(0)&=12,800(0.75)^0\\ &=12,800(1)=12,800\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: Which expression is equivalent to \(2x^2+9x-18\)?
questions.explanation:
Method \(1\): Factor by grouping. To factor by grouping, multiply \(a imes c\). In the expression \(2x^2+9x-18\), \(a=2\) and \(c=-18\), so \(a imes c = -36\). Determine factors of \(-36\) that have a sum of \(9\). The factors are \(12\) and \(-3\) because \(-3 imes 12= -36\) and \(-3 +12=9\). Re-write the given quadratic expression with these factors.
$$\begin{aligned}\ &=2x^2+9x-18\\ &=2x^2+12x-3x-18\end{aligned}$$
Factor the greatest common factor from \(2x^2+12x\) and \(-3x-18\):
$$\begin{aligned}\ &=2x(x+6)-3(x+6)\end{aligned}$$
Re-write the expression as the product of two binomials to match the format of the answer choices. \(2x(x+6)-3(x+6)=(2x-3)(x+6)\).
Method \(2\): Multiply the answer choices to find \(2x^2+9x-18\).
A) \((2x-9)(x+2)=2x^2+4x-9x-18=2x^2-5x-18\) Incorrect
B) \((2x+9)(x-2)=2x^2-4x+9x-18=2x^2+5x-18\) Incorrect
C) \((2x-6)(x+3)=2x^2+6x-6x-18=2x^2-18\) Incorrect
D) \((2x-3)(x+6)=2x^2+12x-3x-18=2x^2+9x-18\) Correct

questions.stem: The equation \(f(x)=2x^2-6x\) defines the function \(f\). What is the value of \(f(3)\)?
questions.explanation:
Substitute \(3\) in for \(x\) in the function \(f(x)=2x^2-6x\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ f(3)&=2(3)^2-6(3)\\ f(3)&=2(9)-18\\ f(3)&=18-18=0\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: Which expression is equivalent to \(\frac{c^6 d^2f }{c^2 d^2 f^3}\), where \(c\), \(d\), and \(f\) are positive?
questions.explanation:
Use the laws of exponents to simplify this expression. But first, cross out \(\frac{d^2}{d^2}\) since anything divided by itself is \(1\). This means there is no power of \(d\) in the answer, so eliminate C and D. Next, use the Quotient Rule; subtract the exponents of powers with the same bases.
$$\begin{aligned}\ &=\frac{c^6 f }{c^2 f^3}\\ &=c^{6-2} f^{1-3} \\ &=c^{4} f^{-2}\end{aligned}$$
Since the only remaining answer with \(c^4\) is B, it must be the correct answer. But to finish simplifying, use the Negative Exponent Rule, so \(f^{-2}=\frac{1}{f^2}\). Then \(c^4 imes \frac{1}{f^2} = \frac{c^4 }{f^2}\).

questions.stem: <div class='stem-text'>Which expression is equivalent to \(\frac{3}{(5+2k)}-\frac{1}{(k+2)}\)?
questions.explanation:
Find the least common denominator, which is the product of the two denominators, so \((5+2k)(k+2)\). Multiply the first fraction by \(\frac{k+2}{k+2}\) and the second fraction by \(\frac{5+2k}{5+2k}\). Then combine the numerators.
$$\begin{aligned}\ &=\frac{k+2}{k+2} imes \frac{3}{5+2k}-\frac{1}{k+2} imes \frac{5+2k}{5+2k}\\ &=\frac{3(k+2)}{(k+2)(5+2k)} - \frac{(5+2k)}{(k+2)(5+2k)}\\ &=\frac{3k+6-5-2k}{(k+2)(5+2k)}\\ &=\frac{k+1}{(k+2)(5+2k)}\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem:
$$6x^2-108kx +6y^2+60ky =-252k^2$$
The graph of the given equation, in the \(xy\)-plane is a circle. The radius has a length of \(bk\), where \(b\) and \(k\) are positive constants. What is the value of \(b\)?
questions.explanation:
Complete the square to put the equation into \((x-h)^2 + (y-k)^2=r^2\) where \(r\) is the radius. From this equation, you can then find the radius, which is also equal to \(bk\), and then solve for \(b\). To begin completing the square, divide the entire equation by \(6\), so \(x^2-18kx +y^2+10ky =-42k^2\).
Completing the square:
For \(x^2-18kx\) add \((\frac{-18k}{2})^2=81k^2\) to both sides.
For \(y^2+10ky\) add \((\frac{10k}{2})^2=25k^2\) to both sides.
Add these values to both sides of the equation:
$$\begin{aligned}\ x^2-18kx+81k^2+y^2+10ky+25k^2&=-42k^2+81k^2+25k^2\end{aligned}$$
Factoring the left is not important because you are only concerned with the right hand side of the equation, which contains the radius. Simplify the right:
$$\begin{aligned}\ -42k^2+81k^2+25k^2=64k^2\end{aligned}$$
Since \(r^2=64k^2\), \(r=8k\). The radius is also given as \(r=bk\), so \(8\cancel{k}=b\cancel{k}\) and \(b=8\).

questions.stem: If \(|\frac{1}{2} n+7|=91\), what is the positive value of \(2n+28\)?
questions.explanation:
Since you only need to solve for the positive value of \(2n+28\), drop the absolute value sign and multiply the entire equation by \(4\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ \frac{1}{2} n+7&=91\\ 4 imes \left(\frac{1}{2} n+7 ight)&=91 imes 4\\ 2n+28&=364\end{aligned}$$
Note: solving for the positive value doesn't always mean you set the equation equal to the positive value (\(91\) instead of \(-91\). For example, in the equation \(|\frac{-1}{2}n+7|=91\), you would need to solve the equation \(\frac{-1}{2}n+7=-91\) in order to find the positive value of \(2n+28\).

questions.stem:
$$g(x)=3x^2+42x-98$$
The function \(h\) is defined by \(h(x)=g(x-8)\). For what value of \(x\) does \(h(x)\) reach its minimum?
questions.explanation:
\(g(x-8)\) is the original function \(g(x)\) shifted to the right \(8\), so the \(x\)-values increase by \(8\). The minimum value of \(x\) occurs at the vertex of the parabola. To find the vertex, use \(x=-\frac{b}{2a}\) where \(y=ax^2+bx+c\). In the function \(g(x)=3x^2+42x-98\), \(a=3\) and \(b=42\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ x&= -\frac{42}{2(3)}\\ x&= -\frac{42}{6)}= -7 \end{aligned}$$
Add \(8\) to this value to shift to the right by \(8\); \(-7+8=1\).

questions.stem:
$$p=-2.5t^2+nt-c$$
The equation above gives the profit \(p\), in dollars, from the sales of number of toys, \(t\), sold and initial cost of \(c\) dollars, and an initial revenue of \(n\) dollars per toy sold. Which of the following gives \(n\) in terms of \(p\), \(t\), and \(c\)?
questions.explanation:
Get \(n\) alone by adding and subtracting the other terms and then dividing by \(t\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ p+c&=-2.5t^2+nt-\cancel{c}+\cancel{c}\\ p+c+2.5t^2&=-\cancel{2.5t^2}+nt+\cancel{2.5t^2}\\ \frac{p+c+2.5t^2}{t}&=\frac{n\cancel{t}}{\cancel{t}}\end{aligned}$$
\(n=\frac{p+c+2.5t^2}{t}\) doesn't appear in any of the answers. This means the expression needs to be further simplified. Separate the expression into two fractions to simplify \(\frac{t^2}{t}\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ n&=\frac{p+c}{t}+\frac{2.5t^2}{t}\\ n&=\frac{p+c}{t}+{2.5t}\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: A rectangle has a width that is six less than its length. If the rectangle has an area of \(91\) square units, what is the width of the rectangle?
questions.explanation:
Method \(1\): let \(w=\) width and \(l=\) length. The width is \(6\) less than the length, so \(w=l-6\). Rearrange this equation to \(l=w+6\) so that the variable you solve for is \(w\). The area of a rectangle is \(A=lw\). Substitute given values,so \(91=w(w+6)\). Simplify the right and set the equation equal to zero.
$$\begin{aligned}\ \cancel{91}-\cancel{91}&=w^2+6w-91\\ 0&=w^2+6w-91\end{aligned}$$
Factor the equation by determining factors of \(-91\) that have a sum of \(6\). These factors are \(13\) and \(-7\) because \(13 imes (-7)=-91\) and \(13 + (-7)=6\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ 0&=w^2+6w-91\\ 0&=(w+13)(w-7)\end{aligned}$$
Use the Zero Product Property to solve for \(w\). \(0=w+13\), and \(0=w-7\), so \(w=-13\) and \(w=7\). Since this is a dimension of a rectangle, only positive values are possible. This means the correct answer is \(w=7\).
Method \(2\): test answers. Since the answer choices give possible values for the width, add \(6\) to find their corresponding lengths. Then multiply to find the area of the rectangle. The correct answer will have a product of \(91\).
WidthLengthArea
\(6\)\(12\)\(72\)
\(7\)\(13\)\(91\)

questions.stem: For the exponential function \(g\), the value of \(g(1)\) is the constant \(c\). Which of the following equivalent forms of the function \(g\) shows the value of \(c\) as either the coefficient or the base?
questions.explanation:
Substitute \(g(1)\) and the correct \(y\)-value (the constant \(c\)) must be \(a\) or \(b\) in the exponential form \(y=a imes b^x\). This is what it means to appear as a coefficient (\(a\)) or base (\(b\)).
$$\begin{aligned}\ g(1)&=200(1.6)^{1-1}\\ g(1)&=200(1.6)^0\\ g(1)&=200(1)=200\end{aligned}$$
\(g(1)=200\), so the constant \(c\) appears as a coefficient. To see what an incorrect answer looks like, below is the work for B.
$$\begin{aligned}\ g(1)&=140(1.6)^1\\ g(1)&=140(1.6)=224\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: The equation \(\frac{-36x^2+47x+27}{kx+3}=9x-5+\frac{42}{kx+3}\) is true for all values of \(x\neq\frac{-3}{k}\), where \(k\) is a constant. What is the value of \(k\)?
questions.explanation:
The equation shows a polynomial long division problem with an unknown coefficient. Since \(9x\) comes from dividing \(-36x^2\) by \(kx\), or \(9\) comes from dividing \(-36\) by \(k\), write this equation and solve \(k\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ \frac{-36}{k}&=9\\ \frac{-36}{9}&=k\\ k&=-4\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: In the \(xy\)-plane, the parabola with equation \(y=(x+4)^2\) intersects the line with equation \(y=36\) at two points, \(C\) and \(D\). What is the length of \(CD\)?
questions.explanation:
This is a non-linear system of equations. To solve, substitute \(36\) into the equation \(y=(x+4)^2\) and solve by taking the square root of both sides.
$$\begin{aligned}\ y&=(x+4)^2\\ 36&=(x+4)^2\\ \pm \sqrt{36}&=\sqrt{(x+4)^2}\\ \pm 6 - 4&= x+\cancel{4}-\cancel{4}\\ x&=\pm 6 - 4\end{aligned}$$
Split this into two equations, so \(x=6-4=2\) and \(x=-6-4=-10\). The length of CD will be the distance between these two \(x\)-values, and distance is found by subtracting, so \(2 - (-10)=2+10=12\).

questions.stem: In working with a one-time donation to a university, a fund manager estimates that, starting from today, the university will spend \(22\) percent of this donation every \(8\) years. If the donation is in the amount of \(\text{900,000}\), which of the following expressions represents the fund manager’s estimate of amount of the donation remaining \(t\) years from today?
questions.explanation: In this problem the bases and the exponents differentiate the answers. Begin with the base; in the exponential function \(y=a imes b^{\frac{t}{r}}\), \(b\) is the growth or decay rate. In an exponential growth problem, \(b\) is found by \(b=100\% + n\%\), and in an exponential decay problem, it is found by \(b=100\% - n\%\). Since the fund in this problem decreases by \(22\%\) each year, \(b=\) \(100\%\) (donation) \(-\) \(22\%\)(donation), or \(b=78\%\) of the donation. Eliminate C and D. The exponent is found by dividing time by the period of time for one increase or decrease to occur, so B is the correct answer. This can be seen by substituting \(8\) in for \(t\) to get \( ext{900,000}(0.78)^{\frac{8}{8}}= ext{900,000}(0.78)^1\), so after \(8\) years, one decrease of \(22\%\) occurs.

questions.stem:
$$y+558=2x^2$$
$$y-14=-18x$$
The graphs of the equations in the \(xy\)-plane of the given system of equations intersect at the point \((x,y)\). What is one possible value of \(x\)?
questions.explanation:
This is a non-linear system of equations. Since the question asks for a value of \(x\), use elimination to eliminate the \(y\) variable. This can be accomplished by subtracting the equations.
$$\begin{array}{rcl}&y + 558 & = 2x^2\\- &y - 14 & = -18x\\\hline &572 & = 2x^2 +18x\end{array}$$
This new equation is a quadratic that can be solved by factoring. Divide the equation by \(2\) to make it slightly easier to factor, so \(286 = x^2 + 9x\). Subtract \(286\) from both sides, so \(0 = x^2 + 9x - 286\). To factor, determine factors of \(-286\) that have a sum of \(9\). It can be challenging to find factors of \(-286\), so use the answers to help you find the factors. The factors are \(22\) and \(-13\) because \(22 imes (-13) = -286\) and \(22 + (-13) = 9\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ 0 &= x^2 + 9x - 286 \\ 0 &= (x + 22)(x - 13)\end{aligned}$$
Use the Zero Product Property to solve for \(x\), so \(0 = x + 22\) and \(0 = x - 13\). The two solutions are \(x = -22\) and \(x = 13\).

questions.stem: In the \(xy\)-plane, a parabola has the equation \(y=3x^2-5x\) and intersects a line at exactly one point. If the line has equation \(6y=k\) for constant \(k\), what is the value of \(k\)?
questions.explanation:
This is a non-linear system of equations. To begin, solve for \(y\) in the equation \(6y = k\), so \(y = \frac{k}{6}\). Substitute \(\frac{k}{6}\) into the equation \(y = 3x^2 - 5x\), so \(\frac{k}{6} = 3x^2 - 5x\).
When a problem states that there are a specific number of solutions or that the graphs intersect a specific number of times, this is a clue that you need to use the discriminant \(b^2 - 4ac\) where \(y = ax^2 + bx + c\).
Since this problem states that the graphs intersect at exactly one point, use \(b^2 - 4ac = 0\). Set the equation equal to \(0\) to determine the values of \(a\), \(b\), and \(c\), so \(0 = 3x^2 - 5x - \frac{k}{6}\). From the equation, the values are \(a = 3\), \(b = -5\), and \(c = -\frac{k}{6}\). Use the discriminant equation to solve for \(k\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ b^2-4ac&=0\\ (-5)^2-4(3)(-\frac{k}{6})&=0\\ 25+\frac{12k}{6}&=0\\ \cancel{25}+2k-\cancel{25}&=0-25\\ \frac{\cancel{2}k}{\cancel{2}}&=\frac{-25}{2}\\ \end{aligned}$$
\(k=-\frac{25}{2}\) or \(-12.5\).

questions.stem:
$$g(x)=(x-2)(x-7)(x+1)$$
The function \(g\) is given. Which table of values represents \(y=g(x)+5\)?
questions.explanation:
The function \(g(x)+5\) is \(g(x)\) shifted up \(5\), so all y-values of all points on the graph increase by \(5\). The \(x\)-intercepts from the factored equation \(g(x)=(x-2)(x-7)(x+1)\) are \((2,0)\), \((7,0)\), and \((-1,0)\). Add \(5\) to all of the \(y\)-values:
\(x\)\(g(x)\)\(g(x)+5\)
\(2\)\(0\)\(5\)
\(7\)\(0\)\(5\)
\(-1\)\(0\)\(5\)

questions.stem:
$$ax^2-24x+2=0$$
In the given equation, \(a\) is a constant. If \(a>q\), the equation has no real solutions. What is the minimum possible value of \(q\)?
questions.explanation:
When a problem states that there are a specific number of solutions or that the graphs intersect a specific number of times, this is a clue that you need to use the discriminant \(b^2-4ac\) where \(y=ax^2+bx+c\) to solve. Since this problem states that the equation has no real solutions, use \(b^2-4ac<0\). From the equation, the values for the discriminant are \(a=a\), \(b=-24\) and \(c=2\). Use the discriminant equation to solve for \(a\), which you can then use to determine the minimum value of \(q\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ b^2-4ac&<0\\ (-24)^2-4(a)(2)&<0\\ 576-8a&<0\\ 576-\cancel{8a}+\cancel{8a}&<8a\\ \frac{576}{8}&<\frac{\cancel{8}a}{\cancel{8}}\\ 72&72\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: In the exponential function \(h(x)=118b^x\), \(b\) is a positive constant. If \(h(4)=\text{1,888}\), what is the value of \(h(5)\)?
questions.explanation:
To find the value of \(h(5)\), first solve for \(b\) by substituting \((4, ext{1,888})\) into the equation \(h(x)=118b^x\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ ext{1,888}&=118b^4\\ \frac{ ext{1,888}}{118}&=\frac{\cancel{118}b^4}{\cancel{118}}\\ \sqrt[4]{16}&=\sqrt[4]{b^4}\\ b&=2\end{aligned}$$
Now replace \(b\) with \(2\) and substitute \(5\) in for \(x\) to evaluate the function.
$$\begin{aligned}\ h(x)&=118(2)^x\\ h(5)&=118(2)^5\\ h(5)&=118(32)\\ h(5)&= ext{3,776}\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: Which of the following could be the equation of the graph shown in the \(xy\)-plane?
questions.explanation: Since all of the answer choices have the same \(a\)-value (\(\frac{1}{20}\)), focus entirely on the \(x\)-intercepts. From the graph the \(x\)-intercepts are \((-5,0)\) \((3,0)\), and \((6,0)\). Write an equation using these intercepts; \(y=\frac{1}{20}(x+5)(x-3)(x-6)\). Eliminate C and D. Examine the multiplicity of the intercepts, or the exponent on the factors. A: \((x-6)^2\), and B: \((x-3)^2\). A multiplicity of \(2\) shows on the graph as touching or ''bouncing'' off of the \(x\)-axis, and this can be seen at \((3,0)\), so B is the correct answer.

questions.stem:
$$h(x)=2x^2+12x+9$$
The given function, \(h(x)\) defines \(x\) and \(y=h(x)\). What is the value of \(y\) at the minimum?
questions.explanation:
The minimum value of the function occurs at the vertex of the parabola. Since the question asks for the \(y\)-value at the vertex, use \(x=-\frac{b}{2a}\) where \(y=ax^2+bx+c\), and substitute that answer into the original function to find \(y\). In the function \(h(x)=2x^2+12x+9\), \(a=2\) and \(b=12\).
$$\begin{aligned}x&=-\frac{12}{2(2)}\\x&=-\frac{12}{4}\\x&=-3\end{aligned}$$
$$\begin{aligned}h(-3)&=2(-3)^2+12(-3)+9\\h(-3)&=2(9)-36+9\\h(-3)&=18-36+9\\ h(-3)&=-9\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem:
For \(x>0\), the function \(g\) is defined as follows:
$$g(x)\, \text{is equal to}\, 52\% \text{of}\, x$$
Which of the following could describe this function?
questions.explanation: The relationship sounds exponential (\(52\%\)), but \(52\%\) of a number means \(\frac{52}{100}\) times the number, and this is constant change (think of \(\frac{52}{100}\) as the slope). The function is \(g(x)=\frac{52}{100}x\), which is increasing (positive slope) and linear.

questions.stem: Function \(h\) is defined by \(h(x)=g(x+3)\) where function \(g\) is defined by \(g(x)=(x+2)(x-2)(x-8)\). The graph of \(y=h(x)\) in the \(xy\)-plane has \(x\)-intercepts at \((q,0)\), \((r,0)\), and \((s,0)\), where \(q\), \(r\), and \(s\) are distinct constants. What is the value of \(q+r+s\)?
questions.explanation:
Method \(1\): \(h(x)=g(x+3)\) is the function \(h(x)\) shifted left \(3\), which means all \(x\)-values decrease by \(3\). The function \(g(x)=(x+2)(x-2)(x-8)\) has \(x\)-intercepts at \((-2,0)\), \((2,0)\), and \((8,0)\). Shift these \(x\)-coordinates left \(3\), or subtract \(3\) from each to get \((-5,0)\), \((-1,0)\), and \((5,0)\). Then add the three values: \(q+r+s=-5+(-1)+5=-1\).
Method \(2\): Substitute \(x+3\) into the \(g(x)\) function and simplify to find the new \(x\)-intercepts.
$$\begin{aligned}\ g(x)&=(x+2)(x-2)(x-8)\\ g(x+3)&=(x+3+2)(x+3-2)(x+3-8)\\ g(x+3)&=(x+5)(x+1)(x-5)\end{aligned}$$
The \(x\)intercepts of \(g(x+3)\) are \((-5,0)\), \((-1,0)\), and \((5,0)\), so the sum of \(q+r+s=-5+(-1)+5=-1\).

questions.stem: In the \(xy\)-plane, the line \(y=n\) intersects the graph of the equation \(y=x^2-7x-200\) at exactly one point. What is the value of \(n\)?
questions.explanation:
This is a non-linear system of equations. To begin, substitute \(n\) into the equation \(y=x^2-7x-200\), so \(n=x^2-7x-200\). When a problem states that there are a specific number of solutions or that the graphs intersect a specific number of times, this is a clue that you need to use the discriminant \(b^2-4ac\) where \(y=ax^2+bx+c\). Since this problem states that the graphs intersect at exactly one point, use \(b^2-4ac=0\). Set the equation equal to \(0\) to determine the values of \(a\), \(b\), and \(c\), so \(0=x^2-7x-200-n\). From the equation, the values are \(a=1\) and \(b=-7\). \(n\) is a constant (a number), so \(c=-200-n\). Use the discriminant equation to solve for \(n\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ b^2-4ac&=0\\ (-7)^2-4(1)(-200-n)&=0\\ 49+800+4n&=0\\ \cancel{849}+4n-\cancel{849}&=0-849\\ \frac{\cancel{4}n}{\cancel{4}}&=\frac{-849}{4}\\ n&=-\frac{849}{4}\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: The function \(g(x)=-\frac{1}{3}(x-5)^2 +9\) gives the height of a projectile above the ground \(g(x)\), in feet, \(x\) seconds after it is launched. Which of the following is the best interpretation of the vertex of the graph of \(y =g(x)\) in the \(xy\)-plane?
choices: The projectile's maximum height was \(5\) feet above the ground.
The projectile's maximum height was \(9\) feet above the ground.
The projectile's height was \(5\) feet above the ground when was first launched.
The projectile's height was \(9\) feet above the ground when was first launched.
questions.explanation: The vertex of a parabola is a maximum or minimum, so eliminate C and D. The given equation is in vertex form, \(y=a(x-h)^2 +k\), where \((h,k)\) is the vertex, so \((5, 9)\) is the vertex. \(x\) is time in seconds and \(y\) or \(g(x)\) is the height of the projectile, so \(9\) is the maximum height (at \(5\) seconds).

questions.stem: An equation of a circle in the xy-plane is \((x-7)^2+(y-8)^2=r^2\), where \(r\) is a positive constant. A diameter of this circle has endpoints \((-1,8)\) and \((15,8)\). What is the value of \(r\)?
questions.explanation:
The circle equation is in the form \((x-h)^2+(y-k)^2=r^2\), where \((h,k)\) is the center and \(r\) is the radius of the circle. Sketch the circle and label the points on the circle \((-1,8)\) and \((15,8)\).
These are endpoints of the diameter, so find the distance between them for the length the diameter. This distance is \(15-(-1)=16\). The diameter is \(16\), which is twice the length of the radius, so the radius is \(8\).
Alternatively, find the distance between the center \((7,8)\) and one of the endpoints \((15,8)\) to find the length of the radius (the center to any point on the circle is the radius); \(15-7=8\).

questions.stem: One of the factors of \(3x^3-66x^2+255x\) is \(x-n\), where \(n\) is a positive constant. What is the largest possible value of \(n\)?
questions.explanation:
To find the value of \(n\), fully factor the given expression. Start by factoring out the greatest common factor, \(3x\), so \(3x(x^2-22x+85)\). To factor, determine factors of \(85\) that have a sum of \(-22\). The factors are \(-5\) and \(-17\) because \(-5 imes (-17) = 85\) and \(-5 + (-17) = -22\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ &=3x(x^2-22x+85)\\ &=3x(x-5)(x-17)\end{aligned}$$
The factor \(x-n\) means \(n=17\) or \(n=5\), and the largest of those two values is \(n=17\).

questions.stem:
$$3x(2x +5)-20= 4x(x+3)$$
What is the sum of the solutions to the given equation?
questions.explanation:
To find the sum of the solutions, begin by solving the equation. Simplify each side and then set the equation equal to \(0\). This is in preparation for factoring the resulting quadratic equation.
$$\begin{aligned}\ 3x(2x +5)-20&=4x(x+3)\\ 6x^2+15x-4x^2-12x-20&=\cancel{4x^2}+\cancel{12x}-\cancel{4x^2}-\cancel{12x}\\ 2x^2+3x-20&=0\end{aligned}$$
Use factoring by grouping. To factor by grouping, multiply \(a imes c\). In the expression \(2x^2+3x-20\), \(a=2\) and \(c=-20\), so \(a imes c = -40\). Determine factors of \(-40\) that have a sum of \(3\). The factors are \(8\) and \(-5\) because \(8 imes (-5)= -40\) and \(8 +(-5)=3\). Re-write the given quadratic expression with these factors.
$$\begin{aligned}\ 2x^2+3x-20&=0\\ 2x^2+8x-5x-20&=0\end{aligned}$$
Factor the greatest common factor from \(2x^2+8x\) and \(-5x-20\):
$$\begin{aligned}\ &=2x^2+12x-3x-18\\ &=2x(x+4)-5(x+4)\end{aligned}$$
Re-write the expression as the product of two binomials: \(2x(x+4)-5(x+4)=(2x-5)(x+4)\). The equation is now \((2x-5)(x+4)=0\) and can be solved by using the Zero Product Property. \(2x-5=0\) and \(x+4=0\).
$$\begin{aligned}2x-\cancel{5}+\cancel{5}&=0+5\\ rac{\cancel{2}x}{\cancel{2}}&=\frac{5}{2}\end{aligned}$$
$$\begin{aligned}x+\cancel{4}-\cancel{4}&=0-4\\x&=-4\end{aligned}$$
Find the sum of the solutions; \(\frac{5}{2}+(-4)=-\frac{3}{2}\) or \(-1.5\).

questions.stem: The equation for a particular sequence is \(s=15(3^{n-1})\) where \(s\) represents the value of the \(nth\) term of the sequence, and \(n\) represents the term number. Which best describes the sequence given by this equation?
choices: The first term is \(15\) and each term after the first term is \(3\) times the preceding term.
The first term is \(15\) and each term after the first term is \(3\) more than the preceding term.
The first term is \(3\) and each term after the first term is \(15\) times the preceding term.
The first term is \(3\) and each term after the first term is \(15\) more than the preceding term.
questions.explanation:
The given equation is exponential, which means that the sequence grows by a common multiplier. Eliminate B and D. Since the answers begin with ``The first term...'', substitute \(n=1\) to find the first term. In the exponential function \(y=a imes b^x\), \(a\) is the starting value and \(b\) is the growth factor. In this equation, \(a=15\), so that is the first term and \(b=3\), so each term is multiplied by \(3\) to find the next term. This means A is the correct answer.
Alternatively, substitute \(n=1\) and \(n=2\) to find the first term and the growth factor.
$$\begin{aligned}s&=15(3^{n-1})\\&=15(3^{1-1})\\&=15(3^{0})\\&=15(1)\\&=15\end{aligned}$$
$$\begin{aligned}s&=15(3^{n-1})\\&=15(3^{2-1})\\&=15(3^{1})\\ &=15(3)\\ &=45\end{aligned}$$
This shows that the first term is \(15\) and each term is multiplied by \(3\) to find the next term (\(15 imes 3=45\)).

questions.stem:
$$y=4x^2-34x+88$$
$$y=6x+d$$
In the given system of equations, \(d\) is a constant. The graphs of the equations in the given system intersect at exactly one point \((x,y)\), in the \(xy\)-plane. What is the value of \(d\)?
questions.explanation:
This is a non-linear system of equations. Substitute \(6x+d\) into the equation \(y=4x^2-34x+88\), so \(6x+d=4x^2-34x+88\). When a problem states that the graphs intersect a specific number of times, this is a clue to use the discriminant \(b^2-4ac\) where \(y=ax^2+bx+c\). This problem states that the graphs intersect at exactly one point, so use \(b^2-4ac=0\). Set the equation equal to \(0\) to determine the values of \(a\), \(b\), and \(c\), so \(0=4x^2-40x+88-d\). From the equation, the values are \(a=4\) and \(b=-40\). \(d\) is a constant (a number), so \(c=88-d\). Use the discriminant equation to solve for \(d\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ b^2-4ac&=0\\ (-40)^2-4(4)(88-d)&=0\\ ext{1,600}- ext{1,408}+16d&=0\\ \cancel{192}+16d-\cancel{192}&=0-192\\ \frac{\cancel{16}d}{\cancel{16}}&=\frac{-192}{16}\\ d&=-12\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: In the graph shown above, \(y=h(x)\), where the function \(h\) is defined as \(h(x)=ax^4+bx^2+c\) and \(a\), \(b\), and \(c\) are constants. \(h(x)=0\) for how many values of \(x\)?
questions.explanation: \(h(x)=0\) occurs at the \(x\)-intercepts. Find how many times the graph intersects the \(x\)-axis; the graph intersects the \(x\)-axis \(4\) times.

questions.stem:
$$-40=-6x^2+43x$$
What is the positive solution to the given equation?
questions.explanation:
Method \(1\): factor by grouping. Set the given equation equal to \(0\), so \(0=-6x^2+43x+40\), then divide the entire equation by \(-1\), so \(0=6x^2-43x-40\). Multiply \(a imes c\). In the expression \(6x^2-43x-40\), \(a=6\) and \(c=-40\), so \(a imes c = -240\). Determine factors of \(-240\) that have a sum of \(-43\). These factors are \(-48\) and \(5\) because \(-48 imes (5)= -240\) and \(-48 + 5 =-43\). Re-write the given quadratic expression with these factors.
$$\begin{aligned}\0&=6x^2-43x-40\\ 0&=6x^2-48x+5x-40 \end{aligned}$$
Factor the greatest common factor from \(6x^2-48x\) and \(5x-40\):
$$\begin{aligned}\ 0&=6x^2-48x+5x-40\\ 0&=6x(x-8)+5(x-8)\end{aligned}$$
Re-write the expression as the product of two binomials. \(6x(x-8)+5(x-8)=(6x+5)(x-8)\). Use the Zero Product Property to solve.
$$\begin{aligned}6x+\cancel{5}-\cancel{5}&=0-5\\ rac{\cancel{6}x}{\cancel{6}}&=\frac{-5}{6}\end{aligned}$$
$$\begin{aligned}x-\cancel{8}+\cancel{8}&=0+8\\x&=8\end{aligned}$$
The correct answer is \(8\).
Method \(2\): test answers. Skip A and B because they contain fractions. Start with C, substituting \(8\) into the original equation:
$$\begin{aligned}\ -40&=-6(8)^2+43(8)\\ -40&=-6(64)+344\\ -40&=-384+344=-40\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: A gaming magazine article estimates that at the end of each year from 2004 to 2010, the number of video games released by a particular gaming company was \(130\%\) more than the number of video games released by that same company during the previous year. This model estimates that during 2005, there were \(414\) video games released by this company. Which of the following equations represents this model, where n is the estimated number of video games released \(n\) years after the end of 2004 and \(n\leq6\)?
questions.explanation:
In the exponential function \(y=a imes b^x\), \(a\) is the starting value and \(b\) is the growth factor. Since the number of video games released each year is \(130\%\) more than those released the previous year, the growth factor is \(b=\) (games released in previous year) + \(130\%\)(games released in previous year). This means \(b=\) \(230\%\)(games released in previous year) or \(b=2.3\). Eliminate A and C. In 2005 there were \(414\) games released, so the equation contains the point \((1,414)\). Substitute these values into the equation \(f(n)=a(2.3)^n\) to solve for \(a\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ 414&=a(2.3)^1\\ \frac{414}{2.3}&=\frac{\cancel{2.3}a}{\cancel{2.3}}\\ 180&=a\end{aligned}$$
The correct equation is \(f(n)=180(2.3)^n\).

questions.stem: One of the factors of \(3x^3-69x^2+378x\) is \(x-c\), where \(c\) is a positive constant. What is the smallest possible value of \(c\)?
questions.explanation:
To find the value of \(c\), fully factor the given expression. Start by factoring out the greatest common factor, \(3x\), so \(3x(x^2-23x+126)\). To factor, determine factors of \(126\) that have a sum of \(-23\). The factors are \(-14\) and \(-9\) because \(-14 imes (-9) = 126\) and \(-14 + (-9) = -23\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ &=3x(x^2-23x+126)\\ &=3x(x-14)(x-9)\end{aligned}$$
The factor \(x-c\) means \(c=14\) or \(c=9\), and the smallest of those two values is \(c=9\).

questions.stem: When the quadratic function \(g\) is graphed in the \(xy\)-plane, where \(y=g(x)\), its vertex is \((-7,-1)\). If one of the \(x\)-intercepts of this graph is \((-\frac{10}{3},0)\), what is the other \(x\)-intercept of the graph?
questions.explanation:
To find the second \(x\)-intercept, use the concept of symmetry about the vertex or axis of symmetry; the \(x\)-intercepts (and any pair of points with the same \(y\)-value) are equidistant from the vertex. Find the distance between \(x=-7\) and \(x=-\frac{10}{3}\) by subtracting.
$$\begin{aligned}\ &=-\frac{10}{3}- (-7)\\ &=-\frac{10}{3}+\frac{21}{3}=\frac{11}{3}\end{aligned}$$
The given \(x\)-intercept is to the right of the vertex, so to find the \(x\)-intercept to the left of the vertex, subtract the distance \(\frac{11}{3}\) from \(-7\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ &=-7-\frac{11}{3}\\ &=-\frac{21}{3}-\frac{11}{3}=-\frac{32}{3}\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem:
\(x\)\(g(x)\)
\(0\)\(-7\)
\(-1\)\(4\)
\(-3\)\(0\)
\(-6\)\(-5\)
The function \(g\) is defined by a polynomial. Some values of \(x\) and \(g(x)\) are shown in the table above. Which of the following must be a factor of \(g(x)\)?
questions.explanation: A polynomial in factored form is \(y=(x-p)(x-q)\) has \(x\)-intercepts at \((p,0)\) and \((q,0)\). From the table, you can see that \((-3,0)\) is an \(x\)-intercept, meaning \(x+3\) is a factor (it would look something like \(y=(x--3)(x-q)\) or \(y=(x+3)(x-q)\).

questions.stem: The function \(g\) is defined by \(g(x)=(x+1)(x - 4)(x-7)\). Function \(h\) is defined by \(h(x)=g(x)-5\). What is the value of \(h(0)\)?
questions.explanation:
To find \(h(0)\), substitute the \(g(x)\) function into \(h(x)=g(x)-5\), so \(h(x)=(x+1)(x -4)(x-7)-5\). Substitute \(0\) in for \(x\) and evaluate the function.
$$\begin{aligned}\ h(0)&=(0+1)(0 - 4)(0-7)-5\\ h(0)&=(1)(-4)(-7)-5\\ h(0)&=28-5\\ h(0)&=23\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem:
$$25x^2-bx+81=0$$
In the given equation, \(b\) is a constant. For which of the following values of \(b\) will the equation have more than one real solution?
questions.explanation:
When a problem states that there are a specific number of solutions or that the graphs intersect a specific number of times, this is a clue that you need to use the discriminant \(b^2-4ac\) where \(y=ax^2+bx+c\). Since this problem states that the equation must have more than one solution, use \(b^2-4ac>0\). From the equation, the values for the discriminant are \(a=25\), \(b=-b\) and \(c=81\). Use the discriminant inequality to solve for \(b\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ b^2-4ac&>0\\ (-b)^2-4(25)(81)&>0\\ b^2-\cancel{8100}+\cancel{8100}&>0+8100\\ \sqrt{b^2}&>\sqrt{8100}\\ b&>90 \, \text{or} \, b<-90\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem:
$$y=n(x^2-4x-32)$$
In the quadratic equation above, \(n\) is a nonzero constant. The graph of the equation in the \(xy\)-plane is a parabola with vertex \((h,k)\). Which of the following is equal to \(k\)?
questions.explanation:
This question asks you to find the \(y-\)value of the vertex, \(k\). For all parabolas, the \(x\)-value of the vertex is \(x=\frac{-b}{2a}\) where \(y=ax^2+bx+c\). In the given equation, you do not need to distribute the \(n\) because it does not effect the \(x-\)value of the vertex. Instead, use \(a=1\) and \(b=-4\), so \(x=\frac{-(-4)}{2(1)}=2\). Substitute \(2\) into the given equation to find the \(y-\)value of the vertex, \(k\):
$$\begin{aligned}\ y&=n(2^2-4(2)-32)\\ k&=n(4-8-32)\\ k&=n(-28)=-28n\end{aligned}$$
The \(y-\)value of the vertex is \(k=-28n\).

questions.stem: The expression \(5ax^2+36x+12\) must have as a factor which of the following expressions, where \(a\) is a positive integer constant?
questions.explanation:
Use factoring and the process of elimination to answer this question. Find the factor pair for each and check whether the middle term can be produced with a positive integer value of \(a\).
A) \(5ax^2+36x+12=(ax+2)( \quad )\). To find the factor pair, find the factor such that \(ax imes ( )=5ax^2\), so \(5x\). Likewise, find the factor such that \(2 imes ( )=12\), so \(6\). The factorization is \((ax+2)(5x+6)\). Multiply the outer and inner terms, so \(6ax+10x\). The sum of these must be \(36x\). Set them equal and solve for \(a\) to see whether it is a positive integer.
$$\begin{aligned}\ 6ax+10x&=36x\\ 6ax&=26x\\ a&=\frac{26}{6}\end{aligned}$$
Since \(a\) is not an integer, \(ax+2\) is not a factor, and A is incorrect.
B) \(5ax^2+36x+12=(ax+1)( \quad )\). Using the same method from answer A, the factorization is \((ax+1)(5x+12)\). The sum of the outer and inner terms is \(12ax+5x\). Set this expression equal to \(36x\) and solve for \(a\) to see whether it is a positive integer.
$$\begin{aligned}\ 12ax+5x&=36x\\ 12ax&=31x\\ a&=\frac{31}{12}\end{aligned}$$
Since \(a\) is not an integer, B is incorrect.
C) \(5ax^2+36x+12=(5ax+2)( \quad )\). Using the same method from answer A, the factorization is \((5ax+2)(x+6)\). The sum of the outer and inner terms is \(30ax+2x\). Set this expression equal to \(36x\) and solve for \(a\) to see whether it is a positive integer.
$$\begin{aligned}\ 30ax+2x&=36x\\ 30ax&=34x\\ a&=\frac{34}{30}\end{aligned}$$
Since \(a\) is not an integer, C is incorrect.
D) \(5ax^2+36x+12=(5ax+6)( \quad )\). Using the same method from answer A, the factorization is \((5ax+6)(x+2)\). The sum of the outer and inner terms is \(10ax+6x\). Set this expression equal to \(36x\) and solve for \(a\) to see whether it is a positive integer.
$$\begin{aligned}\ 10ax+6x&=36x\\ 10ax&=30x\\ a&=\frac{30}{10}=3\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: Which of the following equations represents a circle in the \(xy\)-plane that does not intersect the \(y\)-axis?
questions.explanation:
The equation of a circle is \((x-h)^2+(y-k)^2=r^2\), where \((h,k)\) is the center of the circle and \(r\) is the radius. In order for the circle \textit{not} to intersect the \(y\)-axis, the distance from the center of the circle to a point on the circle (which is the radius) must be \textit{less} than the horizontal shift of the circle to the right or left. The horizontal shift of the circle is equal to the \(x-\)value of the center, or \(h\).
Test answers to determine whether the \(\textit{length of the radius} < k\). In all four answer choices \(r^2=49\), so the radius is \(7\).
A) \((x-3)^2+(y-10)^2=49\): the \(x\)-value of the center is \(3\), so the center is \(3\) units to the right of the \(y\)-axis. Since the radius (\(7\)) is greater than the distance to the \(y-\)axis, this circle will intersect the \(y-\)axis. Eliminate A.
B) \((x+7)^2+y^2=49\): the \(x\)-value of the center is \(-7\), so the center is \(7\) units to the left of the \(y\)-axis. Since the radius is equal to the distance to the \(y-\)axis, this circle will intersect the \(y-\)axis (one time). Eliminate B.
C) \(x^2+(y+8)^2=49\): the \(x\)-value of the center is \(0\), so the center of this circle is extit{on} the \(y-\)axis. Eliminate C.
D) \((x+8)^2+(y-7)^2=49\): the \(x\)-value of the center is \(-8\), so the center is \(8\) units to the left of the \(y\)-axis. Since this distance is greater than the radius, this circle does not intersect the \(y-\)axis. This means the correct answer is \textbf{D}.

questions.stem:
$$3,3,3,9,9,9,10,10,10,10,12,12$$
Which frequency table correctly represents the data listed above?
questions.explanation:
Each table shows the number of times (frequency) that each value (number) appears in the data set. Count how many times each number is listed and match it with the correct table.
The data set matches table B.

questions.stem:

\(\text{Total}\)\(30\)\(50\)\(20\)\(100\)
If one of these shirts is selected at random, what is the probability of selecting a large shirt? (Express your answer as a decimal or fraction, not as a percent).
questions.explanation: The probability of an event is \(\frac{\text{desired outcome}}{\text{total outcomes}}\). The desired outcome is ''large shirts'', so \(20\) and the total outcomes is \(100\). The probability is \(\frac{20}{100}\) or \(\frac{1}{5}\).

questions.stem:

What is the median of the data shown?
questions.explanation:
The median is the middle number of an ordered set of numbers. The given set is already ordered from least to greatest. To find the middle number, count how many values are in the set. There are \(11\) in this set, so there will be \(\frac{11-1}{2}=5\) numbers on either side of the median. This means the median will be the sixth value from the left.
Alternatively, cross out pairs of numbers, starting with the smallest and largest, then the second smallest and second largest, continuing in this pattern until you reach the middle, or the median. This method is pictured below, and the median is \(208\).

questions.stem: A machine at a particular factory labels \(18\) products per minute at a constant rate. At this rate, how long will it take, in minutes, for the machine to label \(270\) products?
questions.explanation:
Use unit conversions to solve this problem. Since the answer is in minutes and the given ratio is in products per minute, multiply by another ratio that will cancel products, leaving minutes in the numerator.
$$\begin{aligned}\ &=\frac{1\, \text{minute}}{18 \, \text{\cancel{products}}} \times 270 \, \text{\cancel{products}}\\ &= \frac{270}{18} \, \text{products}\\ &= 15 \, \text{products} \end{aligned}$$
Alternatively, write and solve an equation that relates the given values. Let \(n=\) number of minutes, so \(18n=270\). Divide both sides by \(18\) to get \(15\).

questions.stem:
The table shows the frequency of test scores for \(30\) students in each of class M and class N. Which of the following statements best compares the median test scores of the two classes?
\(\text{Score}\)\(\text{Class M}\)\(\text{Class N}\)
\(75\)\(12\)\(2\)
\(80\)\(10\)\(3\)
\(85\)\(6\)\(2\)
\(90\)\(1\)\(5\)
\(95\)\(1\)\(5\)
\(100\)\(0\)\(7\)
choices: The median score of class M is less than the median score of class N.
The median score of class M is equal to the median score of class N.
The median score of class M is greater than the median score of class N.
There is not enough information to compare the median scores of the two classes.
questions.explanation:
The median is the middle number of an ordered set of numbers. The given sets are ordered from least to greatest, but they appear in a frequency table instead of listing out all of the scores individually. There are \(30\) scores in each set, so the median score will be occur halfway between the \(15th\) and \(16th\) values.
To find where that would occur add the frequencies of each score until you find the row that would contain the \(15\text{th}\) and \(16\text{th}\) values. For class M, the first score, \(75\), appears \(12\) times and the second score, \(80\) appears \(10\) times, so the \(15th\) and \(16th\) scores will occur in the row that has a score of \(80\) (\(12+10=22\)). For class N, \(2+3+2+5=12\), and then adding the frequency for the next score is \(12+11=23\), so the \(15th\) and \(16th\) values will be in the score of \(95\). Since class M has a median of \(80\) and class N of \(95\), the median for class M is less than that of class N.

questions.stem: There are \(\text{481,000}\) sunflower seeds out of a total of \(\text{650,000}\) seeds. What percentage of the seeds are sunflower seeds?
questions.explanation:
To find the percentage of sunflower seeds, divide the number of sunflower seeds by the total number of seeds and multiply by \(100\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ &=\frac{\text{part}}{\text{total}} \times 100\\ &=\frac{\text{481,000}}{\text{650,000}} \times 100\\ &= 74\%end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: In a certain supermarket, \(61\) out of every \(\text{1,000}\) products have passed their sell by date. If a product is selected at random, what is the probability of selecting a product that is past its sell by date?
questions.explanation:
In a certain supermarket, \(61\) out of every \(\text{1,000}\) products have passed their sell by date. If a product is selected at random, what is the probability of selecting a product that is past its sell by date?
The probability of an event is \(\frac{\text{desired outcome}}{\text{total outcomes}}\). The desired outcome is ``products passed sell by date'', so \(61\) and the total outcomes is \(\text{1,000}\). The probability is \(\frac{61}{\text{1,000}}\).

questions.stem: Out of \(700\) students admitted to a summer internship program, \(60\%\) of them accepted their offer to join. How many students accepted?
questions.explanation: To find \(60\%\) of \(700\), multiply \(0.6 \times 700=420\).

questions.stem: An object travels at a constant speed of \(26\) meters per minute. At this speed, what is the time, in minutes, that it would take for the object to travel \(286\) meters?
questions.explanation:
Use unit conversions to solve this problem. Since the answer is in minutes and the given ratio is in meters per minute, multiply by another ratio that will cancel meters, leaving minutes in the numerator.
$$\begin{aligned}\&=\frac{1\, \text{minute}}{26 \, \text{\cancel{meters}}} imes 286 \, \text{\cancel{meters}}\\ &= \frac{286}{26} \, \text{minutes}= 11 \, \text{minutes} \end{aligned}$$
Alternatively, write and solve an equation that relates the given values. Let \(n=\)number of minutes, so \(26n=286\). Divide both sides by \(26\) to get \(11\).

questions.stem: A hummingbird flaps its wings at a constant rate of \(64\) times per second. At what rate, in flaps per minute, does the hummingbird flap its wings?
questions.explanation:
Use unit conversions to solve this problem. Since the answer needs to be in flaps per minute and the given ratio is in flaps per second, multiply by another ratio that will cancel seconds, leaving flaps per minute as the ratio. Since there are \(60\) seconds in one minute, multiply by \(60\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ &=\frac{64\, \text{flaps}}{1 \, \cancel{\text{second}}} \times \frac{60 \, \cancel{\text{seconds}}}{1 \, \text{minute}}= {\text{3,840}}\frac{\text{flaps}}{\text{minutes}} \end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: \(70\%\) out of \(500\) cell phones in a particular order were shipped internationally. How many of these cell phones were shipped internationally?
questions.explanation: To find \(70\%\) of \(500\), multiply \(0.7 \times 500=350\).

questions.stem: The bar graph shows the distribution of \(399\) tickets sold by \(6\) different age groups for a town fair. Which best approximates how many more tickets were sold by the 11-12 year olds than by the 17-18 year olds?
questions.explanation: Approximate from the graph the number of tickets sold by 11 - 12 year olds and 17 - 18 year olds, and find the difference between them. 11 - 12 y.o. sold approximately \(105\) tickets, and 17 - 18 y.o. sold approximately \(78\) tickets, so the difference is \(105-78=27\).

questions.stem: Which of the following equations is the most appropriate linear model for the data shown in the scatterplot?
questions.explanation: Look at the general trend of the points on the scatterplot to determine whether the slope of a linear model would be positive or negative. Here, the trend is increasing (as \(x\) increases, \(y\) also increases), so eliminate A and B because they have negative slopes. Then look at the \(y\)-intercept to choose the correct answer; the remaining choices are positive \(2.75\) and \(-2.75\). A line through the points would cross on the positive \(y\)-axis, so \(2.75\) is correct, making C the correct answer.

questions.stem:
A list of \(8\) data values is shown.

What is the mean of this data set?
questions.explanation:
The mean, or average, is found by adding the values of a data set and dividing by the number of values in the data set. It is given that there are \(8\) numbers, so you need to find the sum of them and then divide by \(8\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ &=\frac{4+22+19+8+41+5+16+5}{8}\\ &=\frac{120}{8}= 15\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem:
