choices: The mean of data set A is greater than the mean of data set B.
The mean of data set A is less than the mean of data set B.
The means of data set A and data set B are equal.
There is not enough information to compare the means.
questions.explanation: The mean, or average, is found by adding the values of a data set and dividing by the number of values in the data set. Before calculating the mean of each set, take a closer look at the sets to compare them. Data set A contains all of data set B and has one additional \(11\). Find the mean of data set B; \(\frac{1+10+11+22}{4}=11\). Since the mean of data set B is \(11\), the \(4\) overlapping values in data set A also have a mean of \(11\). Adding an additional \(11\) to data set A will keep the mean unchanged, since it is the mean. The means of the two sets are equal.

questions.stem: A certain park has an area of \(\text{76,491,035}\) square feet. What is the area, in square miles, of this park? (\(1\) mile = \(\text{5,280}\) feet)
questions.explanation:
This problem deals with area, so the given conversion between miles and feet needs to be changed to reflect square miles and square feet. Square both sides of the unit conversion equation \(1\) mile \(= \text{5,280}\) feet. \(1^2\) square miles \(= \text{5,280}^2\) square feet, so \(1\) square mile \(= \text{27,878,400}\) square feet. The answer is in square feet, so use unit conversions to cancel square feet so that square miles remains in the numerator.
$$\begin{aligned}\&=\frac{1 \text{square mile}}{ ext{27,878,400 \, \cancel{square feet}}} \times \text{76,491,035} \, \text{\cancel{square feet}}\\ &=\frac{\text{76,491,035}}{\text{27,878,400}} \, \text{square miles}= 2.74 \, \text{square miles} \end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: There are \(800\) lawyers at a conference. A sample of the lawyers was selected at random and asked whether they planned to return to the conference the following year. Of those surveyed, \(70\%\) responded that they planned to return to the conference the following year. Based on this survey, which of the following is the best estimate of the total number of lawyers at the conference this year who plan to attend the following year?
questions.explanation: To find the percentage of lawyers expected to return the following year, multiply the decimal equivalent of \(70\%\) by the total number of lawyers, so \(0.7 \times 800=560\)

questions.stem: Grass Seed X, which is used to grow grass on golf courses, is so efficient that one pound can be used to grow grass for \(3\) golf holes. If one golf hole has an average area of approximately \(1\frac{1}{4}\) hectare, about how many hectares could \(64\) pounds of Grass Seed X cover?
questions.explanation:
The given information in this question is the number of holes covered per pound of grass seed and the answer is in hectares. There are two conversions necessary to get to hectares from holes per pound of grass seed. First multiply by the number of hectares per hole to cancel the unit of ``golf holes''.
$$\begin{aligned}\&=\frac{3\, \text{\cancel{holes}}}{1 \, \text{{pound}}} \times \frac{1.25 \, \text{hectares}}{1 \, \text{\cancel{hole}}}\\ &= \frac{3.75 \, \text{hectares}}{1 \, \text{pound}} \end{aligned}$$
You have the number of hectares covered per pound, so multiply by \(64\) pounds to find out how many hectares can be covered by that amount.
$$\begin{aligned}\ &= \frac{3.75 \, \text{hectares}}{1 \, \text{\cancel{pound}}} \times 64 \, \text{\cancel{pounds}} = 240 \, \text{hectares}\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: Zia can edit \(15\) pages in \(17.2\) minutes. If she edits at this same rate, which of the following is closest to the number of pages she can edit in \(3\) hours?
questions.explanation:
Use unit conversions to solve this problem. The given rate is given in pages per \(17.2\) minutes. The unit for the answer is pages, so to find out how many pages can be edited in \(3\) hours, multiply the rate by \(180\) minutes to cancel minutes and give the answer in pages.
$$\begin{aligned}\&\frac{15\, \text{pages}}{17.2 \, \text{\cancel{minutes}}} \times 180 \, \text{\cancel{minutes}}= 157 \, \text{pages} \end{aligned}$$

questions.stem:
The scatterplot shows the relationship between two variables, \(x\) and \(y\). A line of best fit is also shown.
Which of the following equations best represents the line of best fit shown?
questions.explanation: Determine whether the slope of the line of best fit is positive or negative. The line of best fit is negative (as \(x\) increases, \(y\) decreases), so eliminate A and C because they have positive slopes. Then look at the \(y\)-intercept to choose the correct answer; the remaining choices are positive \(18.4\) and \(-18.4\). This line of best fit has a positive \(y\)-intercept, so \(18.4\) is the correct \(y\)-intercept, making B the correct answer.

questions.stem: From a production run of \(\text{20,000}\) board games, \(\text{2,000}\) were chosen at random and observed for flaws. Based on the test, it is estimated that \(5\%\) of the board games in the full production run have flaws, with an associated margin of error of \(2\%\). Based on these results, which of the following is a plausible value for the total number of board games in the production run that have flaws?
questions.explanation:
The margin of error, \(2\%\), sets up an upper and lower boundary for the plausible number of board games that have flaws. \(5\%\) had flaws with a \(2\%\) margin of error, so up to \(7\%\) and down to \(3\%\) could have flaws. Find \(3\%\) and \(7\%\) of \(\text{20,000}\) and choose the answer that falls between that range.
$$\begin{aligned}\ &=0.03 \times \text{20,000}=600\\ &=0.07 \times \text{20,000}=\text{1,400}\end{aligned}$$
The answer between \(600\) and \(\text{1,400}\) is \(900\).

questions.stem: Based on the graph, which of the following best describes the general trend in annual attendance at this amusement park from 1981 through 1991?
choices: Attendance generally decreased each year since 1981.
Attendance generally increased each year since 1981.
Attendance decreased until 1987 and then increased.
Attendance generally remained consistent from 1981 through 1991.
questions.explanation: This graph has two distinct parts, one where the attendance trends downward and one where it trends upward (as shown by the drawn in lines). The answer that describes this trend is C.

questions.stem:

questions.explanation: The median is the middle number of an ordered set of numbers. The given set is not in order, so first write it in order from least to greatest; \(1, 2, 2, 17, 19, 24, 41, 93\). To find the middle number, count how many values are in the set. Since there are \(8\) numbers in this set (even), the median is halfway between (or the average of) the middle two values. The middle two values are the \(4th\) and \(5th\) values, so \(17\) and \(19\), and the average of those is \(\frac{17+19}{2}=18\). This method is pictured below.

The table above shows the distribution of ages and sports for a collection of \(50\) sports cards. If a card will be auctioned online at random, what is the probability that the auctioned card will be either an antique baseball card or a new release basketball card?
questions.explanation: The probability of an event is \(\frac{\text{desired outcome}}{\text{total outcomes}}\). The desired outcomes are ``antique baseball'' or ``new basketball''. The probability of either one of two events occurring is the sum of the two individual probabilities, so \(7+4=11\). The total number of outcomes is \(50\). The probability is \(\frac{11}{50}\).

questions.stem: A particular piece of fabric has an area of \(6.25\) square yards. What is the area, in square millimeters, of this fabric? (\(1\) yard \(=914.4\) millimeters)
questions.explanation:
This problem deals with area, so the given conversion between yards and millimeters needs to be changed to reflect square yards and square millimeters. Square both sides of the unit conversion equation \(1\) yard \(=914.4\) millimeters. \(1^2\) square yards \(= 914.4^2\) square millimeters, so \(1\) square yard \(= \text{836,127.36}\) square millimeters; multiply both sides of this equation by \(6.25\) to find out how many square millimeters \(6.25\) square yards is.
$$\begin{aligned}\1\, \text{square yard} \times 6.25& = \text{836,127.36} \, \text{square millimeters} \times 6.25\\ 6.25\, \text{square yards}&= \text{5,225,796} \, \text{square millimeters} \end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: A research study was conducted in order to determine if Phonics Program A improves the word decoding skills of children 4 - 6 years old. From a large population of children ages 4 - 6 years old who had beginner word decoding skills, \(400\) participants were selected at random. Half of the participants were randomly assigned to use Phonics Program A over a period of several months, and the other half did not use Phonics Program A or any other phonics program. The resulting data showed that participants who used Phonics Program A had a significant increase in their decoding skills as compared to those who did not use Phonics Program A. Based on the results of the study and the design of the study, which of the following is an appropriate conclusion?
choices: Phonics Program A is likely to improve the decoding skills of children ages 4 - 6 who have beginning decoding skills.
Phonics Program A improves decoding skills for children ages 4 - 6 better than all other available phonics programs.
Phonics Program A will improve the decoding skills of any child who uses it.
Phonics Program A will cause a significant improvement in decoding skills.
questions.explanation: This problem is about the strength of conclusions that can be drawn from a research study. In general (not always), the correct answer uses the least strong language. B, C, and D have language that draws too strong of a conclusion for the type of study done. B says the program improves skills better than ``all'' other programs, which is too strong of a conclusion. C uses the phrase ``will improve'' and for ``any child'', which again is too strong. And D says ``will cause a significant improvement'', which is too strong. There is not enough evidence from this study to prove causation or to use certainty language.

questions.stem: What is the value of \(n\) if \(n\%\) of \(40\) is \(19\). What is the value of \(n\)?
questions.explanation: This percent problem can be directly translated into a math equation; \(n\%\) is \(\frac{n}{100}\) ``and of'' means multiply, so ``\(n\%\) of \(40\)'' is \(\frac{n}{100} \times 40\). Finally, ``is \(19\)'' means equals \(19\), so the final equation to solve is \(\frac{n}{100} \times 40 = 19\), or \(\frac{40}{100} \times n = 19\), which equals \(0.4n = 19\). Divide both sides by \(0.4\), so \(\frac{19}{0.4}=247.5\).

questions.stem: Each of the dot plots shown represents the number of goals scored by each player of two soccer teams, the Tigers and the Falcons. Which statement best describes the standard deviations of the numbers of goals scored by each player of these two teams?
choices: The standard deviation of the number of goals scored by each player on the Tigers team is less than the standard deviation of the number of goals scored by each player on the Falcons team.
The standard deviation of the number of goals scored by each player on the Tigers team is equal to the standard deviation of the number of goals scored by each player on the Falcons team.
The standard deviation of the number of goals scored by each player on the Tigers team is greater than the standard deviation of the number of goals scored by each player on the Falcons team.
There is not enough information to compare these standard deviations.
questions.explanation: Standard deviation is the average distance the data is from the mean. The distribution in these sets is identical; there are equal numbers of dots above each number and the axis numbers are separated by one unit. Even though the means are different (the values along each axis result in different means), the distance of the data from those means are equally distributed.

questions.stem: At Central High School, \(21\) out of every \(50\) students participates in a sport. If a student is selected at random, what is the probability that they do not participate in a sport?
questions.explanation: The probability of an event is \(\frac{\text{desired outcome}}{\text{total outcomes}}\). The desired outcome is ``did \textit{not} participate in a sport'', but the given information is the number of students who \textit{did} participate in a sport. Subtract this number, \(21\), from the total number of students, \(50\), so \(50-21=29\) students did not participate in a sport. The total outcomes is \(50\) because the given information is out of every \(50\) students. The probability of that a randomly selected student did not participate in a sport is therefore, \(\frac{29}{50}\), so C is the correct answer.

questions.stem: In a particular public transportation system, there are \(\text{2,160,000}\) monthly ticket holders out of a total of \(\text{9,000,000}\) commuters. What percentage of the commuters are monthly ticket holders?
questions.explanation:
To find the percentage of monthly ticket holders out of all commuters, divide the number of monthly ticket holders by the total number of commuters, and multiply that result by \(100\).
$$\begin{aligned}\&=\frac{\text{part}}{\text{total}} \times 100\\ &=\frac{\text{2,160,000}}{\text{9,000,000}} \times 100\\&= 24\% \end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: The engineering department at a company has \(33\) new employees whose mean starting salary is \(\$\text{84,000}\). The sales department at this same company has \(42\) new employees whose mean starting salary is \(\$\text{47,000}\). No other department at this company has new employees. The salaries of the new employees of these two departments are entered into a spreadsheet to calculate the mean salary for new employees at this company. What is the mean salary for new employees at this company?
questions.explanation:
To find a combined mean, you must first find the sum of the individual data sets. Since the \(\text{mean}=\frac{\text{sum of values}}{\text{number of values in the set}}\), the sum of the values \(=\) the mean \(\times\) the number of values in the set. Find the sum of the salaries for each group.
To find a combined mean, you must first find the sum of the individual data sets. Since the \(\text{mean}=\frac{\text{sum of values}}{\text{number of values in the set}}\), the sum of the values \(=\) the mean \(\times\) the number of values in the set. Find the sum of the salaries for each group.
$$\begin{aligned} 33 \times \text{84,000}=\text{2,772,000}\end{aligned}$$
$$\begin{aligned} 42 \times \text{47,000}=\text{1,974,000}\end{aligned}$$
Find the combined mean by dividing the sum of these two salary totals by the total number of employees in both departments, so \(33+42=75\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ &= \frac{\text{2,772,000}+\text{1,974,000}}{75}= \text{63,280}\end{aligned}$$

Given that one of the varsity teams is selected, what is the probability that the team is a basketball team? (Express your answer as a decimal or fraction, not as a percent).

questions.stem:
Data set F consists of \(9\) positive integers greater than \(10\).
$$20,27,19,31,29,20,21,17$$
The list above contains \(8\) of the integers from data set F. The mean of these \(8\) integers is \(23\) and the mean of data set F is an integer that is less than \(23\), what is the value of the smallest integer from data set F?
questions.explanation:
Find the mean (average) by adding the values of a data set and dividing by the number of values in the data set. There is a missing number from the given data set F. Let \(n=\) the missing number from data set F, so the average is \(\frac{20+27+19+31+29+20+21+17+n}{9}=\frac{184+n}{9}\). This average must an \textbf{integer} less than \(23\), and \(n\) must be an integer greater than \(10\). Write and solve an inequality for the missing integer.
$$\begin{aligned}\ \frac{184+n}{\cancel{9}} \times \cancel{9}&<23 \times 9\\ 184+n&<207 \end{aligned}$$
Since the average is an integer, the sum must be divisible by \(9\), so find multiples of \(9\) between \(184\) and \(207\). \(9 \times 20 = 180\), so \(189\) and \(198\) are the only options. Discard \(189\) because \(n\neq5\) (\(n>10\)), so the answer is \(n=198-184=14\).

questions.stem: A manufacturer has \(64\) chairs, all of which they plan to sell. The sales goal that the manufacturer plans to meet or exceed is \(\$\text{9,000}\), which includes a \(4\%\) shipping cost. Which of the following is closest to the minimum possible price per chair, before sales tax, the business owner must charge to meet or exceed the sales goal?
questions.explanation:
Write and solve an inequality that relates the price of the chairs and the sales goal. Let \(p=\) price per chair. This means the manufacturer charges \(64p\) for the chairs plus another \(4\%\) of the cost of the chairs, so \(0.04(64p)\). The inequality is \(64p + 0.4(64p) \geq \text{9,000}\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ 64p + 0.4(64p) & \geq \text{9,000}\\ 1.04(64p) & \geq \text{9,000}\\ \frac{\cancel{66.56}p}{\cancel{66.56}} & \geq \frac{\text{9,000}}{\cancel{66.56}}\\ p & \geq 135.216 \end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: Two data sets of \(25\) whole numbered test scores each are summarized in the histograms shown. For each of the histograms, the first interval represents the frequency of test scores greater than or equal to \(0\), but less than \(20\). The second interval represents the frequency of test scores greater than or equal to \(20\), but less than \(40\), and so on. What is the smallest possible difference between the mean of data set K and the mean of data set L?
questions.explanation:
K scores < L scores (generally), so the smallest difference occurs when K scores are maximizes and L scores are minimized. To maximize K: 0 - 20 excludes \(20\), so the max value is \(19\) (then \(39\), \(59\), and \(79\) for K). Intervals include the minimum value in their interval, so L scores are minimized at \(0\), \(20\0, etc.
Find the maximum average for K and the minimum average for L. To find the average, multiply each score by the frequency of that band. For example, K has \(3\) test scores in the 0 - 20 band, so multiply \(19 \times 3\). There are \(25\) test scores for each of K and L, so the denominator is \(25\).
$$\begin{aligned} \text{K Avg}&=\frac{19(3) + 40(5) + 60(8) + 80(8)}{25}\\ &=\frac{1475}{25} \\&=59 \end{aligned}$$
$$\begin{aligned} \text{L Avg}&=\frac{20(4) + 40(5) + 60(8) + 80(8)}{25}\\ &=\frac{1400}{25}\\&=56\end{aligned}$$
The minimum difference between the two averages is \(59-56=3\).

The list above contains \(6\) of the integers from data set A. The mean of these \(6\) integers is \(40\) and the mean of data set A is an integer that is greater than \(40\), what is the value of the largest integer from data set A?

questions.stem:
NumberFrequency
\(1\)\(3\)
\(2\)\(n\)
\(5\)\(4\)
\(8\)\(5\)
\(9\)\(3\)
What is the value of \(n\) if the mean of the data set shown in the frequency table is \(5\)?
questions.explanation:
The mean is given, so use the mean formula to solve for the missing value \(n\). The mean (or average) is \(\text{mean}= \frac{\text{sum of values}}{\text{number of values}}\). A frequency table summarizes the number of times a value occurs in a data set. This frequency table indicates that there are three 1s, an unknown number of 2s, five 4s, etc. To find the sum of the values, therefore, multiply the value (number) by the frequency, and add those products. Then divide the number of values in the data set.
The sum of the values: \((1 \times 3) + 2n + (5 \times 4) + (8 \times 5) + (9 \times 3)= 2n+90\).
The number of values in the set: \(3+n+4+5+3=n+15\)
Substitute the expressions into the mean formula and solve for \(n\):
$$\begin{aligned}\ 5&= \frac{2n+90}{n+15} \end{aligned}$$
To solve, multiply both sides by \(n+15\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ 5(n+15)&= 2n+90\\ 5n\cancel{+75}-2n\cancel{-75}&=\cancel{2n}-\cancel{2n}+90-75\\ 3n&=15\\ n&=5 \end{aligned}$$
The frequency of \(2\) is \(5\).

questions.stem: A square has side lengths of \(72\). What is the perimeter of this square?
questions.explanation: To perimeter of a square is found by the formula \(P=4s\) where \(s=\) the side length. The perimeter is \(P=4(72)=288\).

questions.stem: Triangles \(CDE\) and \(LMN\) are similar triangles, where angles \(D\) and \(M\) are right angles and angle \(C\) corresponds to angle \(L\). If \(\tan(L)=\frac{485}{476}\), what is the value of \(\tan(C)\)?
questions.explanation: Similar triangles have corresponding sides that are proportional and corresponding angles that are congruent. This means that angles C and L are congruent. It also means that their trigonometric ratios will be equal. Here, since angle C corresponds to angle L, \(\tan(C)=\tan(L)\), so \(\tan(C)=\frac{485}{476}\).

questions.stem: In the figure shown, line \(a\) intersects parallel lines \(l\) and \(m\). What is the value of \(y\)?
questions.explanation:
The given angle (\(45 ^\circ\)) and \(y\) are same side interior angles, and if the lines are parallel, same side interior angles are supplementary, which means they have a sum of \(180 ^\circ\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ \cancel{45}+y-\cancel{45}&=180-45\\ y&=135 \end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: What is the area, in square feet, of a rectangular garden with a length of \(28\) feet (ft) and a width of \(61\) ft?
questions.explanation: Use the formula for the area of a rectangle, which is \(A=lw\) where \(l=\) the length of the rectangle and \(w=\) the width of the rectangle. The area of this rectangle is \(A=28 \times 61=\text{1,708}\).

questions.stem: A circle with its center at Point \(A\) has a central angle of \(135^\circ\). What is the measure, in degrees, of its intercepted minor arc \(BC\)?
questions.explanation: Central angles have measures that are equal to their intercepted arcs. Since the measure of the central angle is \(135^\circ\), the measure of its intercepted arc is also \(135^\circ\).

questions.stem: In the figure, line \(l\) is parallel to line \(m\). What is the value of \(x\)?
questions.explanation: The given angle (\(40^\circ\)) and \(x\) are corresponding angles, which are congruent if the lines are parallel. The value of \(x\) is, therefore, \(40\).

questions.stem: The figure above shows parallel lines \(a\) and \(b\), and line \(l\) intersects both lines. What is the value of \(n\)?
questions.explanation: The given angle (\(131^\circ\)) forms a linear pair and is supplementary to the angle adjacent to it. That angle is \(180-131=49\) degrees. This angle is then corresponding to the angle that measures \(n^\circ\), and corresponding angles are congruent if the lines are parallel. This means that \(n=49\).

questions.stem: What is the area, in square yards, of a rectangular field with a width of \(65\) yards (yds) and a length of \(18\) yds?
questions.explanation: Use the formula for the area of a rectangle, which is \(A=lw\) where \(l=\) the length of the rectangle and \(w=\) the width of the rectangle. The area of this rectangle is \(A=65 \times 18=\text{1,170}\) square yards.

questions.stem: An angle has a measure of \(900^\circ\). What is the measure of the angle, in radians?
questions.explanation:
To convert an angle from degrees to radians, multiply the angle by \(\frac{\pi}{180}\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ &=900 \times \frac{\pi}{180}\\ &=5\pi\end{aligned}$$
If you can't remember when to multiply by \(\frac{180}{\pi}\) as opposed to \(\frac{\pi}{180}\), think about what you are trying to accomplish in your answer. Here, to go from radians to degrees, the \(\pi\) must cancel out. Since it is in the numerator, multiply it such that \(\pi\) is in the denominator to cancel the \(\pi\).

questions.stem: The edge length of a cube is \(54\) centimeters. What is the surface area, in square centimeters, of the cube?
questions.explanation: The surface area of a 3D shape is the sum of the areas of all of the faces. Since a cube has six congruent faces, find the area of one face (a square) and multiply it by \(6\). The formula is \(A_c=6s^2\), where \(s=\) side (or edge length). The surface area of this cube is \(A_c=6(54)^2=\text{17,496}\).

questions.stem: In triangles \(GHI\) and \(JKL\), sides \(GH\) and \(JK\) each have lengths of \(4\) inches and angles \(I\) and \(L\) each have measure \(83^\circ\). Which additional piece of information is sufficient to determine whether triangle \(GHI\) is congruent to triangle \(JKL\)?
questions.explanation: Draw triangles \(GHI\) and \(JKL\), \(GH\) and \(JK\) as congruent, and angles \(I\) and \(L\) as congruent. To prove whether these triangles are congruent, additional information has to be about a pair of corresponding angles (AAS), so A is the correct answer. One pair of additional corresponding sides is not enough to conclude whether the triangles are congruent because SSA is not a congruence theorem. Additionally, it is never enough to just know one additional side of one of the triangles (like in answer B).

questions.stem: Triangle \(ABC\) is similar to triangle \(DEF\) such that \(A\), \(B\), and \(C\) correspond to \(D\), \(E\), and \(F\), respectively. The length of \(AB\) is \(18\) inches and \(3EF=BC\). What is the length of \(DE\)?
questions.explanation:
Similar triangles have corresponding sides that are proportional and corresponding angles that are congruent. Begin with a diagram labeling each triangle. Since \(3EF=BC\), let \(x=BC\) and \(3x=EF\). Additionally, let \(y=DE\), the side in question.
\(DE\) corresponds to \(AB\) and \(EF\) corresponds to \(BC\). Set up and solve a proportion for \(y\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ \frac{1\cancel{x}}{3\cancel{x}}&= \frac{y}{18}\\ 18 \times \frac{1}{3}&= \frac{y}{\cancel{18}} \times \cancel{18}\\ y&=6\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: The circle shown has center \(O\), circumference \(160\pi\), and diameters \(BD\) and \(AC\). Then length of arc \(BC\) is one third the length of arc \(CD\). What is the length of arc \(AB\)?
questions.explanation:
Since \(BD\) is a diameter, arc \(BCD\) measures one-half of the circumference, so \(\frac{1}{2}160\pi=80\pi\). That semicircle is further divided into the ratio \(\overset{\frown}{BC}=\frac{1}{3}\overset{\frown}{CD}\), so let \(x=\overset{\frown}{CD}\) and therefore \(\overset{\frown}{BC}= rac{1}{3}x\).
Write and solve an equation where the sum of those two arcs is \(80\pi\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ \frac{1}{3}x+x&= 80\pi\\ \frac{4}{3}x&= 80\pi\\ \frac{\cancel{3}}{\cancel{4}} \frac{\cancel{4}}{\cancel{3}}x&= 80\pi \times \frac{3}{4}\\ x&= 60\pi\end{aligned}$$
Angles AOB and COD are vertical angles, and vertical angles are congruent. They are also congruent central angles, which means the arcs they intercepts are congruent. Therefore, \(\overset{\frown}{CD}=\overset{\frown}{AB}\), and \(\overset{\frown}{AB}\) also has a length of \(60\pi\).

questions.stem: What is the width, in square inches, of a rectangle with a length of \(29\) inches and an area of \(\text{1,856}\) square inches?
questions.explanation:
The formula for the area of a rectangle is \(A=lw\) where \(l=\) the length of the rectangle and \(w=\) the width of the rectangle. Substitute the given values into the formual and solve for the width.
$$\begin{aligned}\ A&= lw\\ \frac{\text{1,856}}{29}&= \frac{\cancel{29}w}{\cancel{29}}\\ w&=64\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: Isosceles triangles \(QRS\) and \(TUV\) are congruent, where \(Q\), \(R\), and \(S\) correspond to \(T\), \(U\), and \(V\), respectively. The measure of angle \(Q\) is \(92^\circ\). What is the measure of angle \(V\)?
questions.explanation:
The problem states that angle \(Q\) corresponds to angle \(T\), so they are congruent, and angle \(T=92^\circ\). This is an obtuse angle in an isosceles triangle, so it must be the vertex angle (the one between the congruent sides). This means the other two angles, \(\angle V\) and \(\angle U\), are congruent. Use the Triangle Sum Property (the sum of the angles of a triangle is \(180^\circ\)) to write and solve an equation. Let \(x=m\angle{V}\) (therefore \(x\) is also the measure of angle \(U\)).
$$\begin{aligned}\ m\angle{T}+m\angle{U}+m\angle{V}&=180\\ 92+x+x&=180\\ \cancel{92}+2x-\cancel{92}&=180-92\\ \frac{\cancel{2}x}{\cancel{2}}&=\frac{88}{2}\\ x&=44\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: What is the volume of a cube that has a surface area of \(\text{22,326}\) square centimeters?
questions.explanation:
To find the volume of the cube, first find the edge length. Use the formula for the surface area of a cube to solve for the edge length. The formula is \(A_c=6s^2\) where \(s=\) the edge (or side) length. Substitute the surface area into the formula to solve for \(s\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ \frac{\text{22,326}}{6}&=\frac{\cancel{6}s^2}{\cancel{6}}\\ \sqrt{\text{3,721}}&=\sqrt{s^2}\\ s&=61\end{aligned}$$
Use the side length \(s=61\) in the volume of a cube formula \(V_c=s^3\) to find the volume. \(V=61^3=\text{226,981}\).

questions.stem: For a certain cylinder, the ratio of its radius to its height is \(30\) to \(48\). If the height of the cylinder decreases by \(4\) units, how much must the radius change to maintain this ratio?
questions.explanation:
Begin with the radius to height ratio; \( rac{30}{48}\). Let this be the actual radius and height, such that the new height is \(48-4=44\). Let \(x=\) the new radius, and write and solve a proportion to solve for \(x\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ \frac{30}{48}&=\frac{x}{44}\\ 44 \times \frac{30}{48}&=\frac{x}{\cancel{44}} \times{\cancel{44}}\\ x&=27.5\end{aligned}$$
Subtract the radii, \(30-27.5=2.5\). This means the radius decreases by \(2.5\).

questions.stem: In a triangle \(FGH\), the measure of \(\angle{G}\) is \(90^\circ\), \(FG=18\), and \(FH=30\). Triangle \(IJK\) is similar to triangle \(FGH\), where vertices \(I\), \(J\), and \(K\) correspond to vertices \(F\), \(G\), and \(H\), respectively, and each side of triangle \(IJK\) is \(\frac{1}{5}\) the length of the corresponding side of triangle \(FGH\). What is the value of tan \(K\)?
questions.explanation:
Similar triangles have corresponding sides that are proportionate and corresponding angles that are congruent. Draw one right triangle and label it with vertices \(F\), \(G\), and \(H\). Label the corresponding angles, \(I\), \(J\), in \(K\) in their corresponding vertices.
The trigonometric ratio for tangent relates the opposite side over the adjacent side, and is equivalent in similar triangles for corresponding (congruent) angles. In other words, \(\tan{H}=\tan{K}\). This means you can use the given side lengths to solve for \(\tan{K}\), so \(\tan(K)=\frac{18}{GH}\). Use the Pythagorean Theorem \(a^2+b^2=c^2\) where \(c=\) the length of the hypotenuse, to solve for \(GH\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ a^2+b^2&=c^2\\ 18^2+{GH}^2&=30^2\\ \cancel{324}+{GH}^2-\cancel{324}&=900-324\\ \sqrt{{GH}^2}&=\sqrt{576}\\ GH&=24\end{aligned}$$
Substitute \(24\) into the tangent ratio equation to evaluate the tangent; \( an(K)= rac{18}{24}=\frac{3}{4}\)

questions.stem: In isosceles triangle \(DEF\), the measure of angle \(D\) is \(96^\circ\). What is the measure of angle \(F\)?
questions.explanation:
Since angle \(Q\) is obtuse (\(90^\circ180\), the sum of the \textbf{three} angles of a triangle. This means \(m\angle{E}=m\angle{F}\). Use the Triangle Sum Property to write and solve an equation for \(x\) where \(x=\) the measure of angle \(F\) (and therefore angle \(E\)).
$$\begin{aligned}\ m\angle{D}+m\angle{E}+m\angle{F}&=180\\ 96+x+x&=180\\ \cancel{96}+2x-\cancel{96}&=180-96\\ \frac{\cancel{2}x}{\cancel{2}}&=\frac{84}{2}\\ x&=42\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: For the right triangle shown, \(a=6\) and \(b=2\). Which expression represents the value of c, then length of the hypotenuse?
questions.explanation:
Use the Pythagorean Theorem, \(a^2+b^2=c^2\) where \(c=\) the length of the hypotenuse (which is also \(c\) in the diagram). Before starting to solve for \(c\) note that the answers are an intermediate step rather than a single, numerical answer. Only go as far as you need to in order to find the correct answer.
$$\begin{aligned}\ a^2+b^2&=c^2\\ 6^2+2^2&=c^2\\ \sqrt{6^2+2^2}&=\sqrt{c^2}\\ c&=\sqrt{6^2+2^2} \end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: In triangle ABC, \(\angle{B}\) has a measure of \(90^\circ\). What is the value of \(\sin(A)\), if \(\cos(C)=\frac{10}{11}\).
questions.explanation:
Since angle \(B\) is a right angle in this triangle, angles \(A\) and \(C\) are complementary, meaning they have a sum of \(90^\circ\). In a right triangle, the \(\sin{(angle)}=\cos{(90-angle)}\). This means that the \(\sin(A)=\cos(C)\), or \(\sin(A)=\frac{\sqrt{10}}{11}\).
If you aren't familiar with that theorem, draw triangle ABC with the right angle at vertex \(B\). You are given that \(\cos(C)=\frac{\sqrt{10}}{11}\), and since cosine is the ratio of the adjacent side over the hypotenuse, label \(BC=\sqrt{10}\) and \(AC=11\). Then the \(\sin(A)=\) the ratio of the opposite side over the hypotenuse, which is \( rac{BC}{AC}\) or \( rac{\sqrt{10}}{11}\).

questions.stem: An angle has a measure of \(\frac{17\pi}{36}\) radians. What is the measure of the angle, in degrees?
questions.explanation:
To convert an angle from radians to degrees, multiply the angle by \(\frac{180}{\pi}\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ &=\frac{17 \cancel{\pi}}{36} \times \frac{180}{\cancel{\pi}}\\ &= rac{17 imes 180}{36}=42\end{aligned}$$
If you can't remember when to multiply by \( rac{180}{\pi}\) as opposed to \(\frac{\pi}{180}\), think about what you are trying to accomplish in your answer. Here, to go from radians to degrees, the \(\pi\) must cancel out. Since it is in the numerator, multiply it such that \(\pi\) is in the denominator to cancel the \(\pi\).

questions.stem: A circle has a center at Point \(P\) with point \(S\) on the circle. Arc \(QR\) is on the circle and measures \(84^\circ\). What is the measure, in degrees, of its associated inscribed angle \(QSR\)?
questions.explanation: Draw a circle with arc \(QR\) and an inscribed angle that intercepts the arc. The central angle that inscribes the arc has a measure that is equal to the arc, so central angle \(QPR=84^\circ\). An inscribed angle has a measure that is half of the central angle that intercepts the same arc, so \(\angle{QSR}= rac{1}{2}\angle{QPR}=\frac{1}{2} imes 84= 42\).

$$BC=60$$

The triangle \(ABC\) is a right triangle with the given side lengths. Triangle \(ABC\) and triangle \(DEF\) are similar, and \(B\) corresponds to \(E\) and \(A\) corresponds to \(D\). What is the value of \(\cos(D)\)?
questions.explanation:
Similar triangles have corresponding sides that are proportionate and corresponding angles that are congruent. Draw one right triangle and label it with vertices \(A\), \(B\), and \(C\). Since \(AB\) is the longest side, it must be the hypotenuse. This means that angle \(C\) is the right angle. Label the sides with their lengths. Then label corresponding angles, \(D\), \(E\), in \(F\) in their corresponding vertices.
The trigonometric ratio for cosine relates the adjacent side over the hypotenuse, and will be equivalent in similar triangles for corresponding (congruent) angles. In other words, \(\cos{D}=\cos{A}\). This means you can use the given side lengths to solve for \(\cos{D}\), so \(\cos(D)=\frac{32}{68}=\frac{8}{17}\).

questions.stem: Triangles \(ABC\) and \(HIJ\) are similar such that \(A\), \(B\), and \(C\) correspond to \(H\), \(I\), and \(J\), respectively. If \(BC=\frac{3}{4}IJ\) and the measure of \(\angle{I}\) is \(36^\circ\), what is the measure of \(\angle{B}\)?
questions.explanation: Similar triangles have corresponding sides that are proportionate and corresponding angles that are congruent. In this triangle, \(\angle{I}\) corresponds to \(\angle{B}\), so they are congruent. Since \(m\angle{I}=36^\circ\), \(m\angle{B}=36^\circ\).

questions.stem: What is the perimeter, in meters, of a rectangular field with a width of \(9\) meters and an area of \(54\) meters?
questions.explanation:
To find the perimeter only knowing the width, first find the length from the given information (the area). Use the area of a rectangle formula \(A=lw\) where \(l=\) the length of the rectangle and \(w=\) the width of the rectangle, to solve for the length.
$$\begin{aligned}\ A&=lw\\ \frac{54}{9}&=\frac{\cancel{9}l}{\cancel{9}}\\ l&=6\end{aligned}$$
Use the perimeter of a rectangle formula \(P=2l+2w\) to find the perimeter, so \(P=2(6)+2(9)=30\).

questions.stem: In triangles \(XYZ\) and \(JKL\), sides \(XY\) and \(JK\) each have length of \(14\) centimeters and angles \(Y\) and \(K\) each have measure \(27^\circ\). Which additional piece of information is sufficient to determine whether triangle \(XYZ\) is congruent to triangle \(JKL\)?
questions.explanation: Draw triangles \(XYZ\) and \(JKL\), labeling \(XY\) and \(JK\) congruent, and angles \(Y\) and \(K\) congruent. To prove whether these triangles are congruent, the additional information can either be about a pair of corresponding angles (which would utilize AAS or ASA if those angles are congruent), or about \(ZY\) and \(KL\) (SAS). Additionally, it is never enough to just know one additional side of one of the triangles (like in answer A). Since C states that \(ZY\) and \(KL\) are known, that would be enough to determine whether the triangles are congruent.

questions.stem: An isosceles triangle and a right triangle are shown above. If \(180-c=3a\) and \(a=40\), what is the value of \(b\)?
questions.explanation:
Substitute \(40\) in for \(a\) in \(180-c=3a\) and solve for \(c\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ 180-c&=3(40)\\ \cancel{180}-c-\cancel{180}&=120-180\\ (-1) imes(-c)&=-60 imes (-1)\\ c&=60 \end{aligned}$$
Since \(c=60^\circ\), the other angle of that right triangle is \(30^\circ\) (complementary with the angle of \(c^\circ\). And the angle with a measure of \(b^\circ\) is supplementary with the \(30^\circ\) angle, so \(180-30=150\).

questions.stem:
In the figure above, lines \(a\), \(b\), \(c\), and \(d\) intersect at a point. If \(f+n=j+h\), which of the following must be true?
$$\mathrm{I}. f=m$$
$$\mathrm{II}. h=k$$
$$\mathrm{III}. g=h$$
questions.explanation:
It is given that \(f+n=j+h\), and since \(j\) and \(n\) are vertical they are congruent. Subtracting \(j\) and \(n\) from both sides, it can be concluded that \(f=h\). Test the answers.
\(\mathrm{I}\). \(f=m\)?
\(h=m\): vertical angles
\(f=h\): from the given information
\(f=m\): substitution/Transitive Property, so \(\mathrm{I}\) is true. Eliminate C.
\(\mathrm{II}\). \(h=k\)?
\(f=k\): vertical angles
\(f=h\): from the given information
\(h=k\): substitution or the Transitive Property, so \(\mathrm{II}\) is true. Eliminate B.
\(\mathrm{III}\). \(g=h\)?
This is difficult to prove, so use a counterexample with real angle measures to prove or disprove.
Let \(h=30^\circ\) and \(n=50^\circ\). Since \(h=f=m=30^\circ\). \(m+n+f+g=180\) because they are on line \(b\). Substituting the values for \(m\), \(n\), and \(f\), \(30+50+30+g=180\) and \(g=70^\circ\). This is a counterexample to \(g=h\); \(g=70\) and \(h=30\), so in this case \(g\neq h\).

questions.stem: Square F has a side length of \(228x\) and square G has a side length of \(4x\), where \(x\) is a positive constant. The area of square F is how many times the area of square G?
questions.explanation:
Method \(1\): Squares F and G both have four right angles (definition of a square) and proportionate side lengths; this means they are similar. In similar figures, the ratio of their areas is the square of the ratio of their sides; \(\frac{\text{Area of square F}}{\text{Area of square G}}=\left( \frac{\text{Side length of F}}{\text{Side length of G}} \right)^2\). Substitute the given side lengths and simplify.
$$\begin{aligned}\ \frac{\text{Area of square F}}{\text{Area of square G}}&=\left({\frac{228\cancel{x}}{4\cancel{x}}}\right)^2=\left({\frac{228}{4}}\right)^2={57}^2=\text{3,249}\end{aligned}$$
Method \(2\): Choose a value for \(x\) and find the areas of the two squares. Then find the ratio of the area of square F to the area of square G. Let \(x=1\). Square F has side length \(228\), so \(A=228^2= ext{51,984}\). Square G has side length of \(4\), so \(A=4^2=16\). The ratio of the areas is \(\frac{\text{Area of square F}}{\text{Area of square G}}=\frac{\text{51,984}}{16}=\text{3,249}\).

questions.stem:
$$AC=300$$
$$AB=315$$
$$BC=435$$
The side lengths of right triangle \(ABC\) are given. Triangle \(ABC\) is similar to triangle \(DEF\), where \(A\) corresponds to \(D\) and \(C\) corresponds to \(F\). What is the value of \(\cos(F)\)?
questions.explanation:
Similar triangles have corresponding sides that are proportionate and corresponding angles that are congruent. Draw one right triangle and label it with vertices \(A\), \(B\), and \(C\). Since \(BC\) is the longest side, it must be the hypotenuse. This means that angle \(A\) is the right angle. Label the sides with their lengths. Label corresponding angles, \(D\), \(E\), in \(F\) in their corresponding vertices.
The trigonometric ratio for cosine relates the adjacent side over the hypotenuse, and is equivalent in similar triangles for corresponding (congruent) angles. In other words, \(\cos{(C)}=\cos{(F)}\). This means you can use the given side lengths to solve for \(\cos{(F)}\), so \(\cos(F)=\frac{300}{435}=\frac{60}{87}\).

questions.stem: The perimeter of an equilateral triangle is \(486\) feet. The height of this triangle is \(n\sqrt3\) feet, where \(n\) is a constant. What is the value of \(n\)?
questions.explanation:
An equilateral triangle has three congruent sides and three congruent angles, each of which measures \(60^\circ\). To find the length of each side, divide the perimeter by \(3\), so \( rac{486}{3}=162\). Draw a diagram of an equilateral triangle and label the sides \(162\) and one of the angles \(60^\circ\). Draw in height \(n\sqrt{3}\). This height then divides the triangle into two congruent triangles.
Each of these smaller triangles is a \(30-60-90\) triangle, the sides of which have a special relationship. The side length across from the \(60^\circ\) angle is given as \(n\sqrt{3}\). This means the side across from the \(30^\circ\) angle is \(n\) and the hypotenuse has a length of \(2n\). The hypotenuse is one of the sides of the equilateral triangle, so \(2n=162\) and \(n=81\).

questions.stem: The measure of angle \(A\) is \(\frac{\pi}{6}\) radians less than the measure of angle \(B\). The measure of angle \(B\) is \(\frac{4\pi}{3}\) radians. What is the measure of angle \(A\), in degrees?
questions.explanation:
To find measure of angle A in radians, subtract \(\frac{\pi}{6}\) from \(\frac{4\pi}{3}\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ &=\frac{4\pi}{3}-\frac{\pi}{6}=\frac{8\pi}{6}-\frac{\pi}{6}=\frac{7\pi}{6}\end{aligned}$$
To convert \(\frac{7\pi}{6}\) from radians to degrees, multiply by \(\frac{180}{\pi}\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ &=\frac{7 \cancel{\pi}}{6} \times \frac{180}{\cancel{\pi}}=\frac{7 \times 180}{6}=210\end{aligned}$$
If you can't remember when to multiply by \(\frac{180}{\pi}\) as opposed to \(\frac{\pi}{180}\), think about what you are trying to accomplish in your answer. Here, to go from radians to degrees, the \(\pi\) must cancel out. Since it is in the numerator, multiply it such that \(\pi\) is in the denominator to cancel the \(\pi\).

questions.stem: Square A has a perimeter that is \(\frac{1}{3}\) the perimeter of square B. Square A has a side length of \(3\) inches. What is the length, in inches, of one side length of square B?
questions.explanation:
Method \(1\): Squares A and B both have four right angles (definition of a square) and proportionate side lengths; this means they are similar. In similar figures, the ratio of their perimeters is equal to the ratio of their sides; since the perimeter of square A is \( rac{1}{3}\) the perimeter of square B, it can also be said that \( ext{side length of A}=\frac{1}{3} ext{side length of B}\). Substitute \(3\) for the side length of square A and solve.
$$\begin{aligned}\ 3&=\frac{1}{3}\times \text{side length of B}\\ 3 \times 3&=\frac{1}{\cancel{3}}\times \text{side length of B} \times \cancel{3}\\ \text{side length of B}&=9\end{aligned}$$
Method \(2\): Find the perimeter of square A; since square A has side lengths of \(3\), \(P=3 \times 4=12\). This is \(\frac{1}{3}\) the perimeter of Square B, so the perimeter of square B is \(3\) times the perimeter of square A, or \(12 \times 3=36\). To find a side length of square B, divide the perimeter by \(4\), so \( rac{36}{4}=9\).

questions.stem: In triangle \(XYZ\), the measure of angle \(Y\) is twice the measure of angle \(Z\) and the measure of angle \(X\) is \(48^\circ\). What is the measure of angle \(Z\)?
questions.explanation:
The measure of angle \(Y\) is given as twice the measure of angle \(Z\), so let \(m\angle Z=n\) and \(m\angle Y = 2n\). Draw a diagram and label the corresponding angles with their measures.
Use the Triangle Sum Property (the sum of the measures of the angles of a triangle is \(180^\circ\) to write an equation and solve for \(n\), which is the measure of \(\angle Z\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ 2n+n+48&=180\\ 3n+\cancel{48}-\cancel{48}&=180-48\\ \frac{\cancel{3}n}{\cancel{3}}&=\frac{132}{3}\\ n&=44\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: The hypotenuse of an isosceles right triangle has a length of \(132\) centimeters. What is the perimeter, in centimeters, of the triangle?
questions.explanation:
To find the perimeter of the triangle, first you have to find the missing side lengths. To do that, use the special side relationships of an isosceles right triangle, also known as a \(45-45-90\) triangle. The two legs of the triangle are equal in length (definition of isosceles) and the hypotenuse is equal to the length of the leg times \(\sqrt{2}\). Let \(x=\) the length of the legs of the triangle. This means the hypotenuse is equal to \(x \sqrt{2}\) (as shown in the diagram).
Since the hypotenuse is also \(132\), write and solve an equation for \(x\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ \frac{x \cancel{\sqrt{2}}}{\cancel{\sqrt{2}}}&=\frac{132}{\sqrt{2}}\\ x&=\frac{132}{\sqrt{2}} \times \frac{\sqrt{2}}{\sqrt{2}}\\ x&=\frac{132 \sqrt{2}}{2}\\ x&=66 \sqrt{2}\end{aligned}$$
To find the perimeter of the triangle, find the sum of the two legs and the hypotenuse, so \(66 \sqrt{2} + 66 \sqrt{2} +132 = 132 \sqrt{2} + 132\).

questions.stem: The edge length of a cube is \(46\) centimeters. Inside the cube is a solid sphere with a radius of \(23\) centimeters, and the sphere touches the center of each face of the cube. Which is closest to the volume of the space in the cube not taken up by the sphere, to the nearest cubic centimeter?
questions.explanation:
To find the space in the cube not taken up by the sphere, find \(\text{Volume}_\text{cube} - \text{Volume}_\text{sphere}\). The volume is \(V=s^3\) where \(s=\) the edge length of the cube. The volume of a sphere is \(V=\frac{4}{3} \pi r^3\) where \(r=\) the length of the radius.
$$\begin{aligned} V_\text{cube} &= (46)^3\\ V_\text{cube} &=\text{97,336} \end{aligned}$$
$$\begin{aligned} V_\text{sphere} &= \frac{4}{3} \pi (23)^3\\ V_\text{sphere} &= \frac{4}{3} \pi \text{12,167}\\ V_\text{sphere} &\approx \text{46,371}\end{aligned}$$
Subtract the volume of the sphere from the volume of the cube, so \(\text{97,336}-\text{46,371} \approx \text{46,371}\)

questions.stem: The figure above shows acute angles and \(\cos(m^\circ)=\sin(n^\circ)\). If \(m=-2y+41\) and \(n=12y+5\), what is the value of \(y\)?
questions.explanation:
If \(\cos(m^\circ)=\sin(n^\circ)\), then the angles are also complementary, which means their measures have a sum of \(90^\circ\). This means \(m+n=90\). Substitute the given expressions for \(m\) and \(n\) and solve for \(y\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ m+n&=90\\ -2y+41+12y+5&=90\\ 10y+\cancel{46}-\cancel{46}&=90-46\\ rac{\cancel{10}y}{\cancel{10}}&=\frac{44}{10}\\ y&=4.4\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: Circle D has a radius with a length that is \(84\) times the length of the radius of circle F. What is the value of \(n\) if the area of circle D is \(n\) times the area of circle F?
questions.explanation:
The question asks how many times the area of circle D is in relation to circle F, which is essentially asking you to find the ratio of the area of circle D to the area of circle F; the Area of circle D \(=\) Area of circle F \(\times n\), or \(n= \frac{\text{Area of circle D}}{\text{Area of circle F}}\).
Method \(1\): All circles are similar, which means their radii are proportionate and there are also special proportional relationships between their circumferences and areas. For all circles, the ratio of their areas is the square of the ratio of their radii; \(\frac{\text{Area of circle D}}{\text{Area of circle F}}=\left( \frac{\text{radius of D}}{\text{radius of F}} \right)^2\). The given radius ratio of circle D to circle F is \(84\) (or \(84\) to \(1\)). This means the ratio of the areas is \(84^2=\text{7,056}\). Since this ratio is also represented by \(n\), \(n=\text{7,056}\).
Method \(2\): If you don't know the relationship between the ratios of radii and areas of circles, choose a value for the radius of circle F and use it to find the radius of circle D and the areas of both circles. Then divide to find the value of \(n\). Let \(\text{radius}_F=2\). This means the radius of circle D is \(2 \times 84= 168\). Use the formula for the area of a circle, \(A=\pi r^2\) to find the area of each circle.
$$\begin{aligned} \text{Area}_\text{circle D} &= \pi (168)^2\\ \text{Area}_\text{circle D} &= \text{28,224} \pi \end{aligned}$$
$$\begin{aligned} \text{Area}_\text{circle F} &= \pi (2)^2 \\ \text{Area}_\text{circle F} &= 4 \pi \end{aligned}$$
Use the equation \(n= \frac{\text{Area of circle D}}{\text{Area of circle F}}\) to find \(n\), so \(n=\frac{\text{28,224}}{4}=\text{7,056}\).

questions.stem: In triangle \(ABC\), angle \(B\) is a right angle and \(\sin(C)=\frac{30}{78}\). What is the value of \(\sin(A)\)?
questions.explanation:
Draw and label right triangle \(ABC\). It is given that the \(\sin(C)=\frac{30}{78}\), and since sine is the ratio of the opposite side over the hypotenuse, label the side across from angle C (side AB) \(30\) and the hypotenuse (side AC) \(78\). The \(\sin(A)=\frac{\text{BC}}{\text{AC}}\), or \(\sin(A)=\frac{\text{BC}}{78}\), so next find the length of side BC. Use the Pythagorean Theorem, or \(a^2+b^2=c^2\) where \(c=\) the length of the hypotenuse.
$$\begin{aligned}\ 30^2+{BC}^2&=78^2\\ \cancel{900}+{BC}^2-\cancel{900}&=\text{6,084}-900\\ \sqrt{{BC}^2}&=\sqrt{\text{5,184}}\\ BC&=72\end{aligned}$$
Substitute \(72\) into the equation for the sine of A; \(\sin(A)=\frac{72}{78}\) or \(\sin(A)=\frac{12}{13}\).

questions.stem: A toy is composed of a right circular cylinder and a right circular cone with the given measurements shown in the figure above. Which of the following is closest to the volume of the toy, in cubic inches?
questions.explanation:
To find the volume of the entire toy, find the volume of the cylinder and the cone and add them together. The volume of a cylinder is \(V=\pi r^2 h\) and from the diagram, \(h=12\) and \(r=6\). The volume of a cone is \(V= rac{1}{3} \pi r^2 h\) and from the diagram, \(h=6\) and \(r=6\). Substitute the values to find the volumes.
$$\begin{aligned} \text{Volume}_ ext{cylinder} &= \pi (6)^2 \times 12\\ &= \pi 36 \times 12\\ &=432 \pi\end{aligned}$$
$$\begin{aligned} \text{Volume}_\text{cone} &= \frac{1}{3}\pi (6)^2 \times 6 \\ &=\frac{1}{3} \pi \times 216\\ &=72 \pi\end{aligned}$$
The volume of the toy is the sum of the volumes, so \(432 \pi + 72 \pi = 504 \pi \approx \text{1,584.4}\).

questions.stem: In a circle with center \(O\), central angle \(COD\) has a measure of \(\frac{5\pi}{3}\) radians. The area of the sector formed by the central angle \(COD\) is what fraction of the area of the circle?
questions.explanation:
The area of a sector as a fraction of the area of the circle can be written as \(\frac{\text{sector area}}{\text{area of the circle}}\) and is in proportion to the central angle out of the degrees (\(360^\circ\)) or radians (\(2 \pi\)) in a full circle, so \(\frac{\text{sector area}}{\text{area of the circle}}=\frac{\text{central angle}}{2 \pi}\). Substitute the central angle and simplify.
$$\begin{aligned}\ \frac{\text{central angle}}{2 \pi}=\frac{\frac{5 \pi}{3}}{2 \pi}=\frac{5 \pi}{3} \times \frac{1}{2 \pi}=\frac{5 \cancel{\pi}}{6 \cancel{\pi}}=\frac{5}{6}\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: The volume of a cylinder is \(605\pi\) cubic inches and its height is \(5\) inches. What is the length, in inches, of the radius?
questions.explanation:
Use the formula for the volume of a cylinder, \(V=\pi r^2 h\), and the given values, to solve for the radius.
$$\begin{aligned}\ \text{Volume} &= \pi r^2 \times 5\\ 605 \cancel{\pi} &= 5 \cancel{\pi} r^2\\ \frac{605}{5} &=\frac{\cancel{5} r^2}{\cancel{5}}\\ \sqrt{121} &= \sqrt{r^2}\\ r&=11\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: The circle shown has center \(O\), area \(112\pi\) square units, and diameters \(\overline{AC}\) and \(\overline{BD}\). The area of minor sector \(ODC\) is one third the area of minor sector \(OAD\). What is the area of minor sector \(OBC\)?
questions.explanation:
To find the area of sector \(OBC\), find the fraction of the circle represented by \(OBC\). It is given that the area of minor sector \(ODC\) is \(\frac{1}{3}\) the area of minor sector \(OAD\). Sectors \(OBC\) and \(OAD\) are equal in area because they are formed by congruent central angles (angles \(AOD\) and \(BOC\) are vertical and therefore congruent). This means \(\text{(area of} ODC) = \frac{1}{3} \text{(area of} OBC)\).
Since \(BD\) is a diameter, sector \(OBD\) is a semi-circle, which is one-half of the area of the circle, so \(\frac{1}{2}112\pi=56\pi\). This semi-circle is also the sum of the two sectors \(OBC\) and \(ODC\). Let \(x=\text{area of sector ODC}\). Write an equation that shows this relationship and solve for \(x\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ \frac{1}{3}x +x &= 56 \pi\\ \frac{\cancel{3}}{\cancel{4}}\frac{\cancel{4}}{\cancel{3}}x &= 56 \pi \times \frac{3}{4}\\ x &= 42 \pi\end{aligned}$$

$$$$
The legs of a right triangle are \(96\) inches and \(144\) inches. If the hypotenuse has a length of \(16\sqrt{n}\) inches, \(n\) is an integer, what is the value of \(n\)?
questions.explanation:
In order to find the value of \(n\), first find the length of the hypotenuse. Use the Pythagorean Theorem, \(a^2+b^2=c^2\) where the hypotenuse is \(c\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ a^2+b^2&=c^2\\ 96^2+144^2&=c^2\\ \sqrt{ ext{29,952}}&=\sqrt{c^2}\\ c&=\sqrt{ ext{29,952}} \end{aligned}$$
Set the hypotenuse equal to the given length \(16\sqrt{n}\) and solve for \(n\), so \(\sqrt{ ext{29,952}}=16\sqrt{n}\). Since \(16\) appears as a factor outside the square root, this means it was \(16^2\) inside the radical, so \(\sqrt{16^2 imes 117}=16\sqrt{n}\), and \(n=117\).

questions.stem: The measure of angle \(A\) is \(\frac{11\pi}{6}\) radians. The measure of angle \(B\) is \(\frac{7\pi}{12}\) radians less than the measure of angle \(A\). What is the measure of angle \(B\), in degrees?
questions.explanation:
To find measure of angle B in radians, subtract \( rac{7 \pi}{12}\) from \( rac{11 \pi}{6}\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ \frac{11 \pi}{6}-\frac{7 \pi}{12}=\frac{22 \pi}{12}-\frac{7 \pi}{12}=\frac{15 \pi}{12}\end{aligned}$$
To convert \(\frac{15 \pi}{12}\) from radians to degrees, multiply by \(\frac{180}{\pi}\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ \frac{15 \cancel{\pi}}{12} \times \frac{180}{\cancel{\pi}}=\frac{15 imes 180}{12}=225\end{aligned}$$
If you can't remember when to multiply by \(\frac{180}{\pi}\) as opposed to \(\frac{\pi}{180}\), think about what you are trying to accomplish in your answer. Here, to go from radians to degrees, the \(\pi\) must cancel out. Since it is in the numerator, multiply it such that \(\pi\) is in the denominator to cancel the \(\pi\).

questions.stem: Triangle \(DEF\) is similar to triangle \(JKL\), where \(D\), \(E\), and \(F\) correspond to \(J\), \(K\) , and \(L\), respectively. In triangle \(JKL\), the length of \(JL\) is \(45\) and the length of \(JK\) is \(22\). In triangle \(DEF,\) the length of \(DF\) is \(495\). What is the length of \(DE\)?
questions.explanation:
Similar triangles have corresponding sides that are proportional and corresponding angles that are congruent. Begin with a diagram labeling each triangle. Since \(DE\) is the side in question, label it \(x\). Since \(DE\) is in a much larger triangle, you can safely eliminate answer choices C and D.
\(DE\) corresponds to \(JK\) and \(DF\) corresponds to \(JL\). Set up and solve a proportion for \(x\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ \frac{x}{495}&=\frac{22}{45}\\ \frac{x}{\cancel{495}} \times{\cancel{495}}&=\frac{22}{45} \times{495}\\ x&=242\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: Cube A has an edge length that is \(6\) times the edge length of cube B. What is the ratio of the volume of cube B to the volume of cube A?
questions.explanation:
Method \(1\): All cubes are similar, which means their side lengths are proportionate and their angles are all congruent (\(90^\circ\)). For all cubes, the ratio of their volumes is the extit{cube} of the ratio of their side lengths; \(\frac{\text{side length of cube B}}{\text{side length of cube A}}^3=\frac{\text{(volume of cube B}}{\text{volume of cube A)}}\). The given side length ratio of cube B to cube A is \(1\) to \(6\). This means the ratio of the volumes is \(1^3=\text{1}\) to \(6^3=\text{216}\). The ratio of the volumes is \(1:216\), so the correct answer is \textbf{D}.
Method \(2\): If you don't know the relationship between the side lengths and volumes of similar cubes, this problem can still be solved. Choose a value for the side length of cube B. Use it to find the side length of cube A and the volumes of both cubes. Then write and simplify the ratio of cube B to cube A. Let \(\text{side length}_B=2\). This means the side length of cube A is \(2 \times 6= 12\). Use the formula for the volume of a cube, \(V=s^3\) where \(s=\) the side length.
$$\begin{aligned} \text{Volume}_\text{cube B} &= 2^3=8\end{aligned}$$
$$\begin{aligned} \text{Volume}_\text{cube A} &= 12^3=\text{1,728} \end{aligned}$$
The volume of the ratio of cube B to cube A is \(\frac{8}{\text{1,728}}=\frac{1}{\text{216}}\). This means the correct answer is \textbf{D}.

questions.stem: The area of an equilateral triangle is \(196 \sqrt{3}\) square inches. What is the length of one side of the triangle?
questions.explanation:
An equilateral triangle has three congruent sides and three congruent angles, each of which measures \(60^\circ\). To find the length of each side of the triangle, use special right triangles and the triangle area formula. The area of a triangle is \(A=\frac{bh}{2}\) where \(b\) is the length of the base and \(h\) is the length of the height. Begin by drawing a diagram of an equilateral triangle and label one of the angles \(60^\circ\). Draw in a height (the segment from one vertex that is perpendicular to the opposite side). The height divides the triangle into two congruent \(30-60-90\) triangles, the sides of which have a special relationship to one another. Label the side length across from the \(30^\circ\) angle \(n\). This means the height is \(n \sqrt{3}\), and the hypotenuse, and therefore, base of the triangle is \(2n\). Substitute the base, height, and area into the triangle area formula, and solve for \(n\).
$$\begin{aligned}\ A&=\frac{bh}{2}\\ 196 \sqrt{3}&= rac{2n \times n\sqrt{3}}{2}\end{aligned}$$
Divide both sides by \(\sqrt{3}\), essentially canceling them on either side of the equation. Additionally, simplify the right hand side by canceling \(\frac{2}{2}\). Now the equation is \(196=n^2\). This means \(n=\sqrt{196}=14\). The length of one side of the triangle is the same as the base \(2n\), so each side of the triangle is \(28\). This means the correct answer is \textbf{C}.
